title: Hybrid vehicle satellite navigation system with integrated contactless payment processing
abstract: A hybrid satellite navigation system consisting of a Bluetooth (RTM) transceiver, 5G/6G (or other Internet connection) transceiver and contactless payment transceiver. The hybrid satellite navigation system will work with a new breed of satellites designed to facilitate wireless payment processing via banking systems' servers. The invention relates to the field of contactless payment processing with particular focus on in-car or in-vehicle contactless payment processing. This in-car or in-vehicle contactless payment processing will be facilitated by the satnav screen in the vehicle and will work seamlessly with contactless credit cards and contactless debit cards.

title: Device for short-circuiting resistor of starter of engine of vehicle to start engine, has external electrodes exposed to outer side of terminal plate, and resistor connected between external electrodes at outer side of terminal plate
abstract: The device has an electromagnetic switch placed in a terminal plate. A pair of electromagnetic switch fixed contacts (6e, 6f) and a pair of fixed contacts (5f, 6f) are connected and disconnected with each other by mobile contacts (5d, 6d) of the electromagnetic switch fixed contacts. External electrodes (6g, 6h) are exposed to outer side of the terminal plate. A resistor (7) is connected between the external electrodes at the outer side of the terminal plate.

title: DISPLAY DEVICE, DISPLAY DEVICE MANUFACTURING METHOD, DISPLAY DEVICE MANUFACTURING APPARATUS
abstract: A display device (1) comprises a shared hole transport layer (6C) shared by a plurality of subpixels (2R, 2G, 2B) between an anode (4) and light emitting layers (8R, 8G, 8B), and an individual hole transport layer (6P) is individually provided for each of the plurality of subpixels between the common hole transport layer and the light emitting layers. The shared hole transport layer is configured using a first hole transport layer material. A first individual hole transport layer (6B) included in a first-color sub-pixel (2B) is configured using a second hole transport layer material. Second individual hole transport layers (6R, 6G) included in the second-color subpixels (2R, 2G) is configured using a mixed material of the first hole transport layer material and the second hole transport layer material.

title: Antenna farm intelligent software defined networking enabled dynamic resource controller in advanced networks
abstract: Facilitating antenna farm intelligent software defined networking enabled dynamic resource controller networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise evaluating a condition, the condition being at a mobile device, and a characteristic related to a defined network service associated with a connection to the mobile device. The operations can also comprise selectively modifying a quantity of antennas utilized to provide the connection to the mobile device based on the condition being determined to influence a level of service associated with the defined network service.

title: Silicone rubber material capable of repairing macroscopic scratches, preparation method and application thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a silicone rubber material capable of repairing macroscopic scratches, a preparation method and application thereof. The silicone rubber material comprises the following raw materials by weight: 60-90 parts of amino-terminated silicon rubber, 0.1-50 parts of conductive carbon black, 8-18 parts of diisocyanate, 3-13 parts of a cross-linking agent and 0.1-5 parts of nanoparticles with photo-thermal conversion effect. The preparation method includes: mixing the preparation raw materials with tetrahydrofuran to obtain a prepolymer, and controlling the use amount of the preparation raw materials at 0.1-0.6g/ml according to the volume of the tetrahydrofuran; and drying the prepolymer to obtain the silicone rubber material capable of repairing macroscopic scratches. The application comprises the application to repair of macroscopic scratches and application as 3D printing consumables. abstract: The invention relates to an active parking assist system (4) for a motor vehicle (2), wherein the parking assist system (4) comprises a plurality of capacitive ultrasonic converters (6a, 6b, 6c, 6d, 6e, 6f, 6g, 6h, 6i, 6j, 6k, 6l, 6m, 6n, 6o) which are essentially spaced evenly in a horizontally circumferential manner outside the motor vehicle (2).

title: 5G-sub-6G antenna for smart home
abstract: The utility model discloses a 5G-sub-6G antenna for smart home, and belongs to the technical field of antennas. The 5G-sub-6G antenna comprises a PCB, an antenna bracket, an antenna and a plurality ofantenna interfaces, wherein the antenna support is arranged on the PCB, the antenna is arranged on the antenna support and is connected with the PCB, the antenna comprises a plurality of antenna structures, and each of the plurality of antenna structures is correspondingly connected with one of the antenna interfaces. The switchable smart home 5G-sub-6G antenna provided by the utility model has very high gain, and can meet the frequency band requirements of almost all common products.

title: PERFORMANCE BASED ON INFERRED USER EQUIPMENT DEVICE SPEED FOR ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating improved performance based on inferred user equipment device speed for advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise estimating a speed of a user equipment device based on a number of times that a layer indicator associated with the user equipment device changes during a defined period of time. The operations can also comprise selecting a multiple input transmission mode for a transmission to the user equipment device based on the speed of the user equipment device, resulting in a selected transmission mode. A closed loop multiple input transmission mode can be selected in response to the speed being below a defined speed. Alternatively, an open loop multiple input transmission mode can be selected in response to the speed being above the defined speed.

title: GENERATION OF DEMODULATION REFERENCE SIGNALS IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating generation of demodulation reference signals in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise evaluating a capability of a mobile device and generating a demodulation reference signal sequence for the mobile device based on the capability of the mobile device. The demodulation reference signal sequence can be a first type based on the capability being a first capability and can be a second type based on the capability being a second capability.

abstract: The invention provides a 5G mobile terminal antenna system working in a millimeter wave and a Sub-6G frequency band and an application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of wireless communication and antennas. The substrate integrated waveguide leaky-wave antenna can work together with a Sub-6G antenna, and the millimeter-wave antenna and the low-frequency antenna are integrated on a mobile phone frame at the same time so that a space is saved. title: FACILITATING HYBRID AUTOMATIC REPEAT REQUEST RELIABILITY IMPROVEMENT FOR ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating hybrid automatic repeat request reliability improvement for advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise obtaining information related to a capability of a user equipment device and configuring the user equipment device with respect to control channel resources and a number of repetitions per slot based on the capability of the user equipment device. The operations can also comprise indicating the control channel resources and the number of repetitions per slot to the user equipment device via a control channel. Further, the operations can comprise detecting an acknowledgement, from the user equipment device, via an uplink control channel that comprises the control channel resources.

title: Random laser
abstract: The invention discloses a random laser. The random laser comprises a pumping source and a fiber network formed by randomly distributing a plurality of optical fibers, each optical fiber comprises a core layer and a cladding layer, the core layer is a PMMA layer (polymethyl methacrylate layer) doped with rhodamine 6G, the cladding layer is a PVDF layer (polyvinylidene fluoride layer), the refractive index of the cladding layer is larger than that of air, and the refractive index of the core layer is larger than that of the cladding layer. The pumping source carries out pumping in an oblique incidence mode, light is effectively coupled at the intersection point of optical fibers in the fiber network through evanescent waves to form a random annular resonant cavity, and stimulated radiation light is subjected to selective gain amplification in the random annular resonant cavity to form laser. abstract: The invention discloses a buffer based on inductance frequency expansion and a sampling front-end circuit of the buffer. title: 6G mobile communication system based on tensor calculation and data processing method thereof
abstract: The invention relates to a 6G mobile communication system based on tensor calculation and a data processing method thereof. The 6G mobile communication system comprises a high-dimensional informationsource, a tensor compression unit, sending equipment, a channel, receiving equipment, a tensor completion unit and an information sink which are connected in sequence. The tensor compression unit is used for compressing the high-dimensional information source to be transmitted to reduce the information redundancy of the high-dimensional information source. The tensor completion unit is used for supplementing information loss items caused by interference of the complete high-dimensional signals in the transmission process. According to the method, the problem of pain points in a future 6G mobile communication system is solved, compression and completion of high-dimensional data can be achieved, and a good effect is achieved. title: Method for preparing super-capacitor electrode material through elamine/thiourea and formaldehyde copolymerization method
abstract: The invention relates to a method for preparing a super-capacitor electrode material through an elamine/thiourea and formaldehyde copolymerization method. The method comprises the following preparation steps: enabling 2.8g of elamine and 17.6g of thiourea to be dissolved in 90ml of deionized water, uniformly stirring the mixture in a three-mouth flask for 0.5h, adding 12ml of formaldehyde, and stirring the mixture for 0.5h at an increased speed; slowly adding 0.4ml of concentrated hydrochloric acid through dripping, and performing the reaction for 3h under the temperature of 55 DEG C to obtainsuspending liquid with the milk white color; carrying out the centrifugation of a product: carrying out the centrifugation alternately through ethyl alcohol and deionized water till the liquid supernatant is neutral, and carrying out the drying for 8h; carrying out the carbonization of copolymer under the protection of argon under the temperature of 750 DEG C, and obtaining the super-capacitor ele

title: NETWORK CORE SOFTWARE DEFINED NETWORKING ENABLED ONBOARDING OF MICRO SERVICES FOR AN ADVANCED WIRELESS COMMUNICATIONS SYSTEM
abstract: A network core software defined networking (SDN) enabled onboarding of microservices in a 6G network is provided to enable a one-time registration of a device with a network. Rather than having a core network facilitates establishing communications channels every time a device attaches or connects to the mobile network, the SDN enabled onboarding system disclosed herein can onboard the device, authenticate the device, register the device with the network, and determine addressing for the device. When the device reconnects to the network, the device has already been onboarded, and so the device does not need to go through these steps again, but can rather immediately start communicating with desired network service.

title: SYSTEM MODEL AND ARCHITECTURE FOR MOBILE INTEGRATED ACCESS AND BACKHAUL IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating operation and support of mobile relays based on an integrated access and backhaul concept for advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided. An embodiment relates to a communication network architecture that can comprise a control plane architecture of a relay node device. The control plane architecture can comprise a star-type architecture. Further, the communication network architecture can comprise a user plane architecture of the relay node device. The user plane architecture can be separated from (or independent of) the control plane architecture. Further, the user plane architecture can comprise a multi-hop architecture. The relay node device can be configured to operate according to a fifth generation wireless network communication protocol, or other advanced communication protocols.

abstract: An antenna system for a mobile terminal and a mobile terminal are provided. The antenna system includes a first antenna group, a second antenna group and a third antenna group distributed on a housing, where the first antenna group includes a plurality of first antennas, the second antenna group includes a plurality of second antennas, the third antenna group includes a plurality of third antennas, the first antennas operates at a 4G band, the second antennas operates at a Sub 6G band, and the third antennas operates at a 5G millimeter-wave band. According to the antenna system of the present disclosure, the mobile terminal applying the antenna system can realize 4G and 5G mobile communications at the same time, and have multiple signal transmission modes and improve the signal transmission efficiency.

title: Cellular system
abstract: A system includes a housing with one or more edge processors to handle processing on behalf of a mobile target or to provide local data to the mobile target or to provide artificial intelligence for the mobile target; one or more antennas coupled to the housing; and a processor to control a directionality of the antennas in communication with the mobile target using 5G or 6G protocols.

title: Broadband Sub-6G external PCB (Printed Circuit Board) antenna
assignee: AUDEN COMMUNICATIONS & MULTIMEDIA TECHNO KUNSHAN CO LTD
abstract: The utility model discloses a broadband Sub-6G external PCB (Printed Circuit Board) antenna. The antenna comprises a PCB base material, the surface of the PCB base material is provided with a radiation oscillator, the surface of the PCB base material is provided with a concave loop oscillator, the radiation oscillator is provided with a resonance gap, the radiation oscillator is connected with a central feeder line, and the central feeder line is provided with a balance-unbalance converter. The utility model relates to a broadband Sub-6G external PCB (printed circuit board) antenna. Accordingto the design, the requirements of the Sub-6G frequency band are considered; the method is realized in a limited antenna environment. title: CAMERA MONITORING SYSTEM
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a CMS (Camera Monitoring System) capable of achieving power saving while ensuring safety.SOLUTION: The camera monitoring system includes: imaging means (side cameras 1L, 1R) for imaging the outside of a car CAR; display means (side monitors 2L, 2R) for displaying an imaged image: detection means 6G, 6E, 6T, 6D.6S for detecting a state of an engine switch, a state of an engine, a state of a gear shift, opening and closing states of a door and a seating state of a driver; and control means 3, 4, 5 for controlling power consumption of the imaging means and the display means on the basis of a detection output of the detection means, and controls the imaging means and the display means to be a low-consumption power state when a predetermined time elapses after the detection output of the detection means changes.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 1

assignee: GUANGZHOU KAIXIN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM CO LTD
abstract: The application relates to a communication system and a communication method. The communication system comprises an access unit used for receiving an initial downlink signal sent by a signal source, converting the initial downlink signal into a downlink interface signal and sending the downlink interface signal to at least one expansion unit, an extension unit being connected with the access unitand being used for receiving the downlink interface signal, converting the downlink interface signal into a downlink signal of a millimeter wave frequency band and sending the downlink signal of the millimeter wave frequency band to at least one remote unit, and the remote unit being connected with the expansion unit and being used for receiving the downlink signal of the millimeter wave frequencyband, converting the downlink signal of the millimeter wave frequency band into a target downlink radio frequency signal and sending the target downlink radio frequency signal to user equipment. title: Two-stage temperature adjusting system based on organic heat carrier critical temperature
abstract: The invention provides a two-stage temperature adjusting system based on organic heat carrier critical temperature. The system can supply continuous and stable heating/cooling/constant temperature regulation to large or multi-layer or multiple production lines under the organic heat carrier critical temperature of 410 DEG C, and the adjustment error during continuous heat supply or cooling is smaller than 0.5 DEG C, and by adopting the two-stage temperature regulating technological process, energy consumption is lowered by 30-40%. While energy consumption is greatly lowered, by means of high-temperature and stable convective heat transfer, and due to the fact that the technological curve is smooth, and the temperature radian is small, the thermal uniformity of large-specification hot discsof large multi-layer thermal presses or multiple thermal presses can be kept within 0.5-1.0 DEG C. The two-stage temperature adjusting system can meet the production machining requirements of large,mu

title: Antenna structure and electronic equipment
abstract: The invention provides an antenna structure and electronic equipment, the antenna structure comprises a first antenna, a second antenna, a first tuning circuit and a second tuning circuit, the first antenna comprises a first antenna body, the second antenna comprises a second antenna body, and a fracture is formed between the first antenna body and the second antenna body; a first end of the firsttuning circuit is connected with the first antenna body, a second end of the first tuning circuit is grounded, and the first tuning circuit is used for exciting the first antenna to generate first resonance; a first end of the second tuning circuit is connected with the second antenna body, a second end of the second tuning circuit is grounded, and the second tuning circuit is used for exciting the second antenna to generate second resonance, wherein the frequency band of the first resonance and the frequency band of the second resonance are Sub-6G frequency bands. According to the scheme prov

title: Truncation convolution coding optimization construction method
assignee: INST OF WAR ACADEMY OF MILITARY SCIENCES PLA CHINA
abstract: The invention discloses a truncation convolution coding optimization construction method. The method comprises the following steps: constructing a protograph and a basis matrix of a truncation convolution LDPC extension code set; obtaining a decoding threshold of a truncated convolutional LDPC extension code set through iterative computation; optimizing and reducing the decoding time delay of thetruncated convolution LDPC extended code set through iterative search; and constructing the check matrix of the truncated convolution LDPC extension code. According to the invention, the truncated convolution LDPC extension code suitable for 5G and 6G heterogeneous cellular network pulse channels is constructed, the transmission reliability of data on the pulse channels can be effectively improved, and the transmission time delay is reduced.

title: Data transmission structure, identification terminal and gate
assignee: CHONGQING ZHONGKE YUNCONG TECH CO LTD
abstract: The utility model belongs to the field of security and protection equipment, and particularly relates to a data transmission structure, an identification terminal and a gate. The data transmission structure comprises a grounding assembly, a mainboard and an antenna mechanism connected with the mainboard, and the antenna mechanism at least comprises one of a WiFi antenna, a main antenna and an auxiliary antenna; wherein the main antenna is a 4G, 5G or 6G main antenna, the auxiliary antenna is a 4G, 5G or 6G auxiliary antenna, the grounding end of the WiFi antenna is connected with the groundingassembly, and the grounding end of the main antenna is connected with the grounding assembly; and the grounding end of the auxiliary antenna is connected with the grounding assembly. The beneficial effects of the utility model are that mutual interference among the antennas of the antenna mechanism is avoided, and effective transmission of data is ensured.

title: Sending and receiving method and device
abstract: The invention discloses a sending method, which comprises the following steps of respectively setting a segment list corresponding to each intermediate node, or respectively setting selectable type length value objects corresponding to each intermediate node, and containing the set segment lists or selectable type length value objects in an SRH of an IPv6 segment routing SRv6 data packet, and sending the SRv6 data packet. The invention also discloses a receiving method, which comprises the following steps of receiving a sixth-generation internet protocol IPv6 segment routing SRv6 data packet,analyzing the segment list, determining processing to be carried out according to parameters contained in the segment list corresponding to the intermediate node, and carrying out the processing, or analyzing the selectable type length value object, determining the processing to be carried out according to each parameter contained in the selectable type length value object corresponding to the inte

title: Electric storage device based on small organic molecule dye and preparation method of electric storage device
abstract: The invention discloses an electric storage device based on a small organic molecule dye and a preparation method of the electric storage device. The preparation method comprises the following steps:dissolving rhodamine B or rhodamine 6G in absolute ethyl alcohol, and coating a substrate with the rhodamine B or rhodamine 6G solution completely dissolved in absolute ethyl alcohol to prepare an organic film layer; and then preparing an electrode on the organic film layer to obtain the electric storage device based on the small organic molecule dye. The organic small molecule rhodamine B or rhodamine 6G is dissolved in absolute ethyl alcohol to prepare the organic electric storage device of a sandwich structure, the organic electric storage behavior is successfully achieved, no reaction is needed before preparation, the preparation process is simple, only spin coating is needed, the device environment is sound, and high-temperature stability is high.

title: FAST MULTI-BEAM LISTEN BEFORE TALK
abstract: Facilitating fast multi-beam listen before talk in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a device can comprise determining a first inactivity in a first beam based on a first performance of a first listen before talk procedure in the first beam and, based on determining the first inactivity, analyzing a presence of an activity in a second beam based on a second performance of a second listen before talk procedure in the second beam. Further, the operations can comprise, based on determining a lack of the presence of the activity in the second beam, transmitting a signal via the second beam. In an example, analyzing the presence of the activity in the second beam can be performed prior to completion of the determining the first inactivity in the first beam.

title: FACILITATING IMPROVED PERFORMANCE OF MULTIPLE DOWNLINK CONTROL CHANNELS IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating improved performance of multiple downlink control channels in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise scheduling multiple data traffic channels for a user equipment device. Scheduling the multiple data channels can comprise scheduling a first data traffic channel of the multiple data traffic channels based on a legacy scheduling procedure and scheduling a second data traffic channel of the multiple data traffic channels based on an iterative procedure. The operations can also comprise transmitting, to the user equipment device, first information via multiple downlink control channels and transmitting, to the user equipment device, second information via the multiple data traffic channels.

title: FACILITATING SELECTION OF DEMODULATION REFERENCE SIGNAL PORTS IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating selection of demodulation reference signal port combinations in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise evaluating a capability of a mobile device. The operations can also comprise assigning a first group of port combinations for the mobile device based on the capability of the mobile device being a first capability and a second group of port combinations for the mobile device based on the capability of the mobile device being a second capability, resulting in a port combination assignment. The port combination assignment can mitigate a peak-to-average power ratio value.

title: FACILITATING IMPROVED PERFORMANCE OF MULTIPLE DOWNLINK CONTROL CHANNELS IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating improved performance of multiple downlink control channels in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise scheduling multiple data traffic channels for a user equipment device. Scheduling the multiple data channels can comprise scheduling a first data traffic channel of the multiple data traffic channels based on a legacy scheduling procedure and scheduling a second data traffic channel of the multiple data traffic channels based on an iterative procedure. The operations can also comprise transmitting, to the user equipment device, first information via multiple downlink control channels and transmitting, to the user equipment device, second information via the multiple data traffic channels.

title: FACILITATING SELECTION OF DEMODULATION REFERENCE SIGNAL PORTS IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating selection of demodulation reference signal port combinations in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise evaluating a capability of a mobile device. The operations can also comprise assigning a first group of port combinations for the mobile device based on the capability of the mobile device being a first capability and a second group of port combinations for the mobile device based on the capability of the mobile device being a second capability, resulting in a port combination assignment. The port combination assignment can mitigate a peak-to-average power ratio value.

title: DEVICE CONTEXTS, OPERATIONAL MODES, AND POLICY DRIVEN ENHANCEMENTS FOR PAGING IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating device contexts, operational modes, and policy driven enhancements for paging in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a network device can comprise analyzing a group of contextual data for a user equipment device, and mobility management behaviors historically implemented for the user equipment device and based on an indication that a page request is scheduled to be sent to the user equipment device. The operations can also comprise configuring a paging message for the user equipment device based on the contextual data and the mobility management behaviors. Further, the operations can comprise sending the paging message to the user equipment device.

title: Film random laser based on environment humidity tuning wavelength
abstract: The invention provides a film random laser device based on environmental humidity tuning wavelength. The laser device comprises rhodamine 6g, silver nano flower particles, a cured chitosan-glycerol film and a glass substrate, wherein the silver nanoflower particles and rhodamine 6g are dispersed in the cured chitosan-glycerol film, and the cured chitosan-glycerol film covers the glass substrate toform the random laser device. According to the invention, the chitosan-glycerol film has good water absorption, the output wavelength of the random laser can be tuned by changing the humidity of thesurrounding environment of the random laser, and the characteristics of flexibility and active interaction with the environment are achieved.

title: 5G/6G optical device full-temperature automatic test system and method
abstract: The invention provides a 5G/6G optical device full-temperature automatic test system and method, and the system comprises: an optical device drive board which is used for driving a to-be-tested 5G/6Goptical device, and meetsthe test conditions of a high-speed signal; ahigh-low temperature circulating box which is used for placing the to-be-tested 5G/6G optical device, so that the to-be-tested 5G/6G optical device is correspondingly tested in a full-temperature environment; anLIV tester, aspectrograph and ahigh-speed network analyzer which are used for testing LIV characteristics, spectral characteristics and high-speed network analysis parameters of the to-be-tested 5G/6G optical device in a full-temperature environment; acomputer which is connected with and drives the LIV tester, the spectrograph and the high-speed network analyzer to perform test actions; and apower supply which provides working current for the to-be-tested 5G/6G optical device, the LIV tester, the spectrograph, theh

title: City inside and outside and land and sea mixed scene propagation method for future mobile communication
abstract: The invention discloses a city inside and outside and land and sea mixed propagation method for future mobile communication. The method comprises the following steps: reading scene topography and landform; determining boundary conditions; according to a three-dimensional parabolic equation algorithm; simulating urban internal and external and mixed scene path loss and field distribution under a 5Gor 6G frequency band; reasonably deploying a base station; wherein the boundary condition determination is specifically as follows: determining an upper boundary condition of radio wave propagation determined by an atmospheric refractive index; determining a lower boundary condition determined by the surface; wherein the three-dimensional parabolic equation algorithm can specifically comprise thefollowing steps: determining a current source; determining an initial magnetic field according to the excitation source; determining a stepping magnetic field according to the initial magnetic field;em

title: Wireless network card
abstract: The utility model discloses a wireless network card. Mainboard comprising PCB, wherein a USB interface and an SIM card seat are arranged on the PCB main board, an SIM card is placed in the SIM card seat, and the slot antenna is characterized in that a square slot window is formed in the inner layer of the PCB main board below the SIM card, a three-finger type feed network is arranged on the PCB main board, and coupled feed is carried out on the slot window to form the slot antenna. The size of the square window is larger than that of the SIM card, and the radiation gain of the antenna at the high-frequency part is changed by adjusting the size of the square window and the distance from the square window to the edge of the SIM card. And the slot antenna works at a plurality of frequency points of 0.8-6G. And the slot antenna works at a working frequency point of 0.8 GHz to 0.9 GHz and a working frequency point of 2.4 GHz of the WLAN. According to the wireless network card of the utility

title: Graphene/monatomic layer GaS/GaAs radio generator and manufacturing method thereof
abstract: The invention relates to a radio power generation device and discloses a graphene/monatomic layer GaS/gallium arsenide radio generator. A layer of monatomic layer GaS is formed on a gallium arsenide substrate passivated by ammonium sulfide, a layer of graphene is attached to the layer of monatomic layer GaS, a first electrode and a side electrode are arranged, and the graphene is in direct contactwith a monatomic layer gallium sulfide surface of gallium arsenide to form a heterojunction. The key point of the generator lies in that graphene contacts gallium arsenide passivated by ammonium sulfide to form a Schottky junction and a Schottky barrier, t radio frequency waves (sub-6G) emitted by an emission source can be collected, the quantum property of the radio frequency waves stimulates hot electrons in the graphene, and a tunneling process occurs to form a current. External radio frequency waves are directly converted into direct-current electric energy, and electric energy can be prov

title: Wireless radio frequency circuit of wireless network bridge
abstract: The utility model discloses a wireless radio frequency circuit of a wireless network bridge. The circuit comprises a chip 6G, a radio frequency amplifier U3, a radio frequency amplifier U4, a filter U1 and a filter U16. The B59 pin of the chip 6G is connected with the 4 pin of the radio frequency amplifier U3, the A69 pin of the chip 6G is connected with the 4 pin of the radio frequency amplifierU4, the A65 pin of the chip 6G is connected with the 16 pin of the radio frequency amplifier U3, and the B57 pin of the chip 6G is connected with the 16 pin of the radio frequency amplifier U4. According to the wireless radio frequency circuit of the wireless network bridge, the radio frequency amplification circuit is additionally arranged at the network signal output end, so that signals are amplified step by step, and radio frequency power output which is large enough is obtained; and the amplified radio frequency signal is filtered by the filter circuit, so that the signal frequency is stab

title: Intelligent LED garden lamp and intelligent lighting method thereof
assignee: DONGGUAN PERFECT SCIENCE & TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses an intelligent LED garden lamp and an intelligent lighting method thereof. The street lamp comprises a lamp post, a main lamp wall, an auxiliary lamp wall, an LED lamp holder,an LED module, an LED lantern, a centralized controller, a storage battery box, a 5G/6G base station antenna and a power generation device, wherein a light sensation sensor is arranged on the centralized controller; a storage battery is arranged in the storage battery box; a single lamp controller is arranged in the LED lamp holder, the LED module and the LED lantern respectively; the centralizedcontroller is electrically connected with the three single lamp controllers, the storage battery, the 5G/6G base station antenna and the power generation device; and the storage battery is electrically connected with the power generation device. The LED garden lamp has the advantages that the three types of LED light source components are suitable for illumination of carriageways, sidewalks and fest

title: INFRARED RADIATION HEAT SOURCE DEVICE USING ELECTRIC HEATING THIN WIRE COIL
abstract: To improve earthquake proof performance of a winding coil used for an infrared radiation heat source device, and simplify and miniaturize a splicing structure.SOLUTION: An infrared radiation heat source device is structured by: a splicing connection plate 6 by a metal thin plate in which a splicing terminal 6f and an external connection terminal 6g are molded; an insulation holding body 7 that insulation-holds the splicing terminal 6f and the external connection terminal 6g; and an electric heating thin wire coil 1 heated by an electric conduction. A coil trunk part 1a of the electric heating thin wire coil 1 has an approximate circular cylindrical heat generation surface, and mainly has an infrared radiation to a right angle direction to a center axis of a coil. title: ZJD application processor architecture
abstract: The invention discloses a ZJD application processor architecture. The architecture comprises a ZJD general control system, at least two groups of independent application processors, at least two groups of public central processing units, a power manager, a public interface and a bus, wherein the ZJD master control system is connected with each group of independent application processors, each group of public central processing units, the power manager, the public interface and the bus; each group of public central processor is connected with the independent application processor; each group ofindependent application processors can independently process Bluetooth, WIFI, GPS/BDS positioning and a group of 5G/6G baseband signals; a plurality of groups of independent application processors can independently process Bluetooth, WIFI, GPS/BDS positioning and a plurality of groups of 5G/6G baseband signals, so the equipment can process a plurality of work tasks and requirements in parallel att

title: Television and SUB-6G antenna assembly thereof
assignee: SHENZHEN ZHONGTIAN COMMUNICATION TECH SHARES CO LTD
abstract: The utility model is suitable for the technical field of antennas, and provides a television and an SUB-6G antenna assembly thereof. The embodiment of the utility model provides an SUB-6G antenna assembly applied to a television. A plurality of groups of antennas are arranged on the inner side of a shell of the OPS box; each group of antennas comprises two antennas with the same working frequency,shape and size; the at least one group of antennas are arranged in an isolated manner, so that the communication rate can be effectively improved, the requirements of an 8K high-definition televisionare met, the influence of the external environment on the antenna performance is effectively reduced, the universality of the antenna is better, the interference between the antennas can be reduced by arranging the at least one group of antennas in an isolated manner, and the working performance of the antenna is more stable.

title: ANTENNA ARRAY ASSEMBLY AND METHOD OF CONSTRUCTION THEREOF
abstract: An antenna array assembly comprises a dielectric film (2) carrying an array of conductive patch radiator elements (7) and a plurality of conductive feed tracks and a ground plate (1), a face of the ground plate (1) having a contoured shape comprising a first part configured to be in contact with the dielectric film (2), and a second part comprising sunken sections with respect to the first part arranged to underlie the conductive feed tracks and conductive patch radiator elements (7), and the ground plate having locating protrusions (6g, 6f) protruding from the first part. The dielectric film is attached to the ground plate by location of holes in the dielectric film with the locating protrusions (6g, 6f) such that the dielectric film is held under tension to maintain spacing of the dielectric film (2) from the sunken sections of the ground plate (1) without the use of a dielectric spacer layer between the dielectric film (2) and the ground plate (1).

title: LIGHT-EMITTING ELEMENT AND LIGHT-EMITTING DEVICE
abstract: Light-emitting elements (6R, 6G, 6B) are each configured such that, in a LUMO level, an energy level difference between a second hole transport layer (12R, 12G, 12B) and a light-emitting layer (14R, 14GH, 14B) on the side of the second hole transport layer is larger than an energy level difference between the light-emitting layer on the side of the second hole transport layer and a layer (14GE, 16) in contact with a negative electrode (20) side of the light-emitting layer on the side of the second hole transport layer, and that, in a HOMO level, an energy level difference between a first electron transport layer (16) and a light-emitting layer (14R, 14GE, 14B) on the side of the first electron transport layer is greater than an energy level difference between the light-emitting layer on the side of the first electron transport layer and a layer (12, 14GH) in contact with a positive electrode (10) side of the light-emitting layer on the side of the first electron transport layer.

title: ALLOCATING AND EXTRAPOLATING DATA FOR AUGMENTED REALITY FOR 6G OR OTHER NEXT GENERATION NETWORK
abstract: Global position system tagging the movement of an object and extrapolating its direction and speed can be used for various services including emergency-based services. Location data can be computed using edge computing nodes. The extrapolation system can account for feedback from responding user devices and utilize the user device's location at the time of reporting to facilitate determining the direction, location, and/or speed of a moving object. This data can then be utilized to generate augmented reality displays for mobile devices and/or vehicles that utilize the system. The ability to calculate directional information with edge computing nodes can comprise an ability to add enriched data by predicting an object's whereabouts, route, and/or final destination.

title: COLUMNAR SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICE, AND METHOD FOR MANUFACTURING SAME
abstract: In an SRAM cell, an Si column 6a of a selected SGT of an array of upper-level Si columns 6a to 6e is at the left end in the X-direction. An Si column 6j of a selected SGT of an array of lower-level Si column rows 6f to 6j is at the right end. In a plan view, a lower-level Si column 6g is present in the width of a contact hole 47a extended in the Y-direction. In the plan view, an upper-level Si column 6d is present in the width of the contact hole 47b extended in the Y-direction. In the upper level, a TiN layer 40b providing gate electrodes of a load SGT and a drive SGT is formed in contact with a side surface of an entire gate region vertically between Si columns 6c, 6d, and 6e. Similarly, in the lower level, a TiN layer 40c providing gate electrodes of the load SGT and the drive SGT is formed in contact with a side surface of an entire gate region vertically between the Si columns 6f, 6g, and 6h.

title: SMART CASCADING SECURITY FUNCTIONS FOR 6G OR OTHER NEXT GENERATION NETWORK
abstract: In a 6G network, microservices can be utilized in the absence of a core network. For example, after a mobile device has authenticated, through its carrier network, with a transport service layer, microservices can be allocated to the mobile device without having to be transmitted via the core network. Thus, removing the core network from the process can generate a direct line of microservices from the transport layer to the end-user. Furthermore, additional microservices and/or resources can be access through a microservices library. Consequently, packets can be securely transmitted be a wireless network facilitating sending packet profile data from one to many node devices in anticipation of the packet traversing the various node devices.

title: Metamaterial-based terahertz wave separator applied to 6G wavelength division multiplexing system
abstract: The invention relates to a metamaterial-based terahertz wave separator applied to a 6G wavelength division multiplexing system, which is used for wavelength division multiplexing of the 6G wavelengthdivision multiplexing system and belongs to the technical field of communication wave separators. The terahertz wave separator is composed of a plurality of same metamaterial unit structures, whereinthe metamaterial unit structures are periodically arranged along x and y directions; and the metamaterial unit structure is a square structure, the side length is P, each metamaterial unit structure comprises a dielectric layer and a metal pattern layer on the surface of the dielectric layer, and the metal pattern layer is a linear cuboid metal wire. When terahertz waves with two specific frequencies are obliquely incident to the surface of the metamaterial wave separator, the terahertz waves of one frequency are transmitted, the terahertz waves of the other frequency are reflected, the terahert

title: FACILITATING AUTOMATIC LATENCY DISCOVERY AND DYNAMIC NETWORK SELECTION USING DATA ANALYTICS IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating automatic latency discovery and dynamic network selection using data analytics in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise inserting, by a system comprising a processor, first information indicative of a first identified time into a first field of a message. Also, the method can comprise transmitting, by the system, the message to a defined device. Further, the method can comprise determining, by the system, an end-to-end latency for the message based on a response received from the defined device in reply to the message. The response can comprise second information indicative of a second identified time that the message was received at the defined device.

title: Outdoor omnidirectional sub-6G antenna with mechanical adjustment downward inclination angle
abstract: The utility model relates to an outdoor omnidirectional sub-6G antenna with a mechanical adjustment downward inclination angle. A phase shifter power dividing plate and a 2G power dividing plate are fixed on the inner wall surface of the reflecting plate through fixing buckles; a phase shifter scribing sheet is arranged on the phase shifter power dividing plate in a sliding manner; the phase shifter scribing is connected with the spiral lifting mechanism; a PCB coupling plate is fixed on the phase shifter scribing sheet; an elastic pressing sheet is arranged between the PCB coupling plate andthe phase shifter scribing sheet; wherein a 2G feeder plate is fixed on the outer wall surface of the reflecting plate through a fixing buckle, a 2G oscillator assembly composed of a first 2G oscillator and a second 2G oscillator is fixed on the outer wall surface of the 2G feeder plate, and the phase shifter power dividing plate, the 2G power dividing plate and the 2G feeder plate are connected wi

title: Multi-channel high-capacity sub-6G antenna
abstract: The utility model relates to a multichannel high-capacity sub-6G antenna. A first printed circuit board is fixedly supported and installed on the upper surface of the metal bottom plate through a first metal supporting stand column. A second printed circuit board is welded on the upper surface of the metal bottom plate; a third printed circuit board is fixedly supported and mounted on the upper surface of the metal bottom plate through a second metal supporting upright post; the second metal supporting stand column penetrates through the first printed circuit board, the installation height ofthe third printed circuit board is larger than that of the first printed circuit board, an annular metal sheet is fixed to the corner position of the upper surface of the metal bottom plate, and a long-strip metal sheet is fixed to the annular metal sheet and stretches across the annular metal sheet. The coaxial line penetrates through the metal bottom plate from bottom to top and is connected with

title: Large-scale cooperative access method irrelevant to source address
abstract: The invention discloses a large-scale cooperative access method irrelevant to a source address. In a sixth generation (6G) de-cellular wireless network, a plurality of access points equipped with large-scale antenna arrays are distributed throughout the interval, and a large number of wireless devices access the network through the access points. In any given time slot, only part of equipment is activated, and other equipment is in a dormant state. Based on an unlicensed random access protocol, all activation devices send information processed by an external encoder to the access points by using the same codebook with all known access points. First, each access point performs internal data decoding based on a large-scale cooperation detection method, and then transmits a decoding result toan external decoder, and the external decoder restores the received information by splicing sub-information sequences, but does not need to detect which devices the information comes from. title: Mainboard assembly with antenna and capable of realizing sub-6G and communication equipment
abstract: The invention discloses a mainboard assembly with an antenna and capable of realizing sub-6G and communication equipment, the mainboard assembly comprises a mainboard and a patch antenna arranged on the mainboard, and the mainboard comprises a substrate and a mainboard ground extension structure connected to one end of the substrate; the substrate comprises a clearance area opposite to the mainboard ground extension structure, and the patch antenna is attached to the interior of the clearance area; the mainboard ground extension structure is coupled with the clearance area and the patch antenna to realize a sub-6G frequency band; according to the mainboard assembly, the mainboard ground extension structure connected with the substrate is arranged; the mainboard ground extension structure,the clearance area and the patch antenna are coupled; the requirements of N41, N77, N78 and N79 frequency band transmission are met, so the Sub-6G requirement with high-speed signal transmission is met

title: FACILITATING DYNAMIC MULTIPLE PUBLIC LAND MOBILE NETWORK RESOURCE MANAGEMENT IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating dynamic satellite and mobility convergence for mobility backhaul in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise dividing resources of a wireless network between a first network device and a second network device based on defined service level agreements. The operations also can comprise receiving a data packet from a mobile device. The data packet can comprise an indication that the first network device provides services for the mobile device. Further, the operations can comprise transferring the data packet to the first network device based on the resources assigned to the first network device and based on the data packet bypassing an access core of the wireless network.

title: FACILITATING DYNAMIC SATELLITE AND MOBILITY CONVERGENCE FOR MOBILITY BACKHAUL IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating dynamic satellite and mobility convergence for mobility backhaul in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise determining that a group of user equipment devices are located in a defined geographic area and are consuming more than a defined level of resources of a wireless communications network based on an amount of network traffic received from the group of user equipment devices. The operations also can comprise configuring an integrated network comprising a first group of terrestrial network devices and a second group of satellite network devices. Further, the operations can comprise routing at least a portion of network traffic associated with the group of user equipment devices among the first group of terrestrial network devices and the second group of satellite network devices.

title: Coating forming method of novel material layer structure of high-frequency circuit board and product thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a coating forming method of a novel material layer structure of a high-frequency circuit board. The coating forming method comprises the following steps: (1) coating a copper foil to synthesize a liquid film; (2) sending the copper foil into an oven for baking, and forming a cured film on the copper foil to obtain a single-sided board; (3) coating a synthetic liquid high-frequency material layer on the cured film; and (4) sending to a tunnel oven for baking, synthesizing a liquid high-frequency material layer into a semi-cured high-frequency material layer, and obtaining the novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board. The invention further discloses a product prepared by implementing the method. The prepared novel material layer structure ofthe high-frequency circuit board has the performance of transmitting high-frequency signals at a high speed, can adapt to the high-frequency and high-speed trend from a wireless network to terminal app

title: Preparation method of novel material layer structure of high-frequency circuit board and product thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a preparation method of a novel material layer structure of a high-frequency circuit board. The preparation method comprises the following steps: (1) coating a cured PI film tosynthesize a liquid TFP film; (2) feeding into a tunnel oven for segmented baking, and forming a semi-cured TFP film on the front surface of the cured PI film; and (3) hot-pressing a copper foil on the semi-cured TFP film to obtain the single-sided novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board. The invention further discloses a novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board, which is prepared by implementing the method. The prepared novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board has the performance of transmitting high-frequency signals at a high speed, can adapt to the high-frequency and high-speed trend from a wireless network to terminal application at present, and is particularly suitable for novel 5G and 6G scientific and te

title: Manufacturing method of multilayer double-sided rigid-flex board and product thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a manufacturing method of a multilayer double-sided rigid-flex board. The manufacturing method comprises the following steps: (1) manufacturing a double-sided FPC flexible board; (2) manufacturing at least one group of soft material layer structures; (3) manufacturing at least two groups of hard material layer structures; and (4) hot-pressing at least one group of soft material layer structures on the circuit on the upper surface and/or the lower surface of the double-sided FPC flexible board, and respectively hot-pressing at least one group of hard material layer structures on the outermost circuit above and below the base film of the double-sided FPC flexible board to obtain the multilayer double-sided flexible circuit board. The invention also discloses a multilayer double-sided rigid-flex board. The manufacturing process is simplified and convenient; the manufactured multilayer double-sided rigid-flex board has high-frequency characteristics, namely has thep

title: Press-fit forming method of novel material layer structure of high-frequency circuit board and product thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a press-fit forming method of a novel material layer structure of a high-frequency circuit board, which comprises the following steps of: (1) coating a semi-cured film on a film to form a TPI film; (2) putting a copper foil on the TPI film for hot pressing, curing the semi-cured film, and integrating the semi-cured film with the film to form a synthetic film; and (3) coating the other surface of the film with a layer of semi-cured high-frequency material to form the novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board. The invention further discloses a novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board, which is prepared by implementing the method. The manufactured novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board has the performance of transmitting high-frequency signals at a high speed, can adapt to the high-frequency and high-speed trend from a wireless network to terminal application at present, and is particula

title: Facilitation of dynamic edge computations for 6G or other next generation network
abstract: Peripheral devices such as wearables can link to mobile devices to access network elements. These peripheral devices can comprise a communication part and a processing part. Each of these parts, specially the processing part of the next item in a communication chain, can be multiple times larger and stronger than the previous linked item. For instance, the peripheral device can be connected to the mobile device which can be in line connected to an NodeB device. Because the processing power of mobile device can be several times stronger than the peripheral, a mobile edge computing platform and a multi-access application function facilitate sharing of processing power capabilities between the peripheral devices, mobile devices, and/or the NodeB devices.

title: Capacity expansion method for 6G communication cellular mobile communication system
assignee: SUZHOU YUNZHIQIU INTELLIGENT TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a capacity expansion method for a 6G communication cellular mobile communication system. Cellular mobile communication adopts a cellular wireless networking mode, a terminal and network equipment are connected through a wireless channel so as to realize mutual communication of users in activities, and the capacity expansion method is characterized by comprising the following five steps. The method comprises the following steps that: 1, macro cells are adopted, and more macro cells with reduced coverage area are constructed by adopting a cell splitting technology; 2, a densification frequency reuse scheme or an MrP multi-reuse technology is adopted, isolated microcells and picocells are established in hot spot areas and important places, and sometimes, the first stepand the second step can be carried out in a crossed and combined mode; and 3, the capacity of the 6G communication cellular mobile communication system is expanded by improving the voice coding rate,r

title: Antenna structure and wearable electronic device having antenna structure
assignee: YULONG COMPUTER TELECOMMUNICATION SCIENTIFIC SHENZHEN CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides an antenna structure, which is applied to a wearable electronic device. The wearable electronic device includes a display module. The antenna structure is arranged on the display module. The antenna structure includes a carrier and multiple groups of array antennas. The carrier is arranged along the periphery of the display module. The multiple groups of array antennas, spaced apart from each other along the carrier, are used for receiving and transmitting sub 6G millimeter waves. The invention also provides an electronic device having the antenna structure.

title: RECONSTITUTED SUBSTRATE FOR RADIO FREQUENCY APPLICATIONS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to methods and apparatus for forming thin-form-factor reconstituted substrates and semiconductor device packages for radio frequency applications. The substrate and package structures described herein may be utilized in high-density 2D and 3D integrated devices for 4G, 5G, 6G, and other wireless network systems. In one embodiment, a silicon substrate is structured by laser ablation to include cavities for placement of semiconductor dies and vias for deposition of conductive interconnections. Additionally, one or more cavities are structured to be filled or occupied with a flowable dielectric material. Integration of one or more radio frequency components adjacent the dielectric-filled cavities enables improved performance of the radio frequency elements with reduced signal loss caused by the silicon substrate.

title: CELLULAR SYSTEM
abstract: A system includes a housing with one or more edge processors to handle processing on behalf of a mobile target or to provide local data to the mobile target or to provide artificial intelligence for the mobile target; one or more antennas coupled to the housing; and a processor to control a directionality of the antennas in communication with the mobile target using 5G or 6G protocols.

title: CELLULAR SYSTEM
abstract: A system includes a cellular transceiver to communicate with a predetermined target; one or more antennas coupled to the 5G or 6G transceiver each electrically or mechanically steerable to the predetermined target; a processor to control a directionality of the one or more antennas in communication with the predetermined target; and an edge processing module coupled to the processor and the one or more antennas to provide low-latency computation for the predetermined target.

title: Three-dimensional hairpin filter applied to 6G communication
abstract: The invention discloses a three-dimensional hairpin filter applied to 6G communication. The hairpin filter comprises a hairpin unit column and an upper grounding RDL which are arranged in parallel, the hairpin unit column is formed by arranging a plurality of hairpin units at intervals; a signal TSV is arranged at one end of the hairpin unit; the other opposite end is a signal RDL; the upper and lower end surface structures of the adjacent hairpin units are opposite; symmetrical signal feeders are arranged at the tops of the two ends of the hairpin unit column. Signal RDLs are arranged on thetop surfaces of the hairpin units at the end parts of the two ends of the hairpin unit column; the top of the upper grounding RDL is provided with a grounding feeder line opposite to the signal feederline, the bottom of the upper grounding RDL is provided with a lower grounding RDL, a grounding TSV opposite to the signal TSV is arranged between the upper grounding RDL and the lower grounding RDL,an

title: Method of detecting rod inclination by receiving signal strength data of rod
assignee: SHANGHAI LUHUI INTELLIGENT SYSTEM CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a method for detecting the rod inclination by receiving signal strength data of the rod, which comprises the following steps: defining data content and meaning of a 4G/5G/6G micro base station signal receiving end, such as time scale, signal strength, ID of a sector cell and communication capability; defining an antenna of a base station signal transmitting end; ensuring the stability of base station signals; carrying out big data analysis under normal conditions, and eliminating instability of base station signals by using big data; on the basis that the base stationsignal is approximately stable, analyzing the orientation change and inclination angle change degree caused by rod inclination and the influence on the signal intensity of a large-range receiving end;upgrading algorithms. The inclination of the rod can be directly detected through the signal intensity; data analysis can be completed in a large-scale range based on signals provided by a three-directi

title: Manufacturing method of multilayer flexible circuit board and product thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a manufacturing method of a multilayer flexible circuit board. The manufacturing method comprises the following steps: (1) manufacturing a double-sided FPC flexible board; (2)manufacturing a novel material layer structure; (3) hot-pressing at least one group of novel material layer structures on the circuit on the upper surface and/or the lower surface of the double-sidedFPC flexible board; and forming a protective layer on the circuit of the outermost novel material layer structure and/or the exposed circuit of the double-sided FPC flexible board to obtain the multi-layer flexible circuit board. The invention further discloses the multi-layer flexible circuit board manufactured by implementing the method. The manufacturing process is simplified and convenient, and the production efficiency is high; the manufactured multi-layer flexible circuit board not only greatly simplifies the structure of a novel material layer and reduces the overall thickness, but alsoha

title: FACILITATING OUTER LOOP LINK ADAPTATION IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating facilitate enablement of a smart HARQ feedback to support outer loop link adaptation in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise sending, by a first device comprising a processor, a data packet to a second device. The method also can comprise receiving, by the first device, negative acknowledgement data from the second device. The negative acknowledgement data can indicate that the second device fails to support a modulation and coding protocol of the data packet.

title: FACILITATING A GROUP HYBRID AUTOMATIC REPEAT REQUEST PROCEDURE FOR SIDELINK GROUP CASE IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating a group hybrid automatic repeat request procedure for sidelink group-case in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise determining, by a first device comprising a processor, that first information received via a sidelink control channel is successfully decoded and that second information received via a sidelink shared channel is not successfully decoded. The first information and the second information can be received from a second device. Further, the method can comprise transmitting, by the first device, negative acknowledgement data based on a group hybrid automatic repeat request feedback procedure. The method also can comprise receiving, by the first device, retransmission of the second information via the sidelink shared channel from a group of devices in response to the negative acknowledgement data.

title: Symmetrical multi-slot terahertz 6G communication application frequency band antenna
abstract: The invention discloses a symmetrical multi-slot terahertz 6G communication application frequency band antenna, and belongs to the field of terahertz antennas. The symmetrical multi-slot terahertz 6Gcommunication application frequency band antenna is characterized in that the main body part of the antenna is an insulating dielectric layer; a butterfly-like radiation patch and a circular patch combined with the butterfly-like radiation patch are arranged in the center of the upper surface of the dielectric layer; the butterfly-like radiation patch and the circular patch are used for feeding together; the circular patch is combined in the radiation patch, so that ultra-wideband effect is achieved; and semicircular grooves are symmetrically formed in the left end and the right end of the radiation patch towards the farthest end of the center of the radiation patch, two sets of four rectangular grooves with the same width are formed in the left end and the right end of the radiation patcht

title: FACILITATING SPARSITY ADAPTIVE FEEDBACK IN THE DELAY DOPPLER DOMAIN IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating sparsity adaptive feedback in the delay doppler domain in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise determining, by a first device comprising a processor, a channel covariance matrix in a time-frequency domain based on a channel estimation associated with reference signals received from a second device. The method also can comprise decomposing, by the first device, the channel covariance matrix into a group of component matrices. Further, the method can comprise transforming, by the first device, respective matrices of the group of component matrices into respective covariance matrices in a delay doppler domain. The method also can comprise determining, by the first device, channel state information feedback in the delay doppler domain.

title: Random laser
abstract: The utility model discloses a random laser. The fiber laser comprises a pumping source and a fiber network formed by randomly distributing a plurality of optical fibers, the optical fiber comprises acore layer and a cladding layer, the core layer is a PMMA layer (polymethyl methacrylate layer) doped with rhodamine 6G, the cladding layer is a PVDF layer (polyvinylidene fluoride layer), the refractive index of the cladding layer is larger than that of air, and the refractive index of the core layer is larger than that of the cladding layer. Wherein the pumping source carries out pumping in an oblique incidence mode, light is effectively coupled at the intersection point of optical fibers in the fiber network through evanescent waves to form a random annular resonant cavity, and stimulated radiation light is subjected to selective gain amplification in the random annular resonant cavity to form laser. title: Cylindrical lithium ion battery with quick charging performance
assignee: TIANJIN SPACE POWER TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a cylindrical lithium ion battery with quick charging performance, and belongs to the technical field of lithium ion batteries. The cylindrical lithium ion battery comprises apositive pole piece, a negative pole piece, electrolyte, a diaphragm and a shell, and is characterized in that the positive pole piece comprises a positive pole material and a current collector, and the positive pole material is composed of an active substance, a conductive agent and a binder; the coating density of the positive pole piece is 15-35mg/cm<2>, and the compaction density of the positive pole piece is 2.8-3.6g/cm<3>; the negative pole piece comprises a negative pole material and a current collector, and the negative pole material is composed of an active substance, a conductive agent and a binder; the coating density of the negative pole piece is 9-22mg/cm<2>, and the compaction density of the negative pole piece is 1.4-1.70g/cm<3>; the diaphragm is one of a PE film, a PP/PE/PPc

title: Macro station user distribution method for unmanned aerial vehicle auxiliary cellular network
abstract: In order to solve the problems that a B5G/6G network faces higher coverage and larger capacity requirements and an existing ground cellular network is difficult to guarantee the performance of edge users, the embodiment of the invention provides a macro station user distribution scheme for an unmanned aerial vehicle auxiliary cellular network, so that dynamic coverage is provided for the edge users. The method is beneficial to high channel gain brought by a large-probability line-of-sight link of an unmanned aerial vehicle base station; according to the scheme, one or more unmanned aerial vehicles take the ground macro base station as the center and do circular motion in the clockwise or anticlockwise direction, and a high data transmission rate is provided for macro cell users by utilizing the sight distance link and the antenna gain. By adjusting parameters such as the movement track radius, the flight height, the flight speed and the transmitting power of the unmanned aerial vehicle

assignee: KUNSHAN YIQU INFORMATION TECH RESEARCH INSTITUTE CO LTD

The other end of the first metal section is connected to the second metal section, the second radiation arm comprises a third metal section and a fourthmetal section, one end of the third metal section is connected to the metal floor, and the other end of the third metal section is connected to the fourth metal section; the length of the first radiation arm is 1030 mm, the length of the second radiation arm is 410 mm, and the distance between the second metal section and the metal floor and the distance between the fourth metal section and the me

assignee: KUNSHAN YIQU INFORMATION TECH RESEARCH INSTITUTE CO LTD
abstract: The utility model provides a Sub6G multi-band antenna system and a mobile terminal, the antenna system comprises a metal floor, a first radiation arm and a second radiation arm, the first radiation arm and the second radiation arm are arranged on the same side of the metal floor, the first radiation arm comprises a first metal segment and a second metal segment, one end of the first metal segmentis connected to the metal floor, and the other end of the second metal segment is connected to the mobile terminal. The other end of the first metal section is connected to the second metal section, the second radiation arm comprises a third metal section and a fourth metal section, one end of the third metal section is connected to the metal floor, and the other end of the third metal section isconnected to the fourth metal section; the length of the first radiation arm is 1030 mm, the length of the second radiation arm is 410 mm, and the distance between the second metal section and the metal

title: Method for reducing task unloading delay of 6G digital twin edge computing network
abstract: The invention discloses a method for reducing task unloading delay of a 6G digital twin edge computing network. The method mainly provides a new digital twin edge computing network, uses digital twinto estimate the state of an edge server so as to provide training data, formalizes a series of unloading decision optimization problems in a user moving process, uses a Lyapunov optimization method tosimplify a long-term migration cost constraint into a multi-target dynamic optimization problem, and finally, a deep learning framework based on Actor-Critic is used for solving the calculation unloading optimization problem, and the training Agent is realized by a digital twin edge calculation network. According to the method, the unloading delay, the task failure rate and the migration rate arereduced to the maximum extent on the premise of keeping relatively low system cost.

title: Two-stage temperature adjusting system based on critical temperature of organic heat carrier
abstract: The invention provides a two-stage temperature regulating system based on the critical temperature of an organic heat carrier, which can provide continuous and stable heating/cooling/constant-temperature regulation for a large-scale, multilayer or multiple production lines at the critical temperature of the organic heat carrier of 410 DEG C, and the regulation error during continuous heat supply or cooling is less than 0.5 DEG C; and the energy consumption is reduced by 30-40% by adopting a two-stage temperature regulation process flow. While energy consumption is greatly reduced, through high-temperature stable convection heat transfer, due to the fact that the process curve is smooth and the temperature gradient is small, the heat uniformity of a large-specification heat plate of a large-scale multilayer hot press or a plurality of hot presses is kept within 0.5-1.0 DEG C. The two-stage adjusting system provided by the invention can meet the production and processing requirements ofl

title: 5G antenna and electronic equipment
assignee: SHENZHEN REALME MOBILE COMMUNICATION CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a 5G antenna and electronic equipment. The 5G antenna comprises a monopole and a parasitic unit. The monopole comprises a main body and an elastic piece, the main body comprises a first end and a second end which are opposite, the distance between the elastic piece and the first end is greater than that between the elastic piece and the second end, the elastic piece is usedfor connecting an excitation source, the parasitic unit is arranged opposite to the second end, the parasitic unit comprises a first branch knot and a second branch knot, the first branch knot is provided with a first groove, at least part of the main body and at least part of the elastic piece extend into the first groove, and the distance between the second branch knot and the second end is greater than that between the first branch knot and the second end, and the connecting piece is used for connecting the first branch and the second branch.
