title: ANTENNA STRUCTURE FOR SUB-6G, PCB BOARD, AND MOBILE TERMINAL
assignee: HUIZHOU TCL MOBILE COMMUNICATION CO LTD [CN]
abstract: An antenna structure for Sub-6G, a PCB, and a mobile terminal. The antenna structure comprises a first branch and a second branch. The first branch comprises an L-shaped arm and a first longitudinal arm extending outwards from the PCB, the first longitudinal arm is connected to the ground of the PCB, and one end of the L-shaped arm is connected to the tail end of the first longitudinal arm; the second branch is L-shaped, and one end of the second branch is connected to an antenna feed end of the PCB. At least one microstrip line is connected between the first longitudinal arm and the second branch.

title: DOWNLINK TRANSMISSION METHOD AND COMMUNICATION APPARATUS
abstract: The present application provides a downlink transmission method and a communication apparatus, which can solve the problem that a data packet received by a terminal is inconsistent with a forwarded data packet, and can be applied to the fields of Internet of Things, automatic driving systems, 4G systems, 5G systems, and future communication systems such as 6G systems. The method comprises: after receiving a first data channel carrying first data from a network device, a first terminal sends to a second terminal a second data channel carrying first data, the first data carried in the second data channel and the first data carried in the first data channel are consistent in data content. Then, the second terminal performs a decoding operation on the received second data channel to obtain the first data.

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR LOAD BALANCING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: A 5th generation (5G) or 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates, compared to that of a 4th generation (4G) communication system such as a long term evolution (LTE) communication system are provided. An apparatus and a method for load balancing in a wireless communication system are provided. The apparatus includes a transceiver, a memory storing one or more instructions, and at least one processor configured to execute the one or more instructions stored in the memory to receive first information about a relation between a base station (BS) and a user equipment (UE) from each of a plurality of BSs, determine, based on the first information, a number of UEs on which handover has to be performed from among UEs served by each of the plurality of BSs, and transmit, to each of the plurality of BSs, priority information determined based on the number of UEs.

title: Small circularly polarized omnidirectional high-efficiency antenna
abstract: The utility model relates to the technical field of 5.5-6G frequency signal transmission, and discloses a small circular polarization omnidirectional high-efficiency antenna, which comprises a shell, a shell cover, a line support, an antenna line, a feeder line and a terminal, the feeder line is fixedly connected with an annular interface of the line support, the line support is located inside the shell, and the terminal is connected with the antenna line. The end, away from the line support, of the feeder penetrates through the shell cover and extends out of the shell, the end, located outside the shell, of the feeder is fixedly connected with the terminal, and the antenna line is arranged on the outer wall of the line support. According to the utility model, the limitation of the weight and the size of the original antenna is solved, and the problem of insufficient fixing and processing precision of the original antenna circuit and the circuit bracket is solved by using the LDS techn

title: Sub-6G antenna, antenna system and terminal
abstract: The embodiment of the invention discloses a Sub-6G antenna, an antenna system and a terminal. The Sub-6G antenna comprises a bearing plate, a radiation oscillator unit, a balun oscillator unit and a coupled oscillator unit. The bearing plate is provided with a first surface and a second surface; the radiation oscillator unit comprises a signal feed-in section, an impedance conversion section and a radiation section which are connected in sequence; the radiation section comprises a first radiation branch knot and a second radiation branch knot; a plurality of through holes are formed in the bearing plate, and the first radiation branch knot and the second radiation branch knot are connected through the through holes; the balun oscillator unit comprises a signal grounding section, a first balun, a second balun and a third balun; and the coupled oscillator unit is arranged on the second surface at a position corresponding to the balun oscillator unit. According to the scheme, different re

title: 6G 6G CELLULAR NETWORKS SYSTEM BASED ON FEDERATED LEARNING AND METHOD OF PERFORMING THEREOF
assignee: UNIV INDUSTRY COOPERATION GROUP KYUNG HEE UNIV [KR]
abstract: According to one embodiment of the present invention, a federated learning-based 6G architecture network system includes: a data side block including a user device module for calculating a local learning model by using a local data set and providing update information for the local learning model to a pre-allocated UAV-based wireless mobile minor module, and the UAV-based wireless mobile miner module for exchanging and authenticating, when the update information for the local learning model is received from the user device module, the update information for the local learning model through communication with another UAV-based wireless mobile miner module and updating the update information for the local learning model to a block on a blockchain according to an authentication result; a control side block including a global controller module for controlling a global switch installed in a core network module, and a global controller module including a local controller module for controlli

title: Multifunctional adaptive router
assignee: SHENZHEN JIELI T INFORMATION TECH CO LTD
abstract: The utility model relates to the technical field of routers, and discloses a multifunctional self-adaptive router, which comprises a base, the upper end of the base is connected with a router main body through a positioning mechanism, the rear side of the router main body is provided with a plurality of fixing frames through an adjusting mechanism, the inside of each fixing frame is fixedly connected with a dust shielding plate, the dust shielding plates are arranged in a transparent manner, and the dust shielding plates are arranged in a transparent manner. The dust shielding plate covers the interface of the corresponding router main body; the adjusting mechanism comprises two baffles, the two baffles are fixedly connected to the side wall, close to the upper end, of the rear portion of the router body, an installation rod is fixedly connected to the opposite side walls of the two baffles, and a fixing sleeve is rotationally connected to the position, corresponding to an interface of

title: DISPLAY DEVICE
abstract: A display device (2) comprises a first light-emitting element (6B) that emits a first light (L1) including light having a first wavelength, and a second light-emitting element (6G) that emits a second light (L2) including light having a second wavelength that differs from the first wavelength. The display device extracts each of the first light and the second light in a first direction (D1). Furthermore, the display device comprises, in a position overlapping the first light-emitting element in a second direction (D2), which is a direction opposite the first direction in relation to the first light-emitting element, a first optical layer (20B) that transmits light having the second wavelength toward the second direction, and reflects or absorbs light having the first wavelength. Moreover, the display device comprises, in a position overlapping the second light-emitting element in the second direction, a second optical layer (20G) that transmits light having the first wavelength toward

title: Endogenous security control method of 6G network, electronic equipment and storage medium
abstract: The invention provides an endogenous security control method of a 6G network, electronic equipment and a storage medium. An endogenous security control system of the 6G network comprises 6G communication equipment and a 6G communication system, and an independent security plane is newly added in a 6G network protocol layer. The 6G communication equipment sends risk event information to the 6G communication system through the security plane; the 6G communication system receives the risk event information sent by the 6G communication equipment, generates a processing strategy instruction according to the risk event information, and sends the processing strategy instruction to the 6G communication equipment; and the 6G communication equipment receives a processing strategy instruction sent by the 6G communication system and processes the risk event. By newly adding an independent security plane, a network endogenous security function is realized, so that the 6G communication network has t

title: Bulk acoustic wave resonator with groove structure
abstract: The invention discloses a bulk acoustic wave resonator with a groove structure, and particularly relates to a bulk acoustic wave resonator structure with an array type groove structure and high resonant frequency. The resonator structure comprises a bottom electrode, a piezoelectric layer, a groove structure in the piezoelectric layer and a top electrode. According to the structure, the equivalent thickness of the piezoelectric layer in the resonator can be reduced, the resonant frequency of the resonator is effectively improved, and the actual requirement of 5G/6G communication can be met.

title: GENERATION OF DEMODULATION REFERENCE SIGNALS IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating generation of demodulation reference signals in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise evaluating a capability of a mobile device and generating a demodulation reference signal sequence for the mobile device based on the capability of the mobile device. The demodulation reference signal sequence can be a first type based on the capability being a first capability and can be a second type based on the capability being a second capability.

title: SMART CASCADING SECURITY FUNCTIONS FOR 6G OR OTHER NEXT GENERATION NETWORK
abstract: In a 6G network, microservices can be utilized in the absence of a core network. For example, after a mobile device has authenticated, through its carrier network, with a transport service layer, microservices can be allocated to the mobile device without having to be transmitted via the core network. Thus, removing the core network from the process can generate a direct line of microservices from the transport layer to the end-user. Furthermore, additional microservices and/or resources can be access through a microservices library. Consequently, packets can be securely transmitted be a wireless network facilitating sending packet profile data from one to many node devices in anticipation of the packet traversing the various node devices.

title: FACILITATING NOTIFICATION AND CORRECTIVE ACTIONS RELATED TO ENDPOINT QUALITY OF SERVICE LOSSES IN FIFTH GENERATION (5G) OR OTHER ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating notification and corrective actions related to endpoint quality of service losses in advanced networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise determining, by a system comprising a processor, that a quality of service level for a mobile device within a communications network has degraded below a threshold quality of service level. The method also can comprise in response to the determining, selecting, by the system, an action to be performed at a mobile device. The action can increase the quality of service level for the mobile device above the threshold quality of service level. Further, the method can comprise providing, by the system and to the mobile device, information indicative of the action.

title: FACILITATING ENABLEMENT OF INTELLIGENT SERVICE AWARE ACCESS UTILIZING MULTIACCESS EDGE COMPUTING IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating enablement of intelligent service aware access utilizing multiaccess edge computing advanced networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise determining a network service being utilized by a first user equipment device has not been instantiated at a distributed network device and based on the first user equipment device approaching a service range of the distributed network device. The method also can comprise deploying the network service, as a microservice, at the distributed network device prior to the first user equipment device entering the service range of the distributed network device. Further, the method can comprise removing the network service from being deployed at the distributed network device based on a determination that the first user equipment device is no longer within the service range of the distributed network device and a second user equipment device is not utilizing the network service.

title: Photon-assisted vector terahertz signal communication system
abstract: The invention discloses a vector terahertz signal generation module, which comprises an optical fiber transmission module, a vector terahertz signal detection module and a vector terahertz signal emission module, the vector terahertz signal generation module comprises a first binary sequence generator, a first electric amplifier and a first intensity modulator which are connected in sequence, and the first binary sequence generator generates binary data representing data to be transmitted; the first intensity modulator carries out amplitude modulation on the optical frequency comb entering the first intensity modulator based on the binary data, and an optical signal obtained after modulation of the first intensity modulator is a vector terahertz signal with data to be transmitted. According to the invention, a digital-to-analog converter is not needed, the generation of the vector terahertz signal can be completed by using the intensity modulator, additional digital signal processing i

title: Omnidirectional broadband WiFi antenna applied to 5G and 6G frequency bands
abstract: The invention provides an omnidirectional broadband WiFi antenna applied to 5G and 6G frequency bands. The antenna comprises: a dielectric substrate provided with a first side surface and a second side surface which are opposite to each other; a coplanar waveguide structure arranged on the first side surface of the dielectric substrate and comprising a central conductor belt and radiation patches symmetrically arranged on the two sides of the central conductor belt; load patches arranged at two ends of the central conductor belt and connected with the central conductor belt; a radiation patch connecting line arranged on the second side surface of the dielectric substrate, wherein the two ends of the radiation patch connecting line are respectively connected with the radiation patches on the two sides of the central conductor belt through metal via holes penetrating through the dielectric substrate; a feeder line arranged on the second side surface of the dielectric substrate and connec

title: ANTENNA ASSEMBLY AND MOBILE TERMINAL
assignee: HUIZHOU TCL MOBILE COMMUNICATION CO LTD [CN]
abstract: Disclosed are an antenna assembly and a mobile terminal. The antenna assembly comprises a metal frame body, and at least one breakpoint is arranged in the metal frame body to partition the metal frame body into a plurality of borders; each border is integrated with a plurality of antennas; and the antennas integrated at the metal frame body comprise low-frequency 2×2 MIMO antennas, medium- and high-frequency 4×4 MIMO antennas, Sub-6G band 4×4 MIMO antennas, WiFi 2×2 MIMO antennas and dual-frequency GPS antennas.

title: FACILITATING FAST RETURN TO STAND ALONE ADVANCED NETWORKS AFTER VOICE FALL BACK
abstract: Facilitating fast return to stand alone advanced networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) after voice fall back is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise receiving, from a first network device and by a second network device, a connection request that comprises an indication of a fall back procedure. The fall back procedure can be an “RRC release and redirect” or an “IRAT Handover.” The method also can comprise facilitating control of the voice communication for the mobile device and triggering a release of the control of the mobile device from the second network device based on a determination that the voice communication has completed. The fast return procedure can be either a “RRC release and redirect’ or an “IRAT handover.” Further, the method can comprise redirecting the mobile device to a third network device selected based on a capability of the mobile device.

title: Outdoor equipment based on 5G technology
abstract: The utility model provides outdoor equipment based on a 5G technology. The outdoor equipment comprises a 5G communication module, an Ethernet controller, a Sub-6G antenna and a millimeter wave antenna. The 5G communication module is in communication connection with the Ethernet controller through a high-speed serial computer expansion bus; and the 5G communication module is respectively connected with the Sub-6G antenna and the millimeter wave antenna. The 5G communication module comprises a 5G Sub-6GHz amp which is specially designed for the application of the Internet of Things and enhanced mobile broadband; an mmWave module supports Sub-6GHz and millimeter waves at the same time, the power is larger, the coverage range is wider, secondary development can be carried out, and peripheral APs are omitted; the Ethernet controller supports communication conversion between a PCIE (Peripheral Component Interface Express) bus and a PHY (Physical Layer), provides a higher network port transmi

title: Sub-6G multi-band antenna system and mobile terminal
assignee: KUNSHAN YIQU INFORMATION TECH RESEARCH INSTITUTE CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a Sub-6G multi-band antenna system and a mobile terminal. The antenna system comprises a metal floor, a first radiation arm and a second radiation arm, the first radiation arm and the second radiation arm are arranged on the same side of the metal floor, the first radiation arm comprises a first metal section and a second metal section, one end of the first metal section is connected to the metal floor, the other end of the first metal section is connected to the second metal section, the second radiation arm comprises a third metal section and a fourth metal section, one end of the third metal section is connected to the metal floor, and the other end of the third metal section is connected to the fourth metal section; and the length of the first radiation arm is 10-30 mm, the length of the second radiation arm is 4-10 mm, and the distance between the second metal section and the metal floor and the distance between the fourth metal section and the metal floor a

title: Sub-6G multi-band antenna system and mobile terminal
assignee: KUNSHAN YIQU INFORMATION TECH RESEARCH INSTITUTE CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a Sub-6G multi-band antenna system and a mobile terminal. The antenna system comprises a metal floor, a first radiation arm, a second radiation arm and a matching tuning network, the first radiation arm comprises a first metal section and a second metal section, one end of the first metal section is connected to the metal floor, the other end of the first metal section is connected to the second metal section the second radiation arm comprises a third metal section and a fourth metal section, one end of the third metal section is connected to the metal floor, and the other end of the third metal section is connected to the fourth metal section; and the length of the first radiation arm is 10-30 mm, the length of the second radiation arm is 4-10 mm, and the distance between the second metal section and the metal floor and the distance between the fourth metal section and the metal floor are both 0.5-3 mm. According to the Sub-6G multi-band antenna system and the m

title: Antenna system and electronic device
assignee: AAC PRECISION MANUFACTURE TECH CHANGZHOU CO LTD
abstract: The present invention provides an antenna system. The antenna system comprises a system ground unit, a metal frame, and a main board. The metal frame and the system ground unit are arranged at intervals to form a clearance area, the metal frame is a first radiator, the antenna system further comprises a second radiator, a switch, a capacitor and a main matching circuit, the second radiator is located in the clearance area, the main board comprises a feed point, the metal frame comprises a first connection portion and a second connection portion, one end of the capacitor is connected with the second radiator, the other end of the capacitor is connected with the main matching circuit, and the second radiator is connected with the main board through the switch. Compared to the prior art, in acondition without increasing the number of antennas, a Sub 6G is put with a 4G antenna together so as to achieve reduction of change of the environment and more clearance requirements without increasi

title: Flood-control 6G base station box
abstract: A 6G base station box capable of preventing flood comprises a base part, a lock catch part and a machine case part. A shell of the base part is fixed underground, the upper end face of the shell is flush with the ground, a box body lifting mechanism and a rope releasing mechanism are installed in the shell, when general flood happens, the box body lifting mechanism jacks the machine case part to a certain height, and when flood happens, the rope releasing mechanism drags the machine case part to reduce damage of the flood to the machine case part; and the lock catch part is fixedly installed in a groove in the upper end face of the base part, the machine case part is fixed over the lock catch part through a lock, the base part works to control whether the machine case part is locked in the lock catch part or not, and the machine case part can be separated from the lock catch part when flood happens. According to the 6G base station box capable of preventing flood, different response mo

title: FACILITATING ADAPTIVE POWER SPECTRAL DENSITY WITH CHROMATIC SPECTRUM OPTIMIZATION IN FIFTH GENERATION (5G) OR OTHER ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating adaptive power spectral density with chromatic spectrum optimization in advanced networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise evaluating, by a system comprising a processor, a capture rate of mobile devices within a radio access network. The capture rate is representative of a quantity of mobile devices using a millimeter wave spectrum of the radio access network. The method also can comprise facilitating, by the system, an adjustment to a power spectral density of the radio access network based on a determination that the capture rate fails to satisfy a target capture rate of mobile devices using the millimeter wave spectrum.

title: ALLOCATING AND EXTRAPOLATING DATA FOR AUGMENTED REALITY FOR 6G OR OTHER NEXT GENERATION NETWORK
abstract: Global position system tagging the movement of an object and extrapolating its direction and speed can be used for various services including emergency-based services. Location data can be computed using edge computing nodes. The extrapolation system can account for feedback from responding user devices and utilize the user device's location at the time of reporting to facilitate determining the direction, location, and/or speed of a moving object. This data can then be utilized to generate augmented reality displays for mobile devices and/or vehicles that utilize the system. The ability to calculate directional information with edge computing nodes can comprise an ability to add enriched data by predicting an object's whereabouts, route, and/or final destination.

title: 6G network generic optical communication architecture constructed based on 6G photonics
assignee: WUHAN RES INSTITUTE OF POSTS & TELECOMMUNICATIONS
abstract: The invention relates to a 6G network generic optical communication architecture constructed based on 6G photonics. According to an exemplary embodiment, the invention provides a floodlight communication base station, which comprises a floodlight air interface device, and the floodlight air interface device comprises a broadband floodlight radiation array which comprises a plurality of floodlight radiation units and is used for transmitting a floodlight signal to a floodlight terminal in a floodlight frequency band; and the broadband floodlight detection array comprises a plurality of broadband floodlight detection units and is used for detecting floodlight signals emitted by the floodlight terminal.

title: Terahertz wave separator based on bilinear metamaterial structure and applied to 6G system
abstract: The invention relates to a terahertz wave separator applied to a 6G system and based on a bilinear metamaterial structure. The terahertz wave separator is used for the wavelength division multiplexing of a 6G wavelength division multiplexing system and belongs to the technical field of communication wave separators. The terahertz wave separator is composed of a plurality of identical metamaterial unit structures, the metamaterial unit structures are periodically arranged along an x direction and a y direction, and each metamaterial unit structure is of a square structure. The side length of each metamaterial unit structure is P, each metamaterial unit structure comprises a dielectric layer and a metal pattern layer on the surface of the dielectric layer, and each metal pattern layer is composed of two linear cuboid metal wires and a coupling wire in the middle. When terahertz waves with two specific frequencies are obliquely incident to the surface of a metamaterial wave separator, the

title: Symmetrical multi-slot terahertz 6G communication application frequency band antenna based on butterfly-like structure
abstract: The invention discloses a symmetrical multi-slot terahertz 6G communication application frequency band antenna based on a butterfly-like structure, and belongs to the field of terahertz antennas. The radiation patch comprises a butterfly patch body and a circular patch body, the circular patch body is arranged in the center of the butterfly patch body, second rectangular grooves with the same width are formed in the left top end and the right top end of the butterfly patch body towards the center of the circular patch body respectively, and first rectangular grooves with the same width are formed in the two sides of each second rectangular groove towards the direction of a radiation arm respectively. A rectangular gap is formed in a symmetric line, in the same direction as the radiation arm, of the circular patch, symmetric circular grooves are formed in the two sides of the rectangular gap, and a whisker patch group is arranged at the top end of the circular patch. The antenna has the

title: Television and SUB-6G antenna assembly thereof
assignee: SHENZHEN ZHONGTIAN COMMUNICATION TECH SHARES CO LTD
abstract: The invention is suitable for the technical field of antennas, and provides a television and an SUB-6G antenna assembly thereof. The embodiment of the invention provides a SUB-6G antenna assembly applied to a television, the multiple sets of antennas are arranged on the inner side of the shell of the OPS box, each set of antennas comprises the two antennas with the same working frequency, shape and size, at least one set of antennas is arranged in an isolated mode, the communication rate can be effectively increased, the requirements of the 8K high-definition television are met, the influence of the external environment on the antenna performance is effectively reduced, the universality of the antenna is good, and the interference between the antennas can be reduced by arranging at least one group of antennas in an isolated manner, so that the working performance of the antenna is more stable.

title: Hybrid beam forming method and device and storage medium
abstract: The invention discloses a hybrid beam forming method and device and a storage medium, and the method comprises the steps: taking an analog beam matrix, a digital beam matrix and a digital merging matrix as variables, and building beam forming objective functions of communication and radar according to a least square criterion; setting weight factors, and combining a joint objective function; decomposing the three variables into three variable solving sub-problems through an alternating minimization criterion, and separately solving the three variable solving sub-problems; and iterating the solving processes of the three variable solving sub-problems by using an alternating iteration method based on the joint objective function to obtain the optimal solutions to the analog beam matrix, the digital beam matrix and the digital merging matrix. Based on the optimal solutions, the joint design of hybrid beam forming considering both communication and radar performance can be realized. The sc

title: Transmission method and network side device
assignee: RES INST CHINA MOBILE COMMUNICATIONS CORP
abstract: The present invention provides a transmission method and a network side device, belonging to the technical field of wireless communications, the method comprising the steps of: acquiring a first channel matrix, the first channel matrix being a channel matrix between each transmitting antenna and a receiving antenna in a multiple-input multiple-output antenna array based on orbital angular momentum; and performing precoding transmission by using a second channel matrix, wherein the second channel matrix is an equivalent matrix of the first channel matrix. On one hand, the capacity of a communication system can be improved through the OAM-MIMO multiplexing technology, on the other hand, interference among multiple users can be restrained through the precoding technology, and the method is particularly suitable for a long-distance LOS scene. The network side device can also provide services for novel terminals (such as unmanned aerial vehicles) in a future 6G network.

title: LIGHT-EMITTING ELEMENT AND LIGHT-EMITTING DEVICE
abstract: Light-emitting elements (6R, 6G, 6B) are each provided with a first electrode (8), a second electrode (10), and a light-emitting layer (12) disposed between the first electrode and the second electrode. The light-emitting layer includes quantum dots (22R) and a cyclic organic compound (24R). At least a portion of the atoms forming the ring of the cyclic organic compound are coordinated at the quantum dots.

title: Antenna device and mobile terminal
abstract: The utility model provides an antenna device and a mobile terminal, the antenna device comprises a metal frame, a first gap is formed on the metal frame, and the metal frame is divided into a first metal section and a second metal section through the first gap. And the first metal section, the first gap and the second metal section jointly form a 4G antenna and a Sub 6G antenna. According to the antenna device provided by the utility model, the 4G antenna and the Sub 6G antenna are jointly formed through the first metal section, the first gap and the second metal section, and the 4G antenna and the Sub 6G antenna formed on the metal frame do not need to occupy the layout space in the mobile terminal, and do not need to provide an independent clearance area for the antenna. And the 4G antenna and the Sub 6G antenna can share the first metal section, the first gap and the second metal section, so that the antenna design of the mobile terminal can be simplified.

title: SYSTEM MODEL AND ARCHITECTURE FOR MOBILE INTEGRATED ACCESS AND BACKHAUL IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating operation and support of mobile relays based on an integrated access and backhaul concept for advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided. An embodiment relates to a communication network architecture that can comprise a control plane architecture of a relay node device. The control plane architecture can comprise a star-type architecture. Further, the communication network architecture can comprise a user plane architecture of the relay node device. The user plane architecture can be separated from (or independent of) the control plane architecture. Further, the user plane architecture can comprise a multi-hop architecture. The relay node device can be configured to operate according to a fifth generation wireless network communication protocol, or other advanced communication protocols.

title: 6G base station box with windproof function
abstract: The utility model discloses a 6G base station box with a windproof function. The 6G base station box comprises a base station box body part, a wind power rotating part, a windproof part and a fixed box body part, the wind power rotating part, the windproof part and the fixed box body parts are installed in the base station box body part, the wind power rotating part is installed at the upper end of the windproof part, and the fixed box body parts are installed on the two sides of the wind power rotating part and the windproof part; the windproof part comprises a bevel gear B, a transmission mechanism, an idle gear, a serial mechanism B and a shaking windproof structure; and the number of the shaking wind-proof structures is four, each shaking wind-proof structure is installed below the corresponding belt wheel of the serial connection mechanism B, a rotating shaft in each shaking wind-proof structure is fixedly connected with the corresponding belt wheel in the serial connection mechan

title: AUTONOMOUS VEHICLE AND METHOD FOR CONTROLLING THE SAME
abstract: An autonomous vehicle and a method for controlling the same are disclosed. A method for controlling an autonomous vehicle including a baseband modem and a plurality of distributed antennas includes applying an initial clock of the baseband modem to clocks of the plurality of distributed antennas, receiving base station data from an external base station via the plurality of distributed antennas to which the initial clock is applied, and synchronizing a clock of the baseband modem with a clock of the base station based on the base station data, and thus can achieve low cost design using a data clock recovery. The autonomous vehicle, a user terminal, and a server can be associated with an artificial intelligence module, a unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV), a robot, an augmented reality (AR) device, a virtual reality (VR) device, devices related to 3G, 4G, 5G and/6G services.

title: Low-spurious and low-phase noise power signal source
abstract: The utility model discloses a power signal source with low stray and low phase noise. A radio frequency signal source of the utility model comprises a reference source circuit, a phase-locked loop circuit, a filter circuit and a gain control circuit. The reference source circuit is used for providing a reference signal for the phase-locked loop circuit; the phase-locked loop circuit adopts a broadband frequency synthesizer integrated with a VCO (Voltage Controlled Oscillator) to form a closed loop to generate a frequency signal; the filter circuit is used for realizing segmented filtering of the frequency signal through shunting and outputting a 20M-6G signal; and the gain control circuit performs gain control on the 20M-6G signal output by the filter circuit to obtain the required signal power and output the signal power. According to the signal source, the broadband frequency synthesizer integrated with the VCO is adopted to replace a traditional VCO, cost is reduced, and stray is op

title: Intelligent metasurface-assisted large-scale passive random access method
abstract: The invention discloses an intelligent metasurface-assisted large-scale passive random access method. In a 6G wireless network, a base station equipped with a large-scale antenna array serves a large number of single-antenna devices at the same time with the assistance of a metasurface. In any given time slot, only a small part of devices is activated. The invention provides a two-stage unlicensed protocol combining data detection and channel estimation. In the stage I, only one activation device sends a pilot frequency sequence to the base station through the intelligent metasurface, and the base station estimates a channel between the metasurface and the base station by using a bilinear generalized approximate message passing algorithm. In the stage II, all the activation devices send data to the base station through the intelligent metasurface by using a public codebook, and the base station detects the data and estimates channels between the activation devices and the metasurface b

title: KEY EXCHANGE SYSTEM, INFORMATION PROCESSOR, KEY EXCHANGE METHOD AND PROGRAM
abstract: To provide an asynchronous ID-basis multi party key exchange protocol.SOLUTION: A key exchange system 1 includes: a first terminal 11; one or more second terminals 121 to 12n belonging to the same group; and an auxiliary server 20 for supporting a group key exchange between the first terminal and the second terminal. The first terminal comprises: first generation means which uses a public key generated in the auxiliary server to generate a group key generation key and a cryptogram to which the generation key is encrypted; second generation means for generating a group key by using a generating key; and third generation means which encrypts a cryptogram or generates signcryption information. The auxiliary server has forth generation means for generating information to which the information generated by the third generation means is re-encrypted by using a pre-calculation key of a first terminal generation. The second terminal comprises: fifth generation means for generating a cryptogram

title: Back drilling composite cover plate for controlling drilling depth of circuit board
abstract: The utility model relates to a back drilling composite cover plate for controlling the drilling depth of a circuit board, along with the popularization of 4G, 5G and future 6G communication, the back drilling process will become an indispensable manufacturing process in the manufacturing process of the circuit board for communication, and the back drilling composite cover plate comprises a processing surface insulating layer, a conductive layer carrier and a conductive layer; the conducting layer carrier and the conducting layer are combined in a manner of electroplating, vacuum evaporation deposition or coating; the processing surface insulating layer and the conductive layer carrier are combined in a bonding, fitting or pressing manner; due to the adoption of the composite structure, on the premise that the drilling precision is ensured, the selection range of materials of an insulating layer of a processing surface is wider, for example, an insulating environment-friendly material w

title: 6G-oriented Raman microstructure optical fiber amplifier
abstract: The utility model relates to a 6G-oriented Raman microstructure optical fiber amplifier, which comprises an optical transmitter, a first section of optical fiber pumping source, a first wave combiner, an amplification part GeO2-doped microstructure optical fiber, a first wave separator, a second section of optical fiber pumping source, a second wave combiner, a compensation part GeO2-doped microstructure optical fiber, a second wave separator and an optical receiver. And the amplification gain is as high as 35.72 dB, which means that a higher amplification factor can be obtained at the output end of the amplifier while more signal light is amplified.

title: AUTONOMOUS DRIVING VEHICLE AND CONTROL METHOD THEREOF
abstract: Disclosed are an autonomous driving vehicle and a control method thereof. According to one embodiment of the present invention, a method of controlling an autonomous driving vehicle including a baseband modem and a plurality of distributed antennas comprises: a step of applying an initial clock of the baseband modem to the clocks of the plurality of distributed antennas; a step of receiving base station data from an external base station through the plurality of distributed antennas to which the initial clock is applied; and a step of synchronizing the clock of the baseband modem with the clock of the base station on the basis of the base station data. Accordingly, a low-cost design using data clock recovery is possible. The autonomous driving vehicle, a user terminal, and a server of the present specification can interwork with an artificial intelligence (AI) module, a drone (unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)), a robot, an augmented reality (AR) device, a virtual reality (VR) device, devi

title: GENERATION NODE B (GNB) CONFIGURED FOR SLOT-LESS OPERATION AT FREQUENCIES ABOVE A 52.6 GHZ CARRIER FREQUENCY
abstract: A generation node B (gNB) for a fifth-generation (5G) new radio (NR) or a sixth-generation (6G) network is configured for slot-less operation at frequencies above a 52.6 GHz carrier frequency. The gNB may generate signalling to configure a user equipment (UE) with a gap between demodulation reference signal (DMRS) symbols for an associated physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH). The gNB may also encode the DMRS symbols for transmission in accordance with the gap and may encode the associated PDSCH for transmission during the gap between the DMRS symbol transmissions at symbol times following the DMRS symbols.

title: METHOD AND APPARTUS FOR DESIGNING AND OPERATING MULTI-DIMENSIONAL CONSTELLATION
abstract: In a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE), a method of a first terminal in a wireless communication system is disclosed and may include performing channel measurement, based on one or more first reference signals received from a base station; identifying channel distribution information between the first terminal and the base station, based on the measured channel; selecting one or more representative channel vectors (RCVs), based on the identified channel distribution information; generating one or more constellations corresponding to the selected one or more RCVs; transmitting constellation set information including the generated one or more constellations to the base station; and performing communication with the base station, based on the generated one or more constellations.

title: Deployment of multiple wavebands of 6G network pan-optical communication architecture constructed by 6G photonics
assignee: WUHAN RES INSTITUTE OF POSTS & TELECOMMUNICATIONS
abstract: The invention relates to multi-band deployment of a floodlight communication air interface architecture for a 6G network, all floodlight bands are uniformly integrated and deployed, and different transmission rates and different coverage ranges are specifically provided in a sight distance range and a non-sight distance range according to different application scenes. The invention provides a pan-optical communication air interface device, a base station and a terminal for a 6G network. According to the application, the defects caused by transmission of a single wave band (such as a visible light wave band) are avoided, the transmission characteristics of direct incidence, reflection, transmission and scattering of various floodlight frequency bands are fully utilized, and floodlight coverage switching of four different frequency bands can be simultaneously realized at different time points in a cell; therefore, the near, medium and long-distance communication links with different rate

title: Method for establishing Internet of Vehicles V2X communication link based on millimeter waves
abstract: The invention discloses an Internet of Vehicles V2X communication link establishment method based on millimeter waves, and belongs to the field of Internet of Vehicles communication in the Internet of Vehicles, and the method specifically comprises the following steps that a target vehicle broadcasts and sends vehicle information in a sub-6G frequency band, and a transmitting vehicle receives and stores the vehicle information; when the two vehicles perform communication interaction, the central processing unit identifies a target vehicle by using a deep learning algorithm; meanwhile, whether shielding exists between antennas of the two vehicles or not is judged; when no shielding exists, the transmitting vehicle selects an optimal communication beam from the beams in all directions according to the relative angle of the antennas of the two vehicles; when shielding exists, a transmitting vehicle identifies all convex reflecting surfaces in the surrounding environment and selects an opt

title: MULTILAYER CERAMIC CAPACITOR
abstract: A ceramic sintered body 1 comprises dielectric layers 6a-6g and internal electrodes (2a-2f), which are alternately laminated. The dielectric layers (6a-6g) are mainly formed from a perovskite compoundrepresented by general formula ABO3; and the perovskite compound contains at least Ti and a volatile element that is solid-solved in the B-site. The internal electrodes (2a-2f) are formed from a basemetal material that contains a volatile element. This ceramic sintered body (1) is configured such that the content of the volatile elements is more than 0 part by mole but 0.2 part by mole or less relative to (100) parts by mole of the constituent elements of the B-site excluding the volatile element. Consequently, the present invention enables the achievement of a multilayer ceramic capacitor which is capable of suppressing deterioration of the insulation performance over time and thereby ensuring good reliability even if the dielectric layers are formed from a perovskite compound and the int

title: Steel mesh device for preventing tin slag from being generated in reflow oven after SMT (Surface Mount Technology)
assignee: SHENZHEN CHENGHE HONGYE TECH CO LTD
abstract: The utility model discloses a steel mesh device for preventing tin slag from being generated in a reflow oven after SMT (Surface Mount Technology) surface mounting. Comprising a 0402 type element steel mesh and bonding pad overlapping structure, a 0603 type element steel mesh and bonding pad overlapping structure, a 0805 type element steel mesh and bonding pad overlapping structure and a 1206/1210 type element steel mesh and bonding pad overlapping structure, and the 0402 type element steel mesh and bonding pad overlapping structure comprises a 0402 type element bonding pad and a 0402 type element steel mesh window. The printing quantity of the solder paste is matched with the size of a bonding pad, and the solder paste passes through a reflow oven after SMT, so that no solder splash is left on the PCB or the FPC, potential quality hazards caused by the fact that fine solder splash is possibly hidden below components are prevented, the quality requirement of 5G or 6G high-end products

title: Cache enabling user association method suitable for heterogeneous communication network
abstract: The invention discloses a cache enabling user association method suitable for a heterogeneous communication network, and the method comprises the following steps: a millimeter wave base station caches the most popular content, and a sub-6G base station caches a part of the most popular content and a part of the most popular content except the cached content of the millimeter wave base station. When the two base stations cache the content required by the user, the user firstly associates to the millimeter wave base station which meets the signal-to-noise ratio condition and has the strongest signal, otherwise, a plurality of sub-6G base stations with the strongest signal perform joint transmission; when only the sub-6G base station caches the content required by the user, a plurality of sub-6G base stations with the strongest signals carry out joint transmission; and in other cases, the sub-6G base station with the strongest signal retrieves the content from the central server through t

title: 6G communication microwave power detection system based on radio frequency diode
abstract: The invention discloses a 6G communication microwave power detection system based on a radio frequency diode. A substrate integrated waveguide is transversely provided with a through rectangular cavity, a coaxial line feed microstrip patch antenna is arranged in the rectangular cavity, and the microstrip patch antenna is electrically connected with the substrate integrated waveguide through an impedance matching network equivalent to a band-pass filter; the substrate integrated waveguide is electrically connected with the radio frequency diode and the low-pass filter through the impedance matching network, the low-pass filter is electrically connected with the data processing module, and the radio frequency diode rectifies received microwave signals of the 6G communication frequency band. An output signal of the radio frequency diode is sent to a low-pass filter to filter fundamental frequency and higher harmonics, a direct-current component is output, the direct-current component is s

title: ANTENNA STRUCTURE FOR SUB-6G, PCB BOARD, AND MOBILE TERMINAL
assignee: HUIZHOU TCL MOBILE COMMUNICATION CO LTD [CN]
abstract: An antenna structure, PCB and mobile terminal for Sub-6G. The antenna structure includes a first branch and a second branch. The first branch includes an L-shaped arm and a first longitudinal arm extending outwardly from the PCB. The first longitudinal arm is connected to the ground of the PCB. One end of the L-shaped arm is connected to the end of the first longitudinal arm. The second branch is L-shaped, and one end of the second branch is connected to the antenna feed point of the PCB. The first longitudinal arm is connected to the second branch through at least one microstrip line.

title: Worldwide indirect to direct on-demand eye doctor support refraction system via a remote administration tool mobile application on any portable Electronic device with broadband wireless cellular network technology 4G ,5G , 6G or Wifi wireless network protocols to interconnect both systems.
abstract: The present disclosure describes clinical workflows, methods, and systems used to perform an indirect to direct subjective refraction to a patient with a mobile smartphone application that works as a encrypted remote administration tool in any portable electronic device and interconnect both systems via by 4G, 5G, 6G, or Wifi. According to various embodiments, an eye doctor may utilize a remote administration tool (RAT) or (RAS) remote access software application on a portable electronic device (PED) (smartphone, tablet, or laptop) to view and control the main control base (MCB) anywhere in the world to interconnect both systems. The eye doctor can perform an on-demand live subjective vision refraction via RAT technology. Furthermore, the eye doctor can control the (MCB) that can control, exam chair, digital phoropter, vision chart software, robotic phoropter arm, exam chair height, exam room lights, and near robotic chart arm anywhere in the world.

title: FACILITATION OF DYNAMIC EDGE COMPUTATIONS FOR 6G OR OTHER NEXT GENERATION NETWORK
abstract: Peripheral devices such as wearables can link to mobile devices to access network elements. These peripheral devices can comprise a communication part and a processing part. Each of these parts, specially the processing part of the next item in a communication chain, can be multiple times larger and stronger than the previous linked item. For instance, the peripheral device can be connected to the mobile device which can be in line connected to an NodeB device. Because the processing power of mobile device can be several times stronger than the peripheral, a mobile edge computing platform and a multi-access application function facilitate sharing of processing power capabilities between the peripheral devices, mobile devices, and/or the NodeB devices.

title: FILM STRETCHING APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR PRODUCING FILM
abstract: PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To reduce damage of a film caused by volatile components generated inside of a stretching furnace.SOLUTION: There is provided a film stretching apparatus (5) which comprises a stretching furnace (6) separated into a plurality of air flow control zones (6a to 6g), wherein when the stretching furnace (6) is separated into 7 air flow control zones (6a to 6g), the total exhaust amount of 3 air flow control zones (6a to 6c) positioned on the side of a carry-in port (61) is larger than the total exhaust amount of 3 air flow control zones (6e to 6g) positioned on the side of a carry-out port (62).SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 5

title: FACILITATING AUTOMATIC LATENCY DISCOVERY AND DYNAMIC NETWORK SELECTION USING DATA ANALYTICS IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating automatic latency discovery and dynamic network selection using data analytics in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise inserting, by a system comprising a processor, first information indicative of a first identified time into a first field of a message. Also, the method can comprise transmitting, by the system, the message to a defined device. Further, the method can comprise determining, by the system, an end-to-end latency for the message based on a response received from the defined device in reply to the message. The response can comprise second information indicative of a second identified time that the message was received at the defined device.

title: FACILITATION OF DYNAMIC EDGE COMPUTATIONS FOR 6G OR OTHER NEXT GENERATION NETWORK
abstract: In 6G, there are multiple radios that can cover the same location at any time, and yet radio failure can occur. However, a mobile edge computing (MEC) platform can increase the footprint of adjacent radios to compensate for a failed radio. To reduce the failure interruption and maintain a quality of experience for a subscriber, the MEC can utilize a virtual session capability to communicate radio change of service characteristics to a service provider. Consequently, the change in service characteristics can comprise an expanded coverage area for adjacent radios such that a mobile device of the subscriber can take advantage of the expanded coverage area without experiencing an interruption in service.

title: Wireless network card
abstract: The invention discloses a wireless network card, which comprises a PCB (printed circuit board) mainboard, a USB (universal serial bus) interface and an SIM (subscriber identity module) card seat are arranged on the PCB mainboard, an SIM card is placed in the SIM card seat, and the wireless network card is characterized in that a square slot window is arranged on the inner layer of the PCB mainboard below the SIM card, a three-finger feed network is arranged on the PCB mainboard, and coupled feed is performed on the slot window to form a slot antenna. The size of the square window is larger than that of the SIM card, and the radiation gain of the antenna in a high-frequency part is changed by adjusting the size of the square window and the distance between the square window and the edge of the SIM card. The slot antenna works on a plurality of frequency points of 0.8-6G. The slot antenna works at a working frequency point of 0.8 to 0.9 GHz and a working frequency point of 2.4 GHz of a W

title: SECONDARY BATTERY, DEVICE, ARTIFICIAL GRAPHITE, AND PREPARATION METHOD THEREFOR
abstract: The present application discloses a secondary battery, an apparatus including the secondary battery, artificial graphite and a preparation method thereof. The secondary battery includes a negative electrode plate, the negative electrode plate including a negative active material, wherein the negative active material comprises an artificial graphite having a volume average particle size Dv50 of 12µm to 22µm, and the artificial graphite satisfies: 12≤Dv50×SSA≤25, in which SSA is the specific surface area of the artificial graphite, in m<2>/g; the negative electrode plate has a compaction density of 1.6g/cm<3> to 1.75g/cm<3>, and the negative electrode plate has an OI value of ≤15, wherein the OI value of the negative electrode plate represents a ratio C004/C110, in which C004 is the peak area of the diffraction peak of 004 crystal plane of the artificial graphite in the negative electrode plate and C110 is the peak area of the diffraction peak of 110 crystal plane of the artificial graph

title: Novel antenna anti-interference module
abstract: The invention discloses a novel antenna anti-interference module. Wireless terminal equipment comprises a PCB main board, and a USB interface and an SIM card seat are arranged on the PCB main board; an SIM card is placed in the SIM card seat, a square gap window is formed in the inner layer of the PCB main board below the SIM card, a three-finger type feed network is arranged on the PCB main board, coupling feed is conducted on the gap window, and a gap antenna is formed. The size of the square window is greater than that of the SIM card, and the radiation gain of the antenna in a high-frequency part is changed by adjusting the size of the square window and the distance between the square window and the edge of the SIM card. And the slot antenna works on a plurality of frequency points of 0.8-6G. And the slot antenna works at a working frequency point of 0.8 to 0.9 GHz and a working frequency point of 2.4 GHz of a WLAN. title: Sub-6G antenna, antenna system and terminal
abstract: The embodiment of the utility model discloses a Sub-6G antenna, an antenna system and a terminal. The Sub-6G antenna comprises a bearing plate, a radiation oscillator unit, a Balun oscillator unit and a coupling oscillator unit, the bearing plate is provided with a first surface and a second surface; the radiation oscillator unit comprises a signal feed-in section, an impedance conversion section and a radiation section which are connected in sequence; the radiation section comprises a radiation first branch knot and a radiation second branch knot; a plurality of through holes are formed in the bearing plate, and the first radiation branch knot and the second radiation branch knot are connected through the through holes; the balun oscillator unit comprises a signal grounding section, a first balun, a second balun and a third balun; and the coupled oscillator unit is arranged on the second surface at a position corresponding to the balun oscillator unit. title: FACILITATING ENABLEMENT OF INTELLIGENT SERVICE AWARE ACCESS UTILIZING MULTIACCESS EDGE COMPUTING IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating enablement of intelligent service aware access utilizing multiaccess edge computing advanced networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise determining a network service being utilized by a first user equipment device has not been instantiated at a distributed network device and based on the first user equipment device approaching a service range of the distributed network device. The method also can comprise deploying the network service, as a microservice, at the distributed network device prior to the first user equipment device entering the service range of the distributed network device. Further, the method can comprise removing the network service from being deployed at the distributed network device based on a determination that the first user equipment device is no longer within the service range of the distributed network device and a second user equipment device is not utilizing the network service.

title: FACILITATING AN INTERFERENCE LEAKAGE DEPENDENT RESOURCE RESERVATION PROTOCOL IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating an interference leakage dependent resource reservation protocol in advanced networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise defining, by a system comprising a memory and a processor, a resource reservation procedure that associates respective amounts of reserved resources available for a mobile device based on a transmission beam width and a transmission power level of the mobile device. The method also can comprise selecting, by the system, an amount of reserved resources from the respective amounts of reserved resources available based on the transmission beam width and the transmission power level of the mobile device.

title: Code domain compatible code acquisition method, and adaptive transmission method and system
assignee: BEIJING ZHONGKE JINGSHANG TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a code domain compatible code acquisition method, and an adaptive transmission method and system. A nested structure between check matrixes is realized on the basis of modular operation, so that the check matrixes have good compatibility therebetween, and then code domains are represented through the check matrixes, so that code domain compatible codes with high performance,high compatibility, the same code rate and code length and different code domains are constructed; when a wireless channel transmission condition meets a set requirement, namely, the transmission condition is better, small code domain codes in the code domain compatible codes are used for transmission; and, when the transmission conditions do not meet the set requirements, namely, the transmission condition is poor, a code of a large code domain in the code domain compatible code is used for transmission. The mode can maintain constant instantaneous transmission efficiency while ensuring trans

title: FACILITATING SELECTION OF DEMODULATION REFERENCE SIGNAL PORTS IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating selection of demodulation reference signal port combinations in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise evaluating a capability of a mobile device. The operations can also comprise assigning a first group of port combinations for the mobile device based on the capability of the mobile device being a first capability and a second group of port combinations for the mobile device based on the capability of the mobile device being a second capability, resulting in a port combination assignment. The port combination assignment can mitigate a peak-to-average power ratio value.

title: LIGHT-EMITTING ELEMENT AND DISPLAY DEVICE
abstract: A light-emitting element (6B, 6G, 6R) comprises an anode (8) and a cathode (20), and includes between the anode and the cathode, in order from the anode side, a first hole transport layer (10), a second hole transport layer (12), a light-emitting layer (14), a first electron transport layer (16), and a second electron transport layer (18). The energy level difference in the HOMO level between the second hole transport layer and the light-emitting layer on the second hole transport layer side is 0.0 to 0.15 eV, and the energy level difference in the LUMO level between the first electron transport layer and the light-emitting layer on the first electron transport layer side is 0.0 to 0.15 eV. The second electron transport layer is a mixed layer which includes an organic material having an electron transport property and an electron accepting material, and proportion of the electron accepting material is more than 50 mass%.

title: Miniaturized WiFi antenna applied to 5G and 6G frequency bands
abstract: The utility model provides a miniaturized WiFi antenna applied to 5G and 6G frequency bands, and the antenna comprises a dielectric substrate which is provided with a first side surface and a second side surface which are opposite to each other; the first radiation line is arranged on the first side surface and comprises a first radiation patch, two second radiation patches and three first radiation patches which are arranged in sequence, and feeder lines arranged between the adjacent radiation patches; the second radiation line is arranged on the second side surface and comprises three first radiation patches and two second radiation patches which are arranged in sequence, and feeder lines arranged between the adjacent radiation patches; protruding parts are arranged at the positions where the first radiation patch and the second radiation patch are connected with the feeder line. title: Broadband ceramic inverted-L antenna covering Sub-6GHz frequency band
assignee: NANJING INTELLIGENT HIGH END EQUIPMENT INDUSTRY RES INSTITUTE CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a broadband ceramic inverted-L antenna covering a Sub-6GHz frequency band. The broadband ceramic inverted-L antenna comprises a metal floor, a coaxial line fixed on the metal floor, and an inverted-L-shaped dielectric waveguide connected with the coaxial line. The coaxial line penetrates through a hole reserved in the metal floor, extends out for a certain length and then is connected with the bottom of the inverted L-shaped dielectric waveguide; and the inverted-L-shaped dielectric waveguide is composed of a first part perpendicular to the metal floor and a second part parallel to the metal floor. The broadband ceramic inverted-L antenna is a non-resonant dielectric inverted-L antenna which is simple in structure, small in size and capable of covering a wide frequency band range, the impedance bandwidth of a traditional metal inverted-L antenna can be improved by about 10 times, the impedance bandwidth ranges from 3.3 GHz to 5.2 GHz, and the antenna can cover t

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CONTROLLING RE-TRANSMISSIONS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). Disclosed is a method by a base station for use in a non-terrestrial network (NTN) comprising: transmitting one of a predetermined number of re-transmissions including a packet in a blind re-transmission mode to an user equipment (UE); receiving a channel quality indicator (CQI) message after the transmission; in response to a particular configuration of the received CQI message, terminating transmission of any remaining of the predetermined number of re-transmissions.

title: OPERATION KNOB DEVICE, MOUNTING JIG, AND OPERATION KNOB DEVICE UNIT
abstract: To improve mountability to a display panel.SOLUTION: An operation knob device unit includes: a cylindrical holder 20; a transmission member 36 arranged in the holder 20; an operation member 30 for bringing the transmission member 36 closer to and away from a display panel 1; and a mounting jig 5 for mounting the holder 20 to the display panel 1. The holder 20 has a locking portion 22 at a portion protruding from an end portion 30d of an outer wall portion 30b on the display panel 1 side. The mounting jig 5 includes: a first member 6 that projects inward from an edge 6k of an insertion groove 6g and has locking pieces 6l to 6n capable of being locked to the locking portion 22 by insertion of the holder 20 into the insertion groove 6g; and a second member 7 for closing an opening portion 6j of the insertion groove 6g.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 2A

title: Reconstruction substrate for radio frequency applications
abstract: The present disclosure relates to methods and apparatus for forming thin form factor reconstruction substrates and semiconductor device packages for radio frequency applications. The substrates and package structures described herein can be utilized in high density 2D and 3D integrated devices for 4G, 5G, 6G, and other wireless network systems. In one embodiment, a silicon substrate is structured by laser ablation to include a cavity for placement of a semiconductor die and vias for deposition of conductive interconnects. Further, the one or more cavities are structured to be filled or occupied with a flowable dielectric material. Integration of one or more radio frequency components adjacent to the dielectric filled cavity enables improved performance of the radio frequency element while reducing signal losses caused by the silicon substrate.

title: Broadband power divider
assignee: FUHUAKE PREC INDUSTRY SHENZHEN CO LTD
abstract: A broadband power divider comprises a signal layer, a reference ground layer, a connecting line and at least two branch lines, the connecting line is arranged on the signal layer, one end of the connecting line serves as the input end of the broadband power divider, the at least two branch lines are arranged on the signal layer and are symmetrical about the connecting line, one end of each branch line is connected with the other end of the connecting line, the other end of each branch line serves as the output end of the broadband power divider, each branch line is further provided with a first open-circuit branch knot and a defected ground structure, the first open-circuit branch knot is arranged on the signal layer and located on the side, close to the output end, of the branch line, and the defected ground structure is arranged on the reference ground layer and is adjacent to the first open-circuit branch knot. According to the broadband power divider, an adjustable open-circuit bra

title: RECONSTITUTED SUBSTRATE FOR RADIO FREQUENCY APPLICATIONS
abstract: The present invention relates to methods and an apparatus for forming thin form factor reconstituted substrates and semiconductor device packages for radio frequency applications. The substrates and package structures described in the present application can be utilized in high density 2D and 3D integrated devices for 4G, 5G, 6G, and other wireless network systems. In one embodiment, a silicon substrate is structured by laser ablation to include cavities for placement of semiconductor dies and vias for deposition of conductive interconnects. Further, the one or more cavities are structured to be filled or occupied with a flowable dielectric material. Integration of one or more radio frequency components adjacent to the dielectric filled cavities enables improved performance of radio frequency elements while reducing signal losses caused by the silicon substrate.

title: Selecting a modulation table to mitigate 5G message faults
abstract: Wireless networks, such as 5G (and future 6G) networks, can recognize opportunities to enhance message performance by analyzing fault types in real-time, and selecting an appropriate mitigating modulation table accordingly. Noise patterns causing adjacent-amplitude faults and adjacent-phase faults indicate the need for different modulation schemes, while the large non-adjacent faults from pulsed external interference lead to yet a different choice. Disclosed examples show how to select an appropriate mitigating modulation table through fault analysis of failed messages. By selecting and switching to different modulation tables according to the types of message faults experienced by user messages, network operators can reduce message failure rates and increase overall throughput while reducing average delay per message, according to some embodiments.

title: Photonic crystal double-band-pass filter
assignee: SHANGHAI INST MICROSYSTEM & INFORMATION TECH CAS
abstract: The utility model provides a photonic crystal double-band-pass filter which comprises a first metal plate, a second metal plate, metal columns and defect metal columns, the first metal plate and the second metal plate are correspondingly arranged, and the metal columns are arranged between the first metal plate and the second metal plate in a two-dimensional periodic mode. Two opposite ends of the metal columns are respectively contacted with the first metal plate and the second metal plate to form a metal column array; the height of the defective metal columns is smaller than that of the surrounding metal columns, the defective metal columns comprise first defective metal columns and second defective metal columns which have different cross sectional areas so as to form defective sub-periodic structures, and the defective sub-periodic structures are arranged in the metal column array in a two-dimensional periodic mode. title: Broadband external antenna
assignee: JIANGSU WUTONG IOT TECH CO LTD
abstract: The utility model discloses a broadband external antenna, which comprises a substrate, a low-frequency radiation module and a high-frequency radiation module, and the low-frequency radiation module and the high-frequency radiation module are arranged on the same side of the substrate. The low-frequency radiation module comprises a low-frequency radiation branch, a first low-frequency radiation unit and a second low-frequency radiation unit which are connected in sequence in the y-axis negative direction of the plane where the substrate is located, the first low-frequency radiation unit partially surrounds the high-frequency radiation module, and a gap is formed between the high-frequency radiation unit and the first low-frequency radiation unit; a coupling structure is arranged between the first low-frequency radiation unit and the high-frequency radiation module, and the first low-frequency radiation unit, the second low-frequency radiation unit and the high-frequency radiation module

title: FUNCTIONAL ARCHITECTURE AND INTERFACE FOR NON-REAL-TIME RAN INTELLIGENT CONTROLLER
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). A service management and orchestration (SMO) entity enabling a functional split between a non-real-time (RT) radio access network (RAN) intelligent controller (RIC) and an external artificial intelligence (AI)/machine learning (ML) server will, during a data collection phase, utilize the SMO entity and the non-RT RIC to collect and process RAN data and non-RAN data and, during a data transfer phase, transfer processed RAN and non-RAN data from the SMO entity to an external AI/ML server via an interface. During a training model input phase, the SMO entity receives a trained AI/ML model, metadata, and training results from the external AI/ML server via an interface and, during a configuration phase, the SMO entity uses the trained AI/ML model within the SMO entity and the non-RT RIC to transfer config

title: Flexible high-frequency protective film
assignee: GUANGDONG YALONGXING NEW MAT CO LTD
abstract: The utility model relates to a flexible high-frequency protective film which comprises a protective film body, and the protective film body is sequentially provided with an oleophobic layer, a repairing layer, an insulating heat-conducting antimagnetic ink layer, a conductive textile fabric, a heat-conducting conductive pressure-sensitive adhesive layer, an explosion-proof layer, a base material layer and a bonding layer from top to bottom. According to the utility model, the heat-conducting and electric-conducting pressure-sensitive adhesive layer is arranged on the electric-conducting textile cloth, the other surface of the electric-conducting textile cloth is coated with the insulating and heat-conducting antimagnetic ink layer, the other layers are welded through a high-frequency membrane structure welding machine, and finally, the bonding layer is coated on one side of the base material layer, so that the product has excellent product flexibility; compared with the prior art, the

title: Distributed resource allocation method for 6G mobile communication satellite ground network
abstract: The invention provides a distributed resource allocation method for a 6G mobile communication satellite ground network, and belongs to the technical field of mobile communication. The method comprises the following steps: constructing a 6G mobile communication satellite ground network consisting of a plurality of satellites and a plurality of mobile users; dividing all satellites in the 6G mobile communication satellite ground network into prey satellites for obtaining service requirements from mobile users and predator satellites for obtaining service loads from the prey satellites; based on a Lotka-Volterra model, respectively constructing service load dynamic change functions of a prey satellite and a predator satellite to describe load balance problems among different satellites; and solving the constructed prey satellite and predator satellite service load dynamic change function to achieve load balance between the prey satellite and the predator satellite. According to the invent

title: 5G ceramic filter
assignee: IKS PVD TECH SHENYANG CO LTD
abstract: The utility model relates to the technical field of filters, in particular to a 5G ceramic filter which comprises a ceramic base body, a mounting groove penetrating through two opposite sides of the ceramic base body and a plurality of blind holes with different depths are formed in the ceramic base body, and the surface of the ceramic base body, the inner surface of the mounting groove and the inner surfaces of the blind holes are all covered with conductive film layers. The 5G ceramic filter can be particularly applied to 5G/6G construction, specifically, the 5G ceramic filter comprises a ceramic substrate and a metal layer, and has a piezoelectric effect after direct-current high-voltage polarization, so that sequential conversion of an electric signal, mechanical vibration and an electric signal is realized, an LC filter circuit in part of electronic circuits is replaced, the work is more stable, and attention is paid to that the 5G ceramic filter has a wide application prospect.
