A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system according to an embodiment of the present disclosure may include the operations of: determining the number of resource block groups (RBG) based on an uplink partial bandwidth part (BWP); determining the number of bits of a frequency domain resource assignment (FDRA) field based on the determined number of resource block groups; and grouping resource blocks of an uplink subcarrier based on the determined number of bits.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR REDUCING POWER CONSUMPTION OF USER EQUIPMENT HAVING WAKE-UP RECEIVER IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. In addition, the present disclosure discloses a method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system, wherein the method includes the steps of: monitoring a wake up signal (WUS); receiving the WUS from a base station; changing a state of a main radio receiver based on the received WUS; and changing a state of a wake up receiver (WUR) based on the changed state of the main radio receiver.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR UPDATING UE POLICY BASED ON NETWORK SLICING
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an embodiment of the present disclosure, a method for operating a policy control function (PCF) in a mobile communication system comprises the steps of: receiving at least one of network slice access stratum (AS) group (NSAG) mapping information and NSAG priority information; changing or setting a UE route selection policy (URSP) rule set for a terminal based on at least one of the NSAG mapping information and the NSAG priority information; and transmitting the changed or set URSP rule to the terminal.

title: MBS QoE METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MEASURING AND REPORTING QOE OF APPLICATION FOR MBS BROADCAST SERVICE IN MOBILE COMMUNICATINO SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Specifically, the present disclosure proposes a method for measuring and reporting qoe of an application for smooth support of an mbs broadcast service.

title: COMMUNICATION METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR XDD USER EQUIPMENT IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an embodiment of the present disclosure, a method performed by user equipment of a wireless communication system is provided. The method comprises the steps of: receiving, from a base station, first configuration information related to time division duplex (TDD), and second configuration information related to duplex in which some frequency resources corresponding to downlink time resources are used for an uplink; identifying at least one valid physical random access channel (PRACH) transmission occasion on the basis of the first configuration information and the second configuration information; and transmitting, on the basis of the at least one valid PRACH occasion, a random access preamble to the base station.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR SUPPORTING INTER-CELL MOVEMENT BASED ON L1 AND L2 IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5th-generation (5G) or 6th-generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates. A user equipment (UE) according to an embodiment of the present disclosure may receive configuration information about channel measurement for supporting an additional cell, perform, based on the configuration information about the channel measurement, channel measurement on at least one additional cell configured for the UE, receive a medium access control (MAC) control element (CE) indicating beam switching by the UE, based on a report on the channel measurement, and perform transmission or reception of data by using a beam switched based on the received MAC CE.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR BEAM FAILURE RECOVERY OF NETWORK COOPERATIVE COMMUNICATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an embodiment disclosed herein, provided is a method of a terminal in a wireless communication system. The method of the terminal is characterized by comprising the steps of: acquiring information about at least one reference signal for beam failure detection; confirming, for each of a first reference signal set, a second reference signal set, and a third reference signal set included in the at least one reference signal, whether beam failure is detected; and when beam failure is detected for at least one of the first reference signal set, the second reference signal set, or the third reference signal set, transmitting beam failure recovery request information for the reference signal set for which beam failure was detected, wherein the first reference signal set corresponds to a first control resource set (CORESET) pool, the second reference signal set corresponds to

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR SUPPORTING NETWORK SWITCHING OF USER EQUIPMENT IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a secondary node (SN) base station in a wireless communication system according to one embodiment of the present disclosure comprises the steps of: receiving a first message comprising gap configuration auxiliary information from a multiple universal subscriber identity module user equipment (MUSIM UE) having configured dual connectivity with the SN base station and a master node (MN) base station; generating scheduling gap configuration information for controlling scheduling for the MUSIM UE, on the basis of the gap configuration auxiliary information; and transmitting a second message comprising the generated scheduling gap configuration information to the MUSIM UE.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS OF TRANSMISSION AND RECEPTION ON REFERENCE SIGNAL FOR SPATIAL MULTIPLEXING AMONG MULTIPLE USERS IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure relates to an operation of a terminal and a base station in a wireless communication system. Specifically, the present disclosure relates to a method for transmitting and receiving data and a reference signal in a wireless communication system, and an apparatus capable of performing the same.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SATELLITE ACCESS IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a base station in a wireless communication system comprises: a step of identifying that a user equipment (UE) is outside a coverage of a satellite due to a discontinuous coverage (DC); and a step of transmitting a UE context release request message comprising a cause value for UE context release to a network entity managing a mobility of the UE.

title: METHODS AND SYSTEMS FOR MANAGING LAYER TWO TUNNELING PROTOCOL CONNECTION ESTABLISHMENT
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a first network entity associated with a session management in a communication system is provided. The method includes transmitting, to a second network entity associated with a user plane function, a first message comprising information for setting up a layer two tunneling protocol (L2TP) tunnel, and as a response to the first message, receiving, from the second network entity, a second message comprising information on a cause of a L2TP tunnel establishment failure.

title: METHODS AND SYSTEM FOR DETERMINING LAST PLMN FOR MINT
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method of a UE attempting to select an allowable PLMN includes registering for disaster roaming services with the VPLMN 502A; and making a first attempt to obtain a service on an allowable public land mobile network (PLMN) after a duration of a second timer and at most a duration of a first timer.

title: METHODS AND SYSTEMS FOR SAVING NETWORK ENERGY IN SPATIAL DOMAIN USING ADAPTATION INFORMATION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein provide a method for network energy saving (NES) performed by a base station in a wireless communication system. The method includes transmitting, to a terminal, an indication of channel state information (CSI) reference signal (CSI RS) ports, and receiving, from a terminal, a measurement report message comprising information on CSI report based on the indication, wherein the indication comprises at least one subset of CSI RS ports for energy saving.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS OF CELL SELECTION AND CELL RESELECTION FOR SUPPORTING EMERGENCY SERVICES IN WIRELESS NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure provides methods for supporting emergency service in wireless network (1000) by UE (100). In an embodiment, when a current cell on which the UE (100) is camped, does not support the emergency service, the method includes executing for the current cell or a frequency of the current cell at least one of: barring the current cell or the frequency of the current cell and starting one of a cell barring timer for the barred current cell or a frequency barring timer for the barred frequency of the current cell. Further, the method includes deprioritizing the current cell or the frequency of the current cell with a lowest priority for performing cell selection or cell reselection. Further, the method includes allowing the UE to perform the cell selection or cell reselection on cells other than the current cell or the frequency of the current cell.

title: METHODS AND APPARATUS IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein disclose methods for managing energy usage in a wireless network (1000) by a network apparatus (200). The method includes sending a configuration message including at least one CSI-RS port muting information to a UE (100) in a wireless network (1000) for indicating to the UE (100) about a muted CSI-RS port to control a UE measurement of CSI using a CSI-RS signal. Further, the method includes controlling the energy usage in the wireless network (1000) based on the configuration message comprising the CSI-RS port muting information and a subsequent UE measurement report.

title: METHODS AND SYSTEMS FOR HANDLING FPLMNS FOR MINT
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein disclose methods and systems for handling FPLMNs for MINT. Embodiments herein disclose methods and systems for managing selection of PLMNs in wireless communication networks, during a disaster situation. Embodiments herein disclose methods and systems for managing selection of PLMNs in wireless communication networks, during a disaster situation, wherein the UE transitions from Disaster Roaming Service to Normal Service on the same PLMN-A, when PLMN-A is included in the list of preferred PLMN/access technology combinations provided in the Steering of Roaming (SOR) information to the UE. Embodiments herein disclose methods and systems for managing selection of PLMNs in wireless communication networks, during a disaster situation, wherein the UE can avail disaster roaming services from PLMN-A for other PLMN(s) with disaster condition (for e.g., PLMN-D2) when the UE

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MANAGING MOBILE BASE STATION RELAY OPERATION IN WIRELESS NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method for managing a mobile base station relay (MBSR) operation a user equipment (UE) by a network entity in a wireless network is provided. The method comprises receiving a registration request from the UE for operating as an MBSR; determining whether the UE is allowed to operate as the MBSR based on receiving the registration request from the UE; sending a registration accept message to the UE, based on determining that the UE is not allowed to operate as the MBSR, and the UE being allowed to register in a public land mobile network (PLMN); and sending a registration reject message to the UE, based on determining that the UE is not allowed to operate as the MBSR and the UE being not allowed to register in the PLMN.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ALLOCATING SOUNDING REFERENCE SIGNAL RESOURCE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method is provided for handling SRS resource allocation in multi-TRP in wireless networks by a BS or a TRP unit. The method includes configuring CS allocation to at least one port of a UE. The CS allocation uses values from a sub-set of a plurality of allowable CS values. Further, the method includes receiving a SRS using the allocated CS. In an embodiment, per-port CS allocation provides flexibility of using the CS resources efficiently to minimize inter-TRP interference. The method can be used to allocate CS and SRS resources efficiently.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR HANDLING SLICE BASED CELL RESELECTION PRIORITIES
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein provide a method for handling slice based cell reselection priorities by a UE (100). The method includes receiving a RRC message including a slice information for a NSAG at a frequency and without any priority information. Further, the method includes receiving a RRC message including a slice information for a NSAG including priority information for a NSAG at another frequency. Further, the method includes determining whether the RRC message includes the slice information with the priority information or the slice information without the priority information. Further, the method includes determining a lowest priority value for the frequency when the RRC message includes the slice information without the priority information. The lowest priority value is lower than any of the priority information configured for the frequency. Further, the method includes performin

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR HANDLING OF WAKE UP SIGNALING AND CSI REPORTING IN WIRELESS NETWORKS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein provide a method and system for wake-up signaling mechanism and Channel State Information (CSI) reporting operation for Extended Reality (XR) in wireless networks. The method includes configuring, by a network apparatus, an unicast DCP (DCI for power saving) and a XR wakeup signal at a user equipment (UE); determining, by the network apparatus, the unicast DCP and the XR wakeup signal are configured at the UE; transmitting, by the network apparatus, one or more offset values to apply to the UE for starting the DRX On duration Timer for XR services; and adjusting, by the UE, offset value to delay or to start in advance of a DRX On Duration Timer at the UE for XR services in the wireless networks.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR NETWORK PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure provides a method and system for network performance management. A method performed by a mobile network operator (MNO) in a wireless communication system for network performance measurement, the method includes receiving, from a distribution system operator (DSO), a request for measuring performance metrics of a cell of a network, generating an information object class (IOC) response for the request, and transmitting, to the DSO, the (IOC) response.

title: A METHOD AND USER EQUIPMENT FOR DETERMINING FALSE NETWORK NODE IN WIRELESS NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an embodiment of the disclosure, the method may include determining that the UE (100) is configured or capable for security protection of at least one system information, receiving first system information from a cell of at least one network node, determining at least one scheduling parameter of second system information for the cell of at least one network node from the first system information, receiving the second system information including the security information of the at least one network node based on the at least one scheduling parameter and determining that the at least one network node is false when validation of the at least one of the first system information and the second system information of the cell including the security information is failed.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS OF SECURE MULTI-PATH TRANSMISSION FOR PROXIMITY SERVICES IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments disclosed herein provide a method for secure Multi-path transmission for Proximity Services (ProSe) in a telecommunication network (900) by a UE (102). The method includes determining whether a ProSe multipath security policy received from at least one relay device (102b) is same as ProSe multipath security policy receiving for a second PDU session. In an embodiment, the method performing the ProSe multipath communication with the at least one relay device (102b) when the ProSe multipath security policy received from the at least one relay device is same as the ProSe multipath security policy receiving for the second PDU session. In another embodiment, the method includes rejecting the ProSe multipath communication with the at least one relay device when the ProSe multipath security policy received from the at least one relay device is not same as ProSe multipath securi

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR PRECODER INDICATION FOR SUPPORTING MULTIPLE PANELS IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5G) or sixth generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method comprises receiving, from a base station, downlink control information (DCI) including a sounding reference signal resource set indicator (SRSI), two sounding reference signal resource indicator (SRI) fields, and two transmission precoding matrix indicator (TPMI) fields and transmitting, to the base station, a physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) based on the DCI.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR PROVIDING CONTENT ACCORDING TO DEVICE TYPE OF AUGMENTED REALITY TERMINAL
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. An operation method of an application function (AF) in a wireless communication system may comprise a step of receiving information on an augmented reality capability (AR capability) of a terminal from the terminal. The operation method of the AF may comprise a step of identifying an AR capability regarding the color of the terminal on the basis of the information on the AR capability, and transmitting information on the AR capability regarding the color of the terminal to an application server (AS). The operation method of the AF may comprise a step of receiving, from the AS, a media player entry (MPE) to which color remapping processed by the AS is applied.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR NON-TERRESTRIAL NETWORK MOBILITY IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Disclosed herein is a method of a user equipment (UE) for non-terrestrial network (NTN)the method comprising: receiving, from a network, assistance information related to the non-terrestrial network (NTN) for cell reselection; and performing a measurement on a neighbouring cell for the cell reselection based on the assistance information, wherein the assistance information includes ephemeris information on the neighbouring cell. In the embodiments, the assistance information is included in a system information block (SIB) broadcast by at least one of a serving cell or the neighbouring cell.

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR PROVIDING AR CONTENT SERVICE BASED ON TERMINAL CAPABILITY IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate, and an operating method of an application function (AF) entity according to the present disclosure comprises the steps of: transmitting a report request message to a terminal; receiving a report message including capability values of the terminal in response to the report request message; compiling service access information on the basis of the capability values of the terminal; and transmitting, to an application provider, a message including a provisioned parameter and address, on the basis of the compiled service access information.

title: METHODS FOR HANDLING NAS MESSAGES FOR UES USING SATELLITE ACCESS FROM A NON-ALLOWED LOCATION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system, the method comprising: receiving, from an access and mobility management function (AMF), a first message comprising information on rejecting a service; and determining not transmitting, to the AMF, a second message any of a predetermined data type, wherein the information includes a cause associated with a location of the terminal.

title: SIDELINK-BASED POSITIONING METHOD AND APPARATUS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate.The present disclosure discloses a method and a device in a wireless communication system. This document provides a method performed by a first user terminal UE in a wireless communication system, comprising: acquiring by the first UE a configuration related to a Sidelink Positioning Reference Signal (SL PRS); and transmitting by the first UE the SL PRS and Sidelink Control Information (SCI) associated with the SL PRS to a second UE based on the configuration related to the SL PRS; wherein a resource used to transmit the SL PRS is determined based on the configuration related to the SL PRS, and/or a resource correspondence between SL PRS resources and PSCCH, and a determined PSCCH resource; and/or wherein a PSCCH resource used to transmit the SCI is determined based on a related configuration of a PSCCH associated with the SL PRS, and/or the resource correspondence between the SL PR

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SERVICE USING TETHERING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an embodiment of the disclosure, a policy control function (PCF) entity in a wireless communication system comprises a transceiver and at least one processor. The at least one processor is configured to update a service-related policy based on a service transmission delay time (delay budget adjustment) per service flow, wherein the service transmission delay time per service flow is transmitted to a tethered device, schedule a quality-of-service (QoS) based on the service-related policy, and transmit, to a session management function (SMF) entity, a first message including the per-service flow service transmission delay time per service flow, the updated service-related policy, and a QoS requirement based on the scheduled QoS.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR PERFORMING CONDITIONAL HANDOVER IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in wireless communication system, the method comprising: receiving, from a source master node (S-MN), a measurement configuration including a first condition for a conditional handover (CHO) and a second condition for a conditional primary secondary cell group cell (PSCell) change (CPC); and in case that the first condition and the second condition are met, performing the CHO to a target master node (T-MN) and the CPC to a target secondary node (T-SN).

title: COMMUNICATION METHOD AND USER EQUIPMENT
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate.Embodiments of the present disclosure provide a communication method and a user equipment. The method comprises steps of: receiving configuration information of a first SSB by a UE; receiving, based on the configuration information, the first SSB for use in synchronization and/or measurement, wherein the first SSB comprises fewer signals than a second SSB. In other words, the first SSB is simpler than the second SSB, so the first SSB is more energy-saving than the second SSB, so that the purpose of saving power on the base station side can be achieved.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVIDING COVERAGE INFORMATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. A method for operating an access and mobility management function (AMF) in a wireless communication system, according to one embodiment, comprises the steps of: transmitting, to a network exposure function (NEF), an authentication request message including a coverage information transmission ID and a coverage information request for transmitting coverage information of a satellite radio access network (RAN); receiving, from a coverage information server, the authentication result and/or the coverage information of a user equipment (UE) corresponding to the coverage information transmission ID; and transmitting, to the UE, the authentication result and/or the coverage information.

title: MBS Method and Apparatus for Broadcast Reception Indication in MBS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. An embodiment of the present invention discloses an operation of a terminal and a base station in a situation in which the terminal can receive an mbs in a non-serving cell.

title: COMMUNICATION METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SERVICE USING TETHERING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an embodiment of the present disclosure, a policy control function (PCF) device in a wireless communication system comprises: a transceiver; and at least one processor. The at least one processor is configured to update a service-related policy based on a service delay budget adjustment for each service floor transmitted to a tethered device, schedule a quality of service (QoS) based on the service-related policy, and transmit a first message including the service delay budget adjustment for each service floor, the updated service-related policy, and a QoS requirement based on the scheduled QoS to a session management function (SMF) device.

title: RIS method and apparatus for acquiring a beam book for RIS control in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate after a 4G communication system such as LTE. According to an embodiment of the present disclosure, provided is a method for generating a beam book for controlling a beam when the beam transmitted from a base station is reflected by RIS and transmitted to a UE in a wireless communication system. The method comprises the steps of: receiving information on an RIS ID from an RIS control unit (RCU) which is a communication entity for controlling the RIS for reflecting a beam transmitted from a base station and transmitting the beam to a user equipment (UE); identifying an incident angle from the base station corresponding to the RIS ID to the RIS based on the information on the RIS ID; and determining information on the beam used for scanning a preset area based on the identified incident angle. A beam book for RIS control in the RCU can be obtained based on the identified incide

title: SoRsteering of roaming METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR HOSTING NETWORK INFORMATION VIA STEERING OF ROAMING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to one embodiment of the present disclosure, a method of a network entity included in a home network in a wireless communication system comprises: a step of receiving terminal information related to a first steering of roaming (SoR) from a serving network wherein a terminal is connected; a step of determining whether to steer the terminal to a hosting network providing a local service based on the terminal information related to the first SoR; and a step of transmitting first SoR information to the terminal through the serving network when determined to steer the terminal to a hosting network providing local service, wherein the first SoR information comprises an instruction for the terminal to switch to a local service access mode.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS TO COLLECT DATA FOR NETWORK DATA ANALYSIS IN RADIO COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a network entity in a wireless communication system includes the steps of: requesting network data collection and transmission; analyzing and transmitting the collected data; and searching for a network function supporting management plane data collection.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVIDING COVERAGE INFORMATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an embodiment of the present invention, an operating method of an amf in a wireless communication system comprises the steps of: receiving a registration request including coverage information from a terminal; and performing a registration procedure based on the received coverage information.

title: Method and apparatus to support edge computing session management in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure discloses a method performed by a session management function (SMF). The method includes the steps of: receiving a protocol data unit (PDU) session creation request message including a home rooted (HR) local session creation permission indicator from an access and mobility management function (AMF); requesting subscriber information related to HR roaming to a unified data management (UDM) based on the received PDU session creation request message; selecting a user plane function (UPF) that supports data network local access in a visited network; and setting the application of an aggregated maximum bit rate (AMBR) for the HR local session to the UPF.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR REGISTRATION MANAGEMENT AND CONNECTION MANAGEMENT IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to the present disclosure, a method of a source base station comprises the steps of: receiving measurement report information for at least one neighbor cell from a terminal; determining whether to perform handover to a target base station based on at least one of the measurement report information and coverage information of the source base station; and transmitting a request for the handover to a source network entity.

title: AR APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR PROVISIONING DEVICE CAPAVILITY-BASED AR CONTENT SERVICE IN WIRELESS SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. An operation method of an application function (AF) entity according to the present disclosure comprises: a step of transmitting a report request message to a terminal; a step of receiving a report message comprising capability values of the terminal in response to the report request message; a step of compiling service access information based on the capability values of the terminal; and a step of transmitting a message comprising a provisioned parameter and an address to the application provider based on the compiled service access information.

title: AR METHOD FOR PROVIDING CONTENTS ACCORDING TO DEVICE TYPE OF AR DEVICE AND APPARATUS THEREOF
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The method for operating an application function (AF) in a wireless communication system comprises a step of receiving information on augmented reality (AR) capability of a terminal from the terminal. The method for operating the AF may include checking an AR capability related to a color of the terminal based on the information related to the AR capability and transmitting AR capability information related to the color of the terminal to an application server (AS). The method may further include receiving a media player entry (MPE) to which color remapping processed by the AS is applied from the AS.

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR PERFORMING CONDITIONALLY HAND OVER IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure relates to operation of a terminal and a base station in a wireless communication system, and more particularly, to a method and apparatus for applying a specific secondary cell group configuration when performing handover on a primary cell. A method for operating a terminal comprises the steps of: receiving a signal including information on a first condition and information on a second condition from a source master node and conditional handover setting information corresponding to each condition; determining whether at least one of the first condition and the second condition is satisfied based on the received signal; applying CHO setting information corresponding to the satisfied condition of the first condition and the second condition based on the determination result; and performing random access to a target master node based on the applied CHO

title: RRC MBS SPS METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MBS SPS RECEPTION IN RRC INACTIVE MODE
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. More specifically, the present disclosure relates to a method and apparatus for receiving an mbs sps in an rrc inactive state.

title: AI Method and apparatus of AI model descriptions for media services
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an embodiment of the present disclosure, a method for transmitting AI model data through IMS is provided. The method includes: transmitting an SDP offer message including a first attribute indicating at least one AI model to UE; receiving an SDP answer message including the first attribute from the UE; and transmitting AI model data to the UE based on the first attribute, wherein the first attribute includes at least one parameter set corresponding to the at least one AI model, and the parameter set includes a first parameter indicating whether the at least one AI model is a partial AI model.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR WAKE-UP RECEIVING IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to various embodiments of the present disclosure, in a wireless communication system, a method performed by a terminal may comprise: a step of receiving information for wake up from a base station by a wake up receiver (WUR) of the terminal; a step of triggering activation or deactivation of a main radio of the terminal based on the information for the wake up; and a step of receiving a downlink signal from the base station by the main radio of the terminal when the activation of the main radio of the terminal is triggered.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVISIONING PROFILE IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method for provisioning a large number of the present disclosure to terminals may comprise: a step of receiving eUICC data comprising key information of eUICC for profile installation from a terminal manufacturer server; a step of transmitting a factory injection profile request message comprising eUICC data to a profile server; a step of receiving a profile request response message comprising a bound profile package (BPP) and a profile installation key from the profile server; and a step of transmitting the received BPP and profile installation key to a terminal.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVISIONING PROFILE IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method for provisioning a large amount of profiles to terminals of the present disclosure may include the steps of: receiving eUICC data including key information of eUICC for profile installation from a terminal manufacturer server; transmitting a profile request message for factory injection including the eUICC data to a profile server; receiving a profile request response message including a bound profile package (BPP) and a profile installation key from the profile server; and transmitting the received BPP and profile installation key to the terminal.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVISIONING PROFILE IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method for provisioning a large amount of profiles to terminals of the present disclosure may include the steps of: receiving eUICC data including key information of eUICC for profile installation from a terminal manufacturer server; transmitting a profile request message for factory injection including the eUICC data to a profile server; receiving a profile request response message including a bound profile package (BPP) and a profile installation key from the profile server; and transmitting the received BPP and profile installation key to the terminal.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS OF TRANSMISSION AND RECEPTION FOR UPLINK REFERENCE SIGNAL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present invention relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present invention relates to operations of a terminal and a base station in a wireless communication system and, more particularly, to a method for transmitting and receiving an uplink reference signal in a wireless communication system and an apparatus capable of performing the same. The present invention provides an apparatus and a method capable of effectively providing a service in a mobile communication system.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR LOCATION BASED TERMINAL MOBILITY MANAGEMENT IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Specifically, the present disclosure provides a method and apparatus for managing mobility of a terminal based on a detailed location.

title: 6G Tbps CIRCULAR ANTENNA ARRAY DESIGN FOR FUTURE 6G TBPS WIRELESS COMMUNICATIONS
abstract: The setting of the line-of-sight multiple-input multiple-output circular antenna array includes setting an integer number of antenna arrays up to the number of antenna ports for the circular antenna array and a maximum radius for any antenna ring in the circular antenna array. An integer number of antenna rings up to a predetermined maximum number of antenna rings, a number of antenna arrays for each of the number of antenna rings, and a capacity for each of a plurality of different combinations of angular offsets for the corresponding antenna arrays in each of the antenna rings are determined. The determined capacities for a plurality of different combinations are compared.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVISIONING PROFILE IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure relates to a method for provisioning a large amount of profiles to terminals. disclosed is a method for obtaining key information for profile installation, transmitting a profile request for factory injection to a profile server, receiving bound profile package (bpp) and profile installation key information from the profile server, and transmitting the received bpp and profile installation key information to a terminal.

title: Network Slice METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVIDING NETWORK SLICE BASED ON POLICY OF SERVICE PROVIDER IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method of the present disclosure includes the steps of: receiving a network slice policy management request message from a val server; and performing a policy management operation on the network slice based on the policy information included in the policy management request message, wherein the policy management request message may include policy information on the network slice.

title: POWER AMPLIFIER AND ELECTRONIC DEVICE COMPRISING SAME
abstract: The present invention relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A power amplifier according to the present invention comprises: a signal processor; a control circuit electrically connected to the signal processor; a distributor electrically connected to the signal processor and the control circuit; a first amplifier electrically connected to the distributor; a second amplifier electrically connected to the control circuit and the distributor; and a coupler electrically connected to the control circuit, the first amplifier, and the second amplifier.

title: Method and apparatus for supporting power headroom reporting of multiple transmission reception points in next generation mobile communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure is a method performed by a terminal, the method comprising: receiving a radio resource control (RRC) message from a base station, the RRC message comprising information associated with power headroom reports (PHRs) of a plurality of transmission and reception points (TRPs); under the condition that the PHR is triggered, obtaining a first power headroom (PH) of the first TRP and a second PH of the second TRP; generating a medium access control (MAC) control element (CE) of the plurality of TRPs, the MAC CE including the first PH of the first TRP and the second PH of the second TRP; and sending the MAC CE of the plurality of TRPs to the base station.

title: 6G-band dense fog environment detection and communication system
assignee: NO 15 INSTITUTE OF CHINA ELECTRONICS TECH GROUP CORPORATION
abstract: The invention discloses a 6G-band dense fog environment detection and communication system, which comprises a signal transmitting module and a detection module, the signal transmitting module is composed of a petal-shaped scattered radiation antenna and a directional radiation antenna. Femtosecond laser is emitted into the beam splitting plate, and the femtosecond laser is split into a plurality of beams and then is emitted into the signal transmitting module; a starting voltage of about 10 V is applied to the petal-shaped scattered radiation antenna, and the petal-shaped scattered radiation antenna starts to radiate terahertz signals; the detection module collects a terahertz signal reflected by an object, generates a corresponding electric signal and applies the electric signal to the directional radiation antenna; and the directional radiation antenna generates a modulated target signal with detection object information, and transmits the target signal out. According to the inventio

title: NR2.6 G large-bandwidth high-power 5G digital remote repeater
assignee: SHANGHAI XZ COM TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses an NR2.6G large-bandwidth high-power 5G digital remote repeater, which comprises a synchronous motor, a sliding block, an arc-shaped plate, a fixed seat, a connecting box, a fixed frame, a first convex block, a limiting column, a cross rod, a second side plate, a baffle plate, a turntable and a clamping groove, and is characterized in that the interior of the connecting box is rotationally connected with a bidirectional screw rod through a bearing; according to the device, heat dissipation processing is performed on the heat dissipation fins through the heat dissipation fan, and the heat dissipation angle is continuously adjusted by using the swing of the baffle plate, so that the heat dissipation processing can be performed on the heat dissipation fins better, the damage rate of the repeater body is reduced, the service life of the device is prolonged, and when the repeater body is installed, the heat dissipation efficiency is improved. When the repeater body i

title: Integrated circuit package
abstract: The utility model provides an integrated circuit packaging structure. The integrated circuit packaging structure comprises a first rewiring structure; an insulating material over the first redistribution structure; a die embedded in the insulating material; a second redistribution structure over the die and the insulating material; and a first via extending through the insulating material, where the first via includes a first inner conductive core and a first outer conductive shielding layer, where the insulating material is disposed between the first inner conductive core and the first outer conductive shielding layer, and where the first outer conductive shielding layer has an annular shape in a top view. Therefore, electromagnetic 5G/6G high-frequency interference on the inner conductive core can be reduced, and the number of shielding wires and through holes of the integrated circuit package can be reduced. In addition, the interconnects between the package elements may have a shor

title: Communication method and device
abstract: The invention provides a communication method and device, which can realize the access of terminal equipment to the Internet of Things, and can be applied to a 4G system, a 5G system and a future communication system, such as a 6G system. The method comprises the following steps: an access or mobility management network element receives a first request message, the access or mobility management network element sends a second request message to a data management network element, and the access or mobility management network element receives a first response message from the data management network element, and the access or mobility management network element establishes an association relationship between the first terminal device and the second terminal device. Wherein the first request message comprises an identifier of the first terminal equipment and an identifier of the Internet of Things, the second request message comprises the identifier of the first terminal equipment and the

title: Channel estimation compensation with constellation
abstract: The embodiment of the invention relates to equipment, a method, a device and a computer readable storage medium for relaxation compensation of improved system performance. The method comprises: determining a first set of estimated channel coefficients, the first set of estimated channel coefficients being associated with data transmission via a channel between a transmitting device and a receiving device; determining a target compensation for the channel estimation based on a reference constellation generated from the set of reference channel coefficients; and generating a second set of estimated channel coefficients by compensating the first set of estimated channel coefficients based on the target compensation. Value-added services can be derived based on human-like expert data analysis by using the proposed solution. Since data analysis does not require additional preambles or expenditures, the proposed solution is overhead-friendly. In addition, the solution can be compatible with

title: 6G-oriented high-dynamic wireless environment multi-source data sensing method
abstract: The invention provides a 6G-oriented high-dynamic wireless environment multi-source data sensing method, which belongs to the technical field of wireless communication and comprises the following steps of: acquiring low-frequency and millimeter-wave wireless channel data in a wireless environment by using a multi-band radio frequency transceiver to obtain parameter-level sensing data; point cloud data and RGB image data in a wireless environment are collected by using a laser radar scanning function and a panoramic vision record, and environment-level sensing data are obtained; constructing a moving state sensing subsystem based on a GNSS antenna, and obtaining real-time dynamic track sensing data; power supply and measurement configuration are carried out on a high-dynamic wireless environment sensing system, and a multi-source sensing data set in a high-dynamic wireless environment is obtained. According to the invention, multi-modal data sensing can be carried out on a wireless tran

title: Distributed space-time block coding transmission system and transmission performance analysis method
abstract: The invention provides a distributed space-time block coding transmission system and a transmission performance analysis method, and the system comprises a source node which is used for transmitting a transmission signal; the plurality of distributed intelligent reflecting surfaces IRS are used for receiving the transmission signals sent from the source node, adjusting the phases of the transmission signals to construct space-time block codes, and reflecting the signals subjected to the space-time block codes to the destination node in a distributed manner; and the destination node is used for receiving the signals after space-time block coding and decoding the signals after space-time block coding to obtain transmission signals. According to the invention, a plurality of distributed IRSs are deployed between the source node and the destination node, the signals sent by the source node are received at the same time, the phase is adjusted to construct space-time block coding, and the si

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SATELLITE ACCESS IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a base station in a wireless communication system includes identifying that a user equipment (UE) is out of a coverage of a satellite due to a discontinuous coverage (DC), and transmitting, to a network entity managing a mobility of the UE, a UE context release request message including a cause value for a UE context release.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR USING LINE-OF-SIGHT CHANNEL VIA ESTIMATION OF MULTI-BAND CHANNEL
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5<sup>th</sup> generation (5G) or 6<sup>th</sup> generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates after a 4<sup>th</sup> generation (4G) communication system such as long term evolution (LTE).According to an embodiment of the present disclosure, a method of identifying, by a base station (BS), a line-of-sight (LOS) channel in a wireless communication system may include: receiving, from a user equipment (UE), a signal for measuring a channel in each of multiple bands; determining, based on the signal, whether the channel is an LOS channel in each of the multiple bands; in response to a determination that the LOS channel exists in each of the multiple bands, identifying the channel as the LOS channel; and transmitting data to the UE, based on the LOS channel.

title: METHODS AND SYSTEMS FOR HANDLING GAPS IN WIRELESS NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure provides methods for handling a measurement gap in a wireless network (1000) by a network apparatus (200). The method includes receiving a message from a UE (100). The message includes at least one of a UE capability information indicating a capability of supporting a preconfigured gap and a concurrent gap together, a capability of supporting NCSG and a concurrent gap together, a capability of a supporting NCSG and a non-NCSG measurement gap together, whether the UE (100) is capable of supporting a preconfigured NCSG, and whether the UE (100) is capable of supporting the concurrent gap, the preconfigured gap, and the preconfigured NCSG together. Based on the received message, the method includes performing an action.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS OF MULTI-BEAM OPERATION FOR MULTI-CELL SCHEDULING
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Methods and apparatuses for multi-beam operation for multi-cell scheduling. A method includes receiving first information for a set of cells, second information for a set of transmission configuration indication (TCI) states associated with each cell in the set of cells, respectively, and a first physical downlink control channel (PDCCH) that provides a first DCI format. The first DCI format schedules receptions of first physical downlink shared channels (PDSCHs) on respective first cells from the set of cells. The first DCI format includes a first TCI field indicating a combination of TCI states that includes a number of TCI states that is equal to a number of cells in the set of cells. The method further includes determining first TCI states from the combination of TCI states and receiving the first PDSCHs, or second PDSCHs after the first PDSCHs, on the first cells based on the

title: METHOD OF COMMUNICATION DEVICE AND COMMUNICATION DEVICE
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a communication system is provided. The method includes monitoring a wake-up signal and determining whether to wake up to receive paging-related information or establish radio resource control (RRC) connection or receive a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH) based on the result of monitoring the wake-up signal.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ERROR CORRECTION IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Disclosed is a method of managing an error situation in a telecommunication network where there exists a plurality of Quality of Service, QoS, rules having a common QoS Flow Indicator, QFI, comprising the steps of: a User Equipment, UE, connected to the network, determining if a resulting QoS rule for a Guaranteed Bit Rate, GBR, QoS flow, which has no QoS flow description, is a default QoS rule and, if so, then the UE shall initiate a Protocol Data Unit, PDU, session release procedure.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CSI REPORT FOR SPATIAL DOMAIN NETWORK ADAPTATION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method comprises receiving, from a base station, first information related to reception of channel state information reference signals (CSI-RSs) on a cell, second information related to determining a first number of CSI reports associated with a first number of operation states on the cell, and third information related to triggering a second number CSI reports associated with a second number of operation states from the first number of operation states, receiving, from the base station, the CSI-RSs based on the first information, determining, based on the second information and the CSI-RSs, the second number of CSI reports; and transmitting, to the base station, the second number of CSI reports. An operation state of the first number of operation states is associated with a configuratio

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR LOW POWER WAKE UP TECHNOLOGY
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The disclosure provides a method performed by a user equipment (UE), including monitoring a wake up signal including at least one data signal, wherein the data signal carries wake up information for at least one UE or at least one UE group, or common wake up information; performing a corresponding behavior based on the wake up information carried by the at least one data signal.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SIDELINK INITIAL BEAM ACQUISITION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Method and apparatus for sidelink (SL) initial beam acquisition in a wireless communication system. A method for operating a user equipment (UE) includes transmitting, to a second UE, first physical sidelink control channels (PSCCHs) and corresponding first physical sidelink shared channels (PSSCHs) using multiple spatial domain transmission filters, respectively. The first PSCCHs and the corresponding first PSSCHs includes a first link establishment message. The method further includes receiving, from the second UE, assistance information associated with a spatial domain transmission filter. The method further includes determining, based on the assistance information, the spatial domain transmission filter. The method further includes transmitting, based on the spatial domain transmission filter, a second PSCCH and a corresponding second PSSCH to the second UE.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DISCARDING PDCP SDU IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. The present disclosure relates to an operating method of a terminal in a wireless communication system, comprising the steps of: receiving a terminal capability request message from a base station; in response to the terminal capability request message, transmitting, to the base station, terminal capability information including information related to discarding of a PDU set; and when the terminal supports the discarding of the PDU set, receiving configuration information regarding the PDU set from the base station.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVIDING MEDIA-BASED QOS FOR REAL-TIME COMMUNICATION SERVICE IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5th generation (5G) or 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. A method of operating a real-time communication application function (RTC AF) entity in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving a service provisioning parameter (parameter for service provisioning) from an RTC service provider, generating a media session quality of service (QoS) parameter (parameter for AF session with QoS), based on the service provisioning parameter, transmitting the media session QoS parameter to a policy control function (PCF), generating a stream endpoint (SEP) configuration parameter (parameter for SEP configuration), based on the service provisioning parameter, and transmitting the stream endpoint configuration parameter to a stream endpoint entity.

title: DEVICE AND METHOD FOR REMOVING E2 INTERFACE-RELATED INFORMATION IN RADIO ACCESS NETWORK
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. According to embodiments disclosed herein, provided is a method performed by a near-real time (RT) radio access network (RAN) intelligent controller (RIC). The method may comprise the steps of: receiving an E2 removal request message, for indicating a shutdown of the second node, from an E2 node via an E2 interface, wherein the shutdown of the E2 node is configured from service management and orchestration (SMO); transmitting an E2 removal response message to the E2 node via the E2 interface; and removing the configuration of the E2 node from the Near-RT RIC in response to the E2 removal request message.

title: INTRA-OPERATOR TERRESTRIAL SPECTRUM SHARING IN COMMUNICATION NETWORKS
abstract: Systems, methods, and computer-readable media herein provide for adjustment of a spectrum sharing allocation between devices operating on 5G and/or 6G technologies. An inter-operator scheduler receives common signals associated with 5G and/or 6G devices and determines time and/or frequency divisions and configurations to a shared technology spectrum to allocate spectrum resources. In response to determining an allocation of the shared spectrum, the shared spectrum can be efficiently shared among 5G and 6G devices in a dynamic and flexible manner.

title: METHOD OF COMMUNICATION DEVICE AND COMMUNICATION DEVICE
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a communication system is provided. The method includes monitoring a wake-up signal and determining whether to wake up to receive paging-related information or establish radio resource control (RRC) connection or receive a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH) based on the result of monitoring the wake-up signal.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVIDING MEDIA-BASED QOS FOR REAL-TIME COMMUNICATION SERVICE IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5th generation (5G) or 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. A method of operating a real-time communication application function (RTC AF) entity in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving a service provisioning parameter (parameter for service provisioning) from an RTC service provider, generating a media session quality of service (QoS) parameter (parameter for AF session with QoS), based on the service provisioning parameter, transmitting the media session QoS parameter to a policy control function (PCF), generating a stream endpoint (SEP) configuration parameter (parameter for SEP configuration), based on the service provisioning parameter, and transmitting the stream endpoint configuration parameter to a stream endpoint entity.

title: AI Model with Error-Detection Code for Fault Correction in 5G/6G
abstract: Message faulting is a critical unsolved problem for 5G and 6G. Disclosed herein is a method for combining an AI-based analysis of the waveform data of each message element, plus the constraint of an associated error-detection code (such as a CRC or parity construct of the correct message) to localize and, in many cases, correct a limited number of faults per message, without a retransmission. For example, the waveform data may include a deviation of the amplitude or phase of a particular message element, relative to an average of the amplitudes or phases of the other message elements that have the same demodulation value. The outliers are thereby exposed as the most likely faulted message elements. In addition, using the error-detection code, the AI model can determine the most likely corrected message, thereby avoiding retransmission delays and power usage and other costs.

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR TRANSMITTING AND RECEIVING SIGNAL ACCORDING TO CHANNEL STATE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as LTE. The disclosure provides a method performed by a UE. The method includes receiving mapping information between a combination of a reflection pattern and a transmission beam of a BS and an RS from the BS; receiving scheduling information including information on a channel state corresponding to the combination of the reflection pattern and the transmission beam of the BS; identifying a reception beam based on at least one RS received from the BS, the mapping information, and the scheduling information; and receiving a downlink signal from the BS through the reception beam. The reflection pattern is related to an RIS.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CONTROLLING AND CONFIGURING AMPLIFYING GAIN OF NETWORK-CONTROLLED REPEATER IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5G) or sixth generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. The disclosure provides an operation and an apparatus for controlling an amplifying gain of a network-controlled repeater (NCR). A method of the NCR repeater includes receiving, from a base station (BS), configuration information on an amplifying gain for the NCR, identifying the amplifying gain based on the configuration information, amplify signals received from a terminal and the BS based on the identified amplifying gain, and transmitting the amplified signals.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PRECODING-BASED UPLINK TRANSMISSION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. In the present disclosure, an apparatus and method for multi-level UL precoding are provided. The method comprises: receiving configuration information about resource allocation for UL transmission, the configuration information indicating: resources allocated for UL transmission and UL precoding information for UL transmission on the allocated resources, the UL precoding information indicating: updating a precoding matrix V and updating a base vector and a coefficient of the precoding matrix V; determining a UL precoding matrix for UL transmission based on the updated precoding matrix V and the previous UL precoding matrix; and performing the UL transmission on the allocated resources using the UL precoding matrix.

title: Method and apparatus for channel occupancy sharing of UE with sidelink
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. An apparatus and method for channel occupancy sharing with a sidelink for a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system. A method of a UE includes performing a channel access procedure to initiate channel occupancy on a channel by shared spectrum channel access; and determining that the channel occupancy is to be shared with the at least one other UE. The method further includes transmitting the first sidelink transmission within the channel occupancy; and receiving a second sidelink transmission from the at least one other UE within the channel occupancy after transmitting the first sidelink transmission within the channel occupancy.

title: Method and apparatus for frequency resource allocation in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving a radio resource control (RRC) message including configuration information related to a physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) repeated transmission from a base station (BS), identifying a frequency resource for the PUSCH repeated transmission based on the configuration information and information on at least one scheduling resource of the UE, and transmitting data to the BS via the PUSCH based on the frequency resource.

title: PDCP SDU METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PDCP SDU DISCARDING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure relates to a terminal and base station operation in a wireless communication system and specifically, relates to a method and device for performing PDCP SDU discarding operation, in units of PDU sets, for efficient use of network wireless resources, when configuring a set of protocol data units (PDUs) corresponding to an application data unit (ADU).

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS ON MEDIA-AWARE QOS PROVISIONING FOR REAL-TIME COMMUNICATION SERVICE IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to the present disclosure, a method of operating a real-time communication application function (RTC AF) entity in a wireless communication system comprises the steps of: receiving a parameter for service provisioning from an RTC service provider; generating a parameter for AF session with QoS based on the parameter for service provisioning; transmitting the parameter for AF session with QoS to a PCF; generating a stream endpoint (SEP) parameter for SEP configuration based on the parameter for service provisioning; and transmitting the stream endpoint parameter for SEP configuration to a stream endpoint entity.

title: Intelligent network connection equipment with antenna strengthening function
assignee: WUHAN ZHIJIAN ELECTRONIC INSTR CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses intelligent network connection equipment with an antenna strengthening function, which comprises a receiving and transmitting end, the receiving and transmitting end takes a GP antenna as a main body, and the GP antenna obeys to be expanded by taking NR as a basic framework; the allocation end is used for carrying out encoding and decoding calculation on the antenna signal generated by the receiving and transmitting end on the basis of an algorithm architecture; the invention relates to the technical field of intelligent network connection equipment. According to the invention, the GP antenna takes the network connection terminal as a base point and transmits the annular frequency wave to the space, and the annular frequency wave recodes SDL and SUL protocols beyond a conventional Sub 6G frequency band and a mm wave frequency band through the NR infrastructure before transmission, so that the channel width of the SDL and SUL protocols is enhanced; and the signal

title: Method and system for coordinating unavailable period parameters for UEs in wireless network
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method for coordinating an unavailable period parameter of a user equipment (UE) in a wireless network is provided. The method includes determining, by an application function (AF) of a network device in the wireless network, an unavailable period parameter of the UE based on at least one event to be performed at the UE. Further, the method includes transmitting, by the AF of the network device, an unavailable period parameter of the UE to an access and mobility management function (AMF) of the network device. Further, the method includes configuring an unavailable period parameter at the UE. In an embodiment, the UE detects that it must perform an event, the UE initiates a non-access stratum (NAS) procedure to indicate that it will become unavailable by indicating an unavailable period parameter to a fifth generation core (5GC), the 5GC forwards the unavailable period pa

title: Vehicle-mounted antenna unit, vehicle-mounted antenna system and vehicle
abstract: The invention relates to a vehicle-mounted antenna unit, a vehicle-mounted antenna system and a vehicle. The vehicle-mounted antenna unit comprises a first antenna module, a second antenna module, a third antenna module and a fourth antenna module, wherein the first antenna module comprises a 2G multi-band antenna, a 3G multi-band antenna and a 4G multi-band antenna; the second antenna module comprises a 5G multi-band antenna, a WIFI multi-band antenna and a Bluetooth multi-band antenna; the third antenna module comprises a multi-band positioning antenna; the fourth antenna module comprises a 6G antenna array; the first antenna module, the second antenna module, the third antenna module and the fourth antenna module are respectively arranged in different areas of the vehicle-mounted antenna unit. According to the vehicle-mounted antenna unit provided by the invention, on the premise of ensuring that each antenna has good isolation and does not influence each other, the antenna modules

title: Wireless communication method and system applied to multi-cell far and near fields, and storage medium
abstract: The invention discloses a wireless communication method and system applied to a multi-cell far and near field, and a storage medium. The method comprises the following steps: arranging reconfigurable holographic metasurface antennas in a plurality of base stations; constructing a first channel between the reconfigurable holographic metasurface antenna and a near-field user and a second channel between the reconfigurable holographic metasurface antenna and a far-field user; and performing hybrid beam forming on a signal to be sent, and sending a hybrid beam signal to a user through a corresponding channel. According to the wireless communication method provided by the embodiment of the invention, the reconfigurable holographic metasurface antenna is adopted, compared with a traditional antenna, the reconfigurable holographic metasurface antenna has the advantages of light weight, low power consumption, low cost and the like, and compared with a traditional communication scheme which onl

title: SYNCHRONIZATION METHOD AND DEVICE IN NON-TERRESTRIAL NETWORK
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 6G communication system for accomplishing ultra-low latency and a data transmission rate higher than that of 4G and 5G communication systems. According to various embodiments of the present disclosure, even if a satellite forming a specific cell is changed in a non-terrestrial network (NTN), the terminal may change a satellite connection without initiating a procedure for synchronization with the changed satellite connection by performing uplink synchronization and downlink synchronization with the changed satellite on the basis of information for uplink synchronization and information for downlink synchronization that have been obtained in advance. Accordingly, even if a satellite in communication is changed, a user's terminal can experience high communication quality without interruption, and there is no need to perform the synchronization procedure which is performed when the existing satellite is changed, such that the number of communication sig

title: USER PLANE FUNCTION SELECTION SUPPORT METHOD AND DEVICE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a session management function (SMF) entity in a wireless communication system, according to various embodiments of the present disclosure, comprises the steps of: receiving a first message comprising a user plane latency (UPL) requirement from a policy control function (PCF) entity; and determining whether to perform PDU session anchor (PSA) user plane function (UPF) relocation, on the basis of the first message..

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSMITTING/RECEIVING UPLINK DATA REPETITIONS FOR NETWORK COOPERATIVE COMMUNICATIONS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. According to an embodiment of the present disclosure, provided is a method performed in a communication system by a terminal. The method is characterized by comparing the steps of: receiving an RRC message comprising configuration information for a first SRS resource set and a second SRS resource set; receiving, from a base station, a DCI for indicating at least one of a first SRI or a second SRI on a PDCCH; and performing repetition of PUSCH transmission on the basis of at least one of the SRI indicated by the DCI, the first SRS resource set, or the second SRS resource set, wherein the first SRI is associated with the most recent transmission before the PDCCH of the SRS resource identified by the first SRI in the first SRS resource set, and the second SRI is associated with the most recent transmission before the PDCCH of the SRS resource identified by the second SRI in the

title: MOBILE POWER SUPPLY DEVICE
abstract: To simplify, downsize, and make lighter a configuration of a mobile power supply, shorten the installation work, reduce costs, and expand a degree of freedom of movement, allowing installation in small spaces without taking up much space.SOLUTION: A power supply device 1 has a pair of solar panels 2 and 3, a rotation shaft 4 that allows the pair of solar panels 2 and 3 to rotate and change between a folded state and an unfolded state, a pair of frames 5 and 6 that fix the pair of solar panels 2 and 3, respectively, a pair of wheels 7, 8 and a pair of stoppers 5g and 6g provided at one ends of lower sides 5b and 6b of the frames 5 and 6 on the opposite side to the upper sides 5a and 6a of the frames 5 and 6 having the rotation shaft 4, a pair of stoppers 9 and 10 provided at the other ends of the lower sides 5b and 6b, and a pair of legs 11 and 12 attached to the lower sides 5b and 6b of the frames 5 and 6 on which the wheels 7 and 8 and the stoppers 5g, 6g, 9 and 10 are provided, and c

title: CHANNEL STATE INFORMATION PREDICTION IN A WIRELESS NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein provide method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless network.
