The method includes receiving, by a next generation radio access network (NG-RAN) node, a multicast group paging message from an access and mobility management function (AMF) node; determining, by the NG-RAN node, one or more user equipments (UEs) that must be paged (e.g., multicast group paging) for MBS multicast session activation notification based on the received multicast group paging message; multicast group paging is performed by the NG-RAN node for the one or more determined UEs, wherein the NG-RAN node pages MBS multicast session activation notifications to the one or more determined UEs.

title: Communication method and device
abstract: The invention provides a communication method and device, which can solve the problem that a terminal device cannot normally trigger an MRU so as to improve the data transmission efficiency, and can be applied to 4G, 5G and future communication systems, such as a 6G system. The method comprises the following steps: a relay node executes mobile registration update (MRU), and the relay node receives an update response message from a network management network element. Wherein the update response message comprises updated tracking area code (TAC) information. And the relay node broadcasts the updated TAC information.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR NETWORK SLICE LOAD ANALYTICS
abstract: The disclosure relates to 5G or 6G communication systems to support higher data rates. According to an embodiment of the disclosure, in a method of analyzing a network slice load in a wireless communication system, a method for analyzing a network slice load based on the number of UEs that have established sessions and actually use data is provided.

title: METHODS FOR PERFORMING LOWER LAYER TRIGGERED MOBILITY IN WIRELESS NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein disclose methods for performing LTM. The method includes: receiving a LTM configuration in a RRC message indicating whether the UE needs to perform one of: a RLC reestablishment and a PDCP recovery during the LTM to a candidate cell; receiving one of a DL MAC CE or a DL L1 message used for triggering the LTM; performing a MAC operation comprising one of a complete MAC reset and a partial MAC reset; transmitting at least one uplink RRC message, wherein at least one uplink RRC message was transmitted within a specificized time interval before receiving the DL MAC CE or the DL L1 message; and performing at least one of the RLC reestablishment and the PDCP recovery based on the received indication.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SI ACQUISITION FOR NETWORK ENERGY SAVINGS IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes transmitting, to a first base station operating a first cell, a first message related to a random access procedure, the first message including information indicating whether the terminal needs broadcast information of a second cell, receiving, from the first base station, a second message as an acknowledgement of the first message, and receiving, from a second base station operating the second cell, the broadcast information.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS SUPPORTING SELFCONFIGURATION AND SELF-OPTIMIZATION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) is provided. The method includes transmitting, to a base station, a first message including information that a successful primary secondary cell (PSCell) report is available; receiving, from the base station, a second message requesting for the successful PSCell report; and transmitting, to the base station, a third message including the successful PSCell report.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSCEIVING FLIGHT PATH OF TERMINAL IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5G) or sixth generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a terminal includes transmitting, to a base station, a first message including first information indicating an update of flight path information, receiving, from the base station, a second message requesting a flight path report, the second message including second information configuring at least one of a number of waypoints for the flight path report, a distance parameter for the flight path report, or a time parameter for the flight path report, generating the flight path report based on the second information, and transmitting, to the base station, a third message including the flight path report.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SUBSCRIPTION OF UPF EVENT EXPOSURE SERVICE BASED ON UP
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5th generation (5G) communication system or a 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. A method is provided for a user plane function (UPF) in a wireless communication system. The method includes receiving, from a network function (NF), a first UPF control message protocol (UCMP) message requesting a service, wherein the first UCMP protocol message is generated by the NF; performing the service based on the first UCMP protocol message; generating a second UCMP protocol message including a result of the performed service; and transmitting, to the NF, the second UCMP protocol message

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR CONTINUITY OF MODEM SERVICE
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein provide a method for continuity of modem service by a user equipment (UE). The method includes detecting modem restart at the UE (100). The method includes determining whether a wireless network (400) and the UE (100) supports a session and mobility management feature upon detecting the restart of the modem (150), and identifying SM-MM contexts used by the UE (100) for accessing the modem service upon determining that the wireless network entity (200) supports the session and the mobility management feature. The method includes transmitting a first signaling message comprising at least one of the SM context and the MM context to the wireless network entity (200) for the continuity of the modem service.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CONTROLLING NETWORK SLICE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5G) or sixth generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method a network entity in a wireless communication system includes determining whether to transmit an update request message for a number of at least one user equipment (UE) with at least one packet data unit (PDU) session or packet data network (PDN) connection established on a network slice, based on the at least one PDU session or PDN connection established on the network slice, transmitting the update request message to a network slice admission control function (NSACF) entity, and receiving, from the NSACF entity, an update response message indicating whether the number of the at least one UE with the at least one PDU session or PDN connection established on the network slice reaches a maximum number of registered UEs with the network slice.

title: SCHEME OF MOBILITY AND METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR THE SAME
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth-generation (5G) or sixth-generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A scheme of mobility and method and apparatus for the same, and a method performed by a first node in a communication system are provided. The method includes transmitting a first message to a second node, the first message including information related to a state in which the first node is, receiving a second message from the second node, the second message including first configuration information related to signal transmission and/or reception by the first node, and transmitting and/or receiving a signal based on the first configuration information.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR COLLECTING DATA FOR ROAMING TERMINAL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a first network data analytics function (NWDAF) of a first public land mobile network (PLMN) in a communication system is provided. The method includes registering information on the first NWDAF to a first network repository function (NRF) of the first PLMN, transmitting, to the first NRF, a first message for requesting NWDAF information to collect, from an operator related to a second PLMN, analytics data for a roaming terminal, receiving, from the first NRF, a second message for providing information on a second NWDAF of the second PLMN, transmitting, to the second NWDAF, a third message for requesting the analytics data, based on the information on the second NWDAF, and receiving, from the second NWDAF, a fourth message notifying that provision of the analytics data is accepted.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DISASTER ROAMING IN A MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a communication system is provided. The method includes receiving disaster configuration information being encrypted; decrypting the disaster configuration information to verify an integrity of the disaster configuration information before acting upon or storing the disaster configuration information; and identifying whether a disaster roaming is available based on the disaster configuration information.

title: METHODS FOR PERFORMING LOWER LAYER TRIGGERED MOBILITY IN WIRELESS NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein disclose methods for performing LTM. The method includes: receiving a LTM configuration in a RRC message indicating whether the UE needs to perform one of: a RLC reestablishment and a PDCP recovery during the LTM to a candidate cell; receiving one of a DL MAC CE or a DL L1 message used for triggering the LTM; performing a MAC operation comprising one of a complete MAC reset and a partial MAC reset; transmitting at least one uplink RRC message, wherein at least one uplink RRC message was transmitted within a specificized time interval before receiving the DL MAC CE or the DL L1 message; and performing at least one of the RLC reestablishment and the PDCP recovery based on the received indication.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SERVICE RECOVERY IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5th generation (5G) or 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes selecting a stand-alone non-public network (SNPN), performing a registration on the selected SNPN, in case that the registration is rejected, adding the SNPN to a list of forbidden SNPNs, and in case that the SNPN is removed from the list of forbidden SNPNs and the UE is in a limited service state, performing an SNPN selection.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR FEDERATION MANAGEMENT
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein disclose a method of federation management. The method comprises establishing a network resource model (NRM) for facilitating edge federation between an originating operator and a participating operator, and enabling the originating operator to use edge resources of the participating operator.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR COORDINATED SERVICE RECOVERY IN WIRELESS NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein disclose a mobile network operation (MNO) entity for coordinated service recovery in a wireless communication system, the MNO entity comprising: a transceiver; and at least one processor coupled to the transceiver and configured to: receive, from a distribution system operator (DSO) entity, a first create managed object instance (MOI) request for creating an information object class (IOC), wherein the create MOI request comprises a plurality of information attributes for outage and rapid recovery, create the IOC for the outage and rapid recovery of the DSO entity based on the plurality of information attributes received from the DSO entity, and transmit, to the DSO entity, a first create MOI response after creation of the IOC.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR DETERMINING BEAM FOR TRANSMISSION OR RECEPTION OF DOWNLINK CONTROL INFORMATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system according to an embodiment of the present disclosure may comprise the steps of: receiving configuration information related to a control resource set (CORESET), wherein the configuration information includes a CORESET group identifier corresponding to each CORESET; identifying a first CORESET associated with a first CORESET group and a second CORESET associated with a second CORESET group on the basis of the CORESET group identifier; receiving an extended medium access control (MAC) control element (CE) for activation/deactivation for multiple transmission configuration indicator (TCI) states, wherein in the extended MAC CE, a value of a first field is configured, or the length of a second field is extended; receiving downlink information (DCI) including a TCI field on the basis of one of the first CORESET

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROCESSING INTEGRITY OF REMOTE UE PACKET TRANSMITTED THROUGH UE RELAY IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR COLLECTING DATA FOR ROAMING TERMINAL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a first network data analytics function (NWDAF) of a first public land mobile network (PLMN) in a communication system is provided. The method includes registering information on the first NWDAF to a first network repository function (NRF) of the first PLMN, transmitting, to the first NRF, a first message for requesting NWDAF information to collect, from an operator related to a second PLMN, analytics data for a roaming terminal, receiving, from the first NRF, a second message for providing information on a second NWDAF of the second PLMN, transmitting, to the second NWDAF, a third message for requesting the analytics data, based on the information on the second NWDAF, and receiving, from the second NWDAF, a fourth message notifying that provision of the analytics data is accepted.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ADAPTIVE BEAMFORMING USING SMART REPEATERS IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). In one embodiment, a method for is provided. The method includes receiving, from a user equipment (UE) via a first wireless communication channel, wireless fidelity (WiFi) signals that include UE location information indicating a location of the UE, wherein the WiFi signals are received by a processor operably connected to a transceiver that includes a first antenna array for communication with the UE and a second antenna array for communication with a base station; transmitting, to the base station via the second antenna array, uplink data and control information that includes the UE location information; receiving, from the base station via the second antenna array, downlink information intended for the UE; and forward-transmitting, to the UE via a second wireless communication channel, the downli

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PERFORMING BEAM MANAGEMENT IN CELLULAR SYSTEMS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Method and apparatuses for prediction based beam management in cellular systems. A method for a user equipment to report information related to a beam prediction is provided. The method includes receiving first information related to reception of reference signals for beam measurements, second information indicating one or more reporting quantities related to the beam prediction, third information related to determining the one or more reporting quantities, fourth information related to transmitting the one or more reporting quantities, and the RSs for the beam measurements based on the first information. The method further includes measuring the RSs, determining the one or more reporting quantities indicated by the second information based on the third information and the measurement of RSs, and transmitting a channel with the one or more reporting quantities based on the fourth i

title: SCHEDULING METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR FULL DUPLEX COMMUNICATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. The present disclosure provides a method for configuring and receiving a downlink control channel in a wireless communication system.

title: ELECTRONIC DEVICE FOR DISPLAYING CHANGE OF VIRTUAL OBJECT, AND METHOD THEREOF
abstract: At least one processor of a wearable device, according to an embodiment, may display a virtual space on a display on the basis of reception of an input for entering a virtual space. The at least one processor may display a first avatar, which has a first appearance and is a current representation of the user, in the virtual space. The at least one processor may display a visual object for a second avatar, which has a second appearance different from the first appearance of the first avatar and is a previous representation of the user, in the virtual space together with the first avatar. For example, this metaverse service may be provided through a network based on fifth generation (5G) and/or sixth generation (6G).

title: HYBRID JOINT TRANSMISSION FOR MULTIPLE ANTENNA COORDINATED MULTIPOINT SYSTEMS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. In an embodiment, a method performed by a network entity may be provided. The network entity may determine at least one hybrid joint transmission (Hybrid-JT) signal, wherein the at least one Hybrid-JT signal includes a diversity joint transmission (Div-JT) signal and a multiplexing joint transmission (Mux-JT) signal. The network entity may identify a first transmit receive point (TRP) and a second TRP to transmit the at least one Hybrid-JT signal to a user equipment (UE). The Div-JT signal may be transmitted by the first TRP and the second TRP. The Mux-JT signal may include a first signal transmitted by the first TRP and a second signal transmitted by the second TRP.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR BEAM INDICATION AND ASSOCIATION FOR CONTROL AND DATA CHANNELS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method by a UE includes receiving a first RRC configuration related to one or more TCI states for reception of a PDCCH; receiving a second RRC configuration related to one or more TCI states for reception of a PDSCH; receiving, in DCI, an indicator related to the one or more TCI states for reception of the PDSCH; and receiving a timing threshold for reception of the PDSCH. The method further includes identifying, based on the first RRC configuration, one or more TCI states to use for reception of the PDCCH and identifying, based on the timing threshold and the second RRC configuration or the indicator in the DCI, one or more TCI states to use for reception of the PDSCH.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR COMMON CHANNEL TRANSMISSION HANDLING FOR NETWORK ENERGY SAVING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Specifically, the present disclosure provides method and apparatus for common channel transmission handling or avoiding for network energy saving in wireless (mobile) communication system.

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR SELECTING BEAM IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: This disclosure relates to 5G or 6G communication systems for supporting higher data transmission rates following 4G communication systems, such as LTE. A method performed by a base station in a wireless communication system, may include transmitting a first signal containing information related to received power to a terminal; receiving a second signal containing information about the received power for the terminal from the terminal; transmitting the information about the received power to a network entity; and receiving a reception power matrix from the network entity.

title: Timeline and conditions for transmission of acknowledgement information
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method and apparatus for providing hybrid automatic repeat request acknowledgement (HARQ-ACK) information. A method includes receiving a first physical downlink control channel (PDCCH) that schedules transmission of a physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) in a time slot, and receiving received PDCCHs that respectively schedule first multicast physical downlink shared channels (PDSCHs). The first number of PDCCHs is not received after the first PDCCH, and the second number of PDCCHs is received after the first PDCCH. The method further includes determining that a transmission of a PUCCH for providing HARQ-ACK information associated with reception of a multicast PDSCH including the first multicast PDSCH will overlap with a PUSCH transmission in the time slot in time, and transmitting the PUSCH. The PUSCH includes a first HARQ-ACK information associated with the first numbe

title: Method and apparatus for same power transmission
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Methods and apparatus for identical power transmission. The method includes receiving first information indicating transmission of a channel using the same power over more than one time slot and second information indicating transmission of a channel using the same power over more than one time slot after a power change. The method further includes determining a first number of consecutive time slots, a second number of consecutive time slots from the first number of consecutive time slots for transmission of the channel at the same power, and a third number of consecutive time slots for transmission of the channel at the same power from the first number of consecutive time slots and after the second number of consecutive time slots. The method further includes transmitting the channel with the first power in a slot from the second number of consecutive slots, and transmitt

title: Ultra-wideband high-gain flatness high-linearity low-noise amplifier
abstract: An ultra-wideband low-noise amplifier with high gain flatness and high linearity comprises a radio frequency input module, a noise cancellation module, a current multiplexing module, a gain tuning module and a radio frequency output module, and the gain tuning module is arranged between the noise cancellation module and the radio frequency input module; the noise cancellation module is arranged between the radio frequency output module and the radio frequency input module and between the gain tuning module and the radio frequency output module; a first transistor in the radio frequency input module and a second transistor in the noise cancellation module form a current multiplexing module; the input matching and noise coefficient of the common-gate transistor M1 are decoupled through noise cancellation, the current multiplexing module is adopted to save the power consumption when the noise cancellation module is applied, and the gain tuning module is adopted to realize the high gain fl

title: Method and system for controlling ad hoc group communications using security context for critical task services
abstract: The present disclosure relates to 5th-Generation (5G) and/or 6th-Generation (6G) communication systems for supporting higher data transmission rates. Embodiments herein disclose a method for controlling ad hoc group communications using a security context for a critical task (MCX) service. The method comprises sending, by a first MC serving client device 100, an ad hoc group communication request message to an MC serving server to establish an ad hoc group communication with a second MC serving client device. The MC service server determines whether ad hoc group communication is supported and successfully authorized based on the received ad hoc group communication request. Further, the method includes receiving an ad hoc group communication request return message from the MC service server when the ad hoc group communication is supported and successfully authorized. Further, the method includes generating a security context for the second MC serving client device for the ad hoc group c

title: 6G MIMO transmission-oriented signal detection method based on geometric structure learning
abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of signal detection, and relates to a 6G MIMO transmission-oriented signal detection method based on geometric structure learning, which comprises the following steps of: S1, establishing an MIMO transmission system model; s2, setting an initial sphere radius; s3, executing an intra-domain sphere decoding algorithm by taking the set initial sphere radius as a radius and taking a 0 vector as a receiving signal; storing all receiving constellation point sets in a radius d range, and recording as a set phi; s4, enabling # imgabs0 # to be a solution of MMSE (Minimum Mean Square Error), finding a signal closest to the received signal y from the sum of # imgabs1 # and the set phi, updating # imgabs2 #, and S5, repeating the step S4 until convergence; according to the method, the complexity is about 1/2-1/10 of the SD algorithm and is smaller than the complexity of the SD algorithm, the detection performance is close to ML, in addition, the storage

title: 6G Internet of Vehicles access authentication method based on certificateless aggregation proxy signature
assignee: NO 30 INST CHINA ELECTRONIC TECH GROUP CORP
abstract: The invention discloses a certificateless aggregation proxy signature-based 6G Internet of Vehicles access authentication method, which comprises the following steps of: establishing a third-party key generation center, generating a public key and a private key, constructing interconnection and intercommunication of upper-layer management nodes and finishing deployment of access points in each subnet; point-to-point direct authentication is realized based on a certificateless proxy signature algorithm, an authentication initiator recovers a proxy private key, and a disturbance factor is introduced to randomize identity information and public commitment; generating a certificateless proxy signature with specified verifier characteristics as a certificate for authenticating the legality of the certificateless proxy signature by taking the target public key as a constraint; after the vehicle node completes the bidirectional identity authentication with the ground base station, the node au

title: Method and wireless network for managing over-the-air subscription information for UAV
abstract: Disclosed is a method related to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The method for updating over-the-air subscription information of a UAV in a wireless network by an AMF device includes receiving, from the UAV, a first registration request message including a CAA-level UAV identifier for accessing a UAS service; determining whether the UDM device allows over-the-air subscription of the UAV to the UAS service; in response to determining that the over-the-air subscription of the UAV to the UAS service is not allowed, transmitting a registration rejection message with an indication that the UAS service is unavailable to the UAV; and registering the UAV for the standard service.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PAGING BASED ON PAGING SUBGroup IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method may include receiving system information associated with a paging sub-group from a base station; identifying, based on the system information, a value associated with a number of paging subgroups for a subpacket assigned by a core network (CN); and determining, based on the identified value, a paging subgroup ID associated with the UE identity (ID)-based subpacket.

title: Microwave and millimeter wave integrated dual-frequency circularly polarized antenna
abstract: The invention discloses a microwave and millimeter wave integrated dual-frequency circularly polarized antenna, which comprises a millimeter wave frequency band first radiation unit, a millimeter wave frequency band second radiation unit, a millimeter wave frequency band third radiation unit, a millimeter wave frequency band fourth radiation unit, a millimeter wave circularly polarized feed network, a microwave radiation slot and a microwave circularly polarized filtering feed network, arranging in two rows and two columns; the microwave radiation slots are two rectangular slots which are orthogonally arranged; the microwave circular polarization feed network provides equal-amplitude and unequal-phase feed for the equivalent square patch of the whole millimeter wave circular polarization antenna through two rectangular gaps which are orthogonally arranged. The antenna structure can generate circularly polarized waves with relatively wide impedance bandwidth and relatively wide axial ra

title: C-NOMA enabled 6G heterogeneous network unmanned aerial vehicle trajectory optimization method
abstract: The invention discloses a C-NOMA enabled 6G heterogeneous network unmanned aerial vehicle track optimization method. According to the scheme, firstly, a C-NOMA enabled air-ground integrated heterogeneous network model is provided, and a ground user takes an unmanned aerial vehicle as an intermediate node and is connected to an airship by sending information to the unmanned aerial vehicle; secondly, the unmanned aerial vehicle is set to fly at a fixed height, a propulsion power consumption model and a time slot of the unmanned aerial vehicle are introduced, and a trajectory optimization non-convex problem is converted into a convex problem by adopting an approximate convex optimization (SCA) method; and finally, obtaining an approximate optimal result of the flight path and the flight speed of the unmanned aerial vehicle by using a CVX tool. According to the invention, the energy efficiency and spectrum efficiency of the system are improved, and the system performance is effectively imp

title: DESIGN RELATED TO TXRU CARRIER SWITCHING FOR B5G/6G
abstract: Apparatus and methods are provided for TxRU carrier switching. In an embodiment, the UE is configured with an anchor carrier and one or more secondary carriers in an anchor cell. In an embodiment, the TxRU carrier switching is configured as a supplementary uplink (SUL)-based carrier switching with a supplementary carrier, or as a CA-based carrier switching with a supplementary cell. In an embodiment, one or more secondary carriers are supplementary carriers of an anchor cell, the anchor carrier is a TDD carrier or a frequency division duplex (FDD) carrier, and the supplementary carrier is configured as a TDD carrier, an FDD carrier, a supplementary uplink carrier (SUL) or a supplementary downlink carrier (SDL). In another embodiment, the one or more secondary carriers are supplementary cells different from the anchor cell, and the supplementary cells are configured with MAC control elements (CEs).

title: Method and apparatus for repetition scheme of downlink data channel in communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure generally relates to operation of a terminal and a base station in a wireless communication system. Specifically, the present disclosure relates to a method and an apparatus for repeated transmission of a downlink data channel in a wireless communication system.

title: DAG-based fragmented cascade distributed 6G network joint optimization method and device
abstract: The invention discloses a fragmented cascade distributed 6G network joint optimization method and device based on a DAG, and belongs to the technical field of information communication, and the method comprises the steps: solving a constructed joint optimization model with the goal of minimizing the weighted value of energy consumption and time delay in a round of training, and obtaining a joint optimization model; obtaining an optimal fragment number, an optimal network topology structure and an optimal computing resource allocation scheme, thereby performing joint optimization on the fragment number, the network topology structure and computing resource allocation of the constructed DAG-based fragment cascade distributed 6G network; wherein the fragmented cascade distributed 6G network based on the DAG comprises a communication network, a DML network and a block chain network, the communication network is used for realizing network communication among nodes, the DML network is used f

title: WIRELESS NETWORK AND METHODS TO MAINTAIN MA PDU SESSION AT NSACF
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein disclose a wireless network and methods to maintain a MA PDU session at a NSACF entity. The method includes determining, by a SMF entity, that a PDU session is the MA PDU session based on an indication from a UE. Further, the method includes indicating, by the SMF entity, that the PDU session is the MA PDU session to the NSACF entity in response to determination. The wireless network may maintain the count accurately even though the PDU session is a MA PDU session.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSCEIVING FLIGHT PATH OF TERMINAL IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. a method and apparatus of a terminal according to the present disclosure include a process of receiving setting information for reporting a flight path from a base station and reporting information on a predicted flight path to the base station. the method and apparatus of the base station according to the present disclosure include a process of transmitting the setting information for reporting the flight path to the terminal and receiving the information on the predicted flight path from the terminal.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR COLLECTING DATA OF A ROMAING MOBILE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. the present disclosure proposes a method for collecting user and terminal information for a roaming terminal, and can be applied to a case where a communication service provider wants to collect and utilize user and terminal information for performance or the like.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS OF REPETITION SCHEME FOR DOWNLINK DATA CHANNEL IN COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure relates generally to operations of a terminal and a base station in a wireless communication system. Specifically, the present disclosure relates to a method and apparatus for repetitive transmission of a downlink data channel in a wireless communication system.

title: A METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CHARGING FOR COLLECTION AND PROVISION OF ANALYSIS DATA IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATIONS SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. the present disclosure proposes a method for managing charging for transmission of related data when collecting state and analysis data from a terminal or providing the state and analysis data to the terminal in a mobile communication system. the present disclosure can be applied to a case where a communication service provider collects or provides user and network data to the terminal for the purpose of improving the performance of the terminal and network.

title: A METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVIDING ANALYTICS ON A MOBILE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATIONS SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. In addition, the present disclosure proposes a method for providing analysis information on a terminal to an application server or the like in a mobile communication system, and may be used when an application provider wants to use terminal analysis information for performance improvement of an application through a contract with a communication provider or the like.

title: NODE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM AND METHOD PERFORMED BY THE SAME
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. specifically, the present disclosure provides a node of a wireless communication system and a method performed thereby. the method performed by a second node in the wireless communication system according to embodiments of the present disclosure includes the steps of: receiving a second message including information on measurement relaxation from a first node; and transmitting a seventh message including a reference signal transmission proposal to a third node.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVIDING DATA CHANNEL APPLICATION IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. A method of a terminal in a communication system of the present disclosure includes the steps of: selecting an individual data channel application based on a portal data channel application provided through a first bootstrap data channel; transmitting an SDP proposal associated with a second bootstrap data channel for the selected individual data channel application to a first network entity; receiving an SDP response corresponding to the SDP proposal from the first network entity; and acquiring the individual data channel application through the second bootstrap data channel based on the SDP response.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR NETWORK SLICE LOAD ANALYTICS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. according to an embodiment of the present invention, in a method for analyzing a network slice load in a wireless communication system, a method for analyzing a network slice load based on the number of terminals in which a session is established and data is actually used is proposed.

title: ELECTRONIC DEVICE AND METHOD FOR REQUESTING NSSAI IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5G) or sixth generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transfer rate. A method performed by a user equipment in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes executing an application in the user equipment, identifying a user equipment route selection policy (URSP) rule mapped to the application, based on configuration information on the application, identifying a single-network slice selection assistance information (S-NSSAI) mapped to the application, based on the URSP rule mapped to the application, and transmitting a message requesting registration of the S-NSSAI mapped to the application to a core network via a base station. The S-NSSAI may correspond to a network slice selection assistance information (NSSAI) not allowed by the core network to be registered among NSSAIs configured for the user equipment.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SUPPORTING TIME SYNCHRONIZATION
abstract: The disclosure relates to 5G or 6G communication systems to support higher data rates. Disclosed is a method performed by a TS CTSF in a wireless communication system, including receiving, from an AF, a first request message for time synchronization including a first time synchronization error budget for a UE, in response to receiving the first request message from the AF, transmitting to a UDM, a second request message for time synchronization subscription data of the UE, receiving, from the UDM, the time synchronization subscription data of the UE including a second time synchronization error budget for the UE, determining a third time synchronization error budget based on the first time synchronization error budget and the second time synchronization error budget, and transmitting, to a PCF, a third request message for time synchronization including the third time synchronization error budget.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ACCESSING NETWORK BY REDUCED-CAPABILITY TERMINAL HAVING LIMITED RECEPTION CAPABILITY IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a terminal of a wireless communication system, according to one embodiment of the present disclosure, comprises the steps of: receiving a master information block (MIB) from a base station; receiving, on the basis of the MIB, a system information block (SIB) 1 from the base station; receiving, on the basis of the SIB 1 from the base station, at least one piece of system information including cell reselection-related information; and performing a cell reselection procedure on the basis of the cell reselection-related information. The cell reselection-related information includes cell reselection priority information for each frequency for a terminal having limited reception capability and cell reselection priority information for each frequency for an ordinary terminal.

title: METHOD OF CONFIGURING HANDOVER USING PHYSICAL LAYER AND MAC LAYER SIGNALING IN NEXT GENERATION MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5G) or sixth generation (6G) communication system to support a data transmission rate higher than before. A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving, from a base station, a radio resource control (RRC) message including lower layer triggered mobility (LTM) configuration information, the LTM configuration information being associated with at least one RRC reconfiguration, receiving, from the base station, a medium access control (MAC) control element (CE) for triggering a LTM handover, the MAC CE including an identifier (ID) corresponding to one of the at least one RRC reconfiguration, and performing the LTM handover to a target cell based on the one of the at least one RRC reconfiguration.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR UPDATING RNA DURING SDT IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system is provided. The method may include: receiving, from a base station, a radio resource control (RRC) release message including a parameter indicating a timer that triggers a radio access network based notification area (RNA) update; transitioning to an RRC inactive state and starting the timer, based on the RRC release message; in case that criteria for small data transmission (SDT) are met while the timer is running, transmitting, to the base station, an RRC resume request message for the SDT, wherein the timer is not stopped when the RRC resume request message is transmitted; and in case that the timer expires while the SDT is ongoing, setting a variable indicating whether the RNA update is pending, to a true value.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR LOGGING LBT FAILURE INFORMATION IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5G) or sixth generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method and an apparatus for logging listen-before-talk (LBT) failure information in a mobile communication system are provided. The operation method of a terminal in a wireless communication system includes attempting to transmit a preamble in a random access procedure via an unlicensed spectrum, in case that one or more failures of the attempting to transmit the preamble according to LBT failure is detected, generating information on random access report including a number of the one or more failures of the attempting to transmit the preamble, accessing to a base station based on the preamble after the attempting to transmit the preamble is successful, and transmitting, to the base station, the information on random access report.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS OF QOE REPORTING FOR XR MEDIA SERVICES
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method of a user equipment (UE) performing a quality of experience (QoE) reporting operation for extended reality (XR) media services is disclosed. The method comprises receiving configuration information for a QoE reporting operation, determining whether an event for the QoE reporting operation is triggered based on the configuration information, generating a frame rate QoE metric comprising pose information of the UE, generating a round-trip time (RTT) QoE metric comprising a tethered delay between at least one tethered device and the UE, generating a codec QoE metric comprising 3D media codec type for 3D media, in response to the event being triggered, generating a QoE report comprising at least one of the frame rate QoE metric, the RTT QoE metric, or the codec QoE metric, and transmitting the generated QoE report.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR MEASURING QOE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving, from a base station, configuration information for a quality of experience (QoE) measurement, storing a result of the QoE measurement performed in a radio resource control (RRC) idle state or an RRC inactive state in an access stratum (AS) layer memory of the UE, and transmitting, to the base station, a QoE report including the result of the QoE measurement based on the configuration information.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CONNECTING QOS FLOW BASED TERMINAL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a UE in a wireless communication system includes receiving, from at least one electronic device, a first message requesting a connection to a PIN, identifying to generate a QoS flow for the at least one electronic device, based on the first message, transmitting, to an SMF entity, via an AMF entity, a second message requesting a PDU session modification corresponding to the PIN, receiving, from a BS, a PDU session modification command message including a QoS rule, and applying the QoS rule to the at least one electronic device.

title: DESIGN ON TXRU CARRIER SWITCH FOR B5G/6G
abstract: Apparatus and methods are provided for TxRU carrier switch. In one embodiment, the UE is configured with an anchor carrier in an anchor cell and one or more secondary carriers. In one embodiment, the TxRU carrier switch is configured as supplementary uplink (SUL)-based carrier switch with supplementary carriers or configured as a CA-based carrier switch with supplementary cells. In one embodiment, the one or more secondary carriers are supplementary carriers of the anchor cell, and wherein the anchor carrier is TDD carrier or frequency division duplex (FDD) carrier, and wherein the supplementary carrier is configured as a TDD carrier, a FDD carrier, a supplementary uplink carrier (SUL), or a supplementary downlink carrier (SDL). In another embodiment, the one or more secondary carriers are supplementary cells different from the anchor cell, and wherein the supplementary cells are configured with MAC control element (CE).

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR INFORMATION TRANSMISSION AND RECEPTION IN SATELLITE COMMUNICATIONS SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving, from a base station, configuration information on a set of one or more numbers for a PUCCH repetition; receiving, from the base station, DCI scheduling a PDSCH; receiving, from the base station, the PDSCH based on the DCI; determining a plurality of slots for the PUCCH repetition based on the configuration information in case that the set of one or more numbers comprises a single value or based on the configuration information and the DCI in case that the set of one or more numbers comprises more than one value; and transmitting, to the base station, a PUCCH including HARQ-ACK information for the PDSCH over the determined plurality of slots.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR AUTHORIZATION BASED ON UPF IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth-generation (5G) or sixth-generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user plane function (UPF) entity in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving, from a network entity requesting a subscription of a UPF service, a UPF service subscription request message, identifying whether an authorization of the UPF service is required and an entity associated with the authorization of the UPF service, transmitting, to the entity associated with the authorization of the UPF service, an authorization request message for the UPF service, receiving, from the entity associated with the authorization of the UPF service, an authorization response message for the UPF service.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR PROVIDING NETWORK SLICE WITH FINE GRANULARITY IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure provides a method for providing subdivided network slices in a wireless communication system. A method for operating a user equipment (UE) according to an embodiment may comprise transmitting, to an access and mobility management function (AMF), a registration request message including a request slice (requested NSSAI) and a per-cell slice service area support indicator (CSSA support indication) and receiving, from the AMF, a registration accept message including service area information per-allowed slice (slice area per allowed NSSAI) about the UE determined based on the request slice and the per-cell slice service area support indicator.

title: Test Signals and Feedback for Beam Optimization in 5G and 6G
abstract: Base stations and user devices in 5G and 6G networks can save enormous amounts of power, while minimizing background generation, by directing narrow transmission and reception beams toward each other. To optimize those beam directions, and their lateral beam widths, and the frequency and power transmitted, a small set of test signals can be appended to each message, and a feedback message selecting the best-received test signal can be appended to the acknowledgement. In this way, the base station can develop a custom set of beam parameters optimized for each user device, and each user device can maintain its uplink beam squarely toward the base station. Result: higher signal quality per watt transmitted, fewer faults and retransmissions, and improved network operations overall.

title: SIXTH GENERATION (6G) SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE AND FUNCTIONS
abstract: Various embodiments herein provide techniques related to sixth generation (6G) system architecture and functions. For example, embodiments may relate to one or more of: Design principle and system architecture; Orchestration frontend service; Dynamic device-network computing scaling; RDMA over radio; Cloud workload offloading to network; Computing-embedded air interface; Service chain aware transport; and/or Enabling Al capabilities. Other embodiments may be described and/or claimed.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR QOE MEASUREMENT OF UE IN DUAL CONNECTIVITY IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method of UE comprises: receiving, from a base station, configuration information on a quality of experience (QoE) measurement, wherein the configuration information includes information indicating a signaling radio bearer (SRB) for transmitting a report of the QoE measurement; generating a QoE report by performing the QoE measurement based on the configuration information; and transmitting the QoE report via the SRB based on the information.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR TRANSMITTING AND/OR RECEIVING SIGNALS FOR POSITIONING
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a UE in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving configuration information for a positioning signal, wherein the configuration information includes at least one of time domain information or frequency domain information related to frequency hopping, and wherein the frequency domain information includes at least one of information related to a frequency hopping BWP or information related to a frequency hopping RB; and transmitting the positioning signal based on the configuration information.

title: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR HANDLING KEY DISTRIBUTION FOR MULTICAST AND BROADCAST SERVICES IN WIRELESS NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Accordingly, the embodiments herein provide a method for handling key distribution for multicast and broadcast services (MBS) in a wireless network. The method includes sending, by an application function (AF) server (100), an MB session announcement message to the UE (300) in the wireless network, where the MB session announcement message includes the TMGI and the HL MC address. Further, the method includes generating, by the AF server (100), the sessionkey (KMBS) for the TMGI and the HL MC address, where the sessionkey (KMBS) is provided to the UE (300) and the plurality of network entities (200). Further, the method includes protecting, by the AF server (100), a MBS traffic associated with the UE (300) and the plurality of network entities (200) using the generated session key (KMBS).

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR MANAGING SECURITY DOMAIN ACCESS INFORMATION OF MIGRATED USERS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method and a first mission critical (MC) server are provided for managing security domain access information of migrated users in a private communication in MC services. A first MC server receives a private call request from an MC service user of a first UE for establishing the private communication with an MC service user of a second UE. It is determined whether the MC service user of the second UE is migrated from the first MC server to at least one secondary MC server. A private call redirection message is generated, and the private call redirection message is transmitted to the MC service user of the first UE to establish the private communication with the MC service user of the second UE based on the private call redirection message.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR BACKHAUL INFORMATION BASED SESSION MANAGEMENT IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. a method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system according to an embodiment of the present disclosure may include the steps of: receiving a first control signal transmitted from a base station; processing the received first control signal; and transmitting a second control signal generated based on the processing to the base station.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR BUFFER STATUS REPORT IN A WIRELESS COMMUNCATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. the present disclosure provides a communication method comprising the steps of: receiving a first control signal transmitted from a base station; processing the received first control signal; and transmitting a second control signal generated based on the processing to the base station.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVIDING NETWORK SLICE WITH FINE GRANULARITY IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates. The present disclosure proposes a method for providing a segmented network slice in a wireless communication system. The method for operating a user equipment (UE) according to an embodiment of the present disclosure may comprise: a step of transmitting a registration request message including a requested slice (requested NSSAI) and a cell-based slice service area support indicator (CSSA support indication) to an access and mobility management function (AMF); and a step of receiving a registration acceptance message including allowed slice-based service area information (slice area per Allowed NSSAI) for the UE, which is determined based on the requested NSSAI and the CSSA support indication, from the AMF.

title: MAC APPARATUS AND METHOD TO HANDLE THE FAILURE OF THE HANDOVER USING PHYSICAL LAYER AND MEDIUM ACCESS LAYER SIGNALING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. the present disclosure relates to an operation of a terminal in a mobile communication system, a method for defining a failure during handover, and an operation performed by the terminal during handover failure.

title: QOE METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MEASURING QOE IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. according to various embodiments of the present disclosure, a method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system may comprise the steps of: receiving a first control signal transmitted from a base station; processing the received first control signal; and transmitting a second control signal generated based on the processing to the base station.

title: TCI METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CONFIGURATING TCI STATE FOR SWITCHING CELL
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. a control signal processing method in a wireless communication system of the present disclosure includes the steps of: receiving a first control signal transmitted from a base station; processing the received first control signal; and transmitting a second control signal generated based on the processing to the base station.

title: A METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SUPPORTING TIME SYNCHRONIZATION
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. according to an embodiment of the present invention, disclosed are a method and an apparatus for providing stable time synchronization by notifying a specific time synchronization method to a terminal or an application when a problem occurs in the specific time synchronization method and replacing the same with a time synchronization method in which a problem does not occur.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MANAGING BINDING INFORMATION FOR TERMINAL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. Disclosed is a method for managing binding information for a roaming terminal by a home-policy control function (H-PCF) in a wireless communication system. The method includes the steps of: receiving a protocol data unit (PDU) session creation request for a terminal from a visited-session management function (V-SMF) and performing a session management policy association procedure for the terminal; generating binding information for the terminal; and transmitting a message for registering the generated binding information to a home binding support function (H-BSF).

title: QoE METHOD AND APPRATUS FOR QOE MEASUREMENT OF UE IN DUAL CONNECTIVITY IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. the present disclosure provides a communication method comprising the steps of: receiving a first control signal transmitted from a base station; processing the received first control signal; and transmitting a second control signal generated based on the processing to the base station.

title: Method and apparatus for managing slice-based cell reselection priority in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system supporting a higher data rate. The present disclosure relates to a method performed by a UE in a wireless communication system, may include: camping on a first cell of a base station based on first system information (SI) received from the first cell; receiving second system information from the first cell, the second system information including information related to slice-based cell reselection; identifying a second cell to be reselected on the basis of the information related to the slice-based cell reselection and the information related to the priority of the slice group received through the upper layer; and performing a cell reselection procedure on the identified second cell.

title: Method and apparatus for managing cell barring in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an embodiment, the method comprises: receiving a system information block 1 (SIB1) from a cell; and if the UE is a redcap UE and there is no intra-frequency reselection field in SIB1, excluding the cell as a candidate for cell selection or reselection within a predetermined duration, and reselecting another cell on the same frequency as the cell.

title: Preparation method and application of ultralow-profile copper foil in boric acid system
abstract: The invention discloses a preparation method and application of an ultralow-profile copper foil in a boric acid system. The preparation method comprises the following steps: providing a boric acid system which comprises benzotriazole, organic ammonium salt and boric acid; the to-be-treated copper foil serving as the working electrode, the counter electrode and the electrolyte jointly form an electrochemical reaction system, and the electrolyte comprises a phosphoric acid-ethanol water solution and a boric acid system; and electrifying the electrochemical reaction system, and carrying out electrochemical polishing on the surface of the copper foil to be treated to prepare the ultralow-profile copper foil. According to the preparation method of the ultralow-profile copper foil provided by the invention, the ultralow-profile copper foil suitable for the PCB for 6G communication is prepared by utilizing a boric acid system, and meanwhile, pitting corrosion in an electrochemical polishing p

title: Large-bandwidth OTFS signal receiving channel frequency response automatic compensation method and device
abstract: The invention discloses a large-bandwidth OTFS signal receiving channel frequency response automatic compensation method and device, and belongs to the technical field of 6G broadband communication wireless. Comprising the steps of down-conversion, A/D acquisition, IQ demodulation, decimation filtering, FIR filtering, CP elimination, M-point FFT, M-point IFFT, N-point FFT, digital demodulation, digital modulation, ISSFT conversion, group delay and frequency response error information acquisition, shaping filter h factor setting, M-point DFT, group delay and frequency response error information elimination and filtering factor conversion. Performing down-conversion, signal processing, FIR (Finite Impulse Response) filtering and signal demodulation measures on a received signal to recover an original code stream; meanwhile, an original code stream is compared with a theoretical signal, a frequency response error and a group time delay error of a receiving channel are calculated, error da

title: Method and system for minimizing network delay based on federated learning for 6G network
abstract: The invention provides a federated learning-based network delay minimization method and system for a 6G network, and the method comprises the steps: building a federated learning model for the interaction behavior of a wireless terminal device and a server of the 6G network, and carrying out the multi-round local model training based on federated learning. And constructing a utility function of wireless terminal equipment of a follower in the Stackelberg model, and establishing an incentive mechanism to promote the wireless terminal equipment to be interested in the maximized utility function. And then, on the premise of maximizing the utility function of the wireless terminal equipment, the global convergence time of the server is minimized, so that the 6G network delay is minimized, and game equilibrium is achieved. According to the method, the effective return of the wireless terminal equipment can be increased, the global convergence time of the server in the federated learning pro

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR MANAGING SECURITY DOMAIN ACCESS INFORMATION OF MIGRATED USERS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments are directed to a method and a first mission critical (MC) server for managing security domain access information of migrated users in a private communication in MC services. The method includes receiving, by a first MC server, a private call request from an MC service user of a first UE for establishing the private communication with an MC service user of at least one second UE. Further, the method includes determining whether the MC service user of the at least one second UE is migrated from the first MC server to at least one secondary MC server. Further, the method includes generating a private call redirection message, and includes transmitting the private call redirection message to the MC service user of the first UE to establish the private communication with the MC service user of the at least one second UE based on the private call redirection message.

title: ENHANCED COORDINATED SERVICE RECOVERY IN WIRELESS NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a first server in a mobile communication system, the method comprising: performing, with a second server, a create managed object instance (MOI) procedure for a first information object class (IOC) associated with recovery of an energy service; and creating a first MOI associated with uninterruptable power supply (UPS) related information of the first server, based on the create MOI procedure, wherein the UPS related information of the first server is included in the first IOC.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MANAGING FORBIDDEN TRACKING AREAS IN COMMUNICATION NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure relates to method and apparatus for managing forbidden tracking areas in communication network. The method comprises receiving, from an access and mobility function (AMF), a reject message including a rejection cause regarding an access to at least one network slice, wherein the UE is camped on a first tracking area of a plurality of tracking areas associated with a public land mobile network (PLMN), and wherein the rejection cause is based on an error indicating unavailability of the at least one network slice in the first tracking area; storing information of the first tracking area in a list of forbidden tracking areas for the UE; and recording the error while storing the information of the first tracking area in the list of forbidden tracking areas for the UE.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR KEY SYNCHRONIZATION AND KEY UPDATE AND KEY DISTRIBUTION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments disclosed herein provide a method for key synchronization for integrated access and backhaul (IAB) migration in a wireless network (600) by a source IAB Donor-Control Unit (CU) (214). The method includes detecting a migration of an IAB-MT (504) from a source IAB Donor-CU (214) to a target IAB Donor-CU node (224). The IAB-MT remains connected to the source IAB Donor-CU (214) via a F1 connection and establishes radio resource control (RRC) connection with the target IAB Donor-CU (224). Further, the method includes retaining a security context associated with the F1 connection of the IAB-MT based on the detection. Further, the method includes migrating the IAB-MT from the source IAB Donor-CU (214) to the target IAB Donor-CU (224) by establishing a secure RRC connection between the IAB-MT and the target IAB Donor-CU (224).

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR HANDLING FORBIDDEN TRACKING AREA IDENTITY BELONGS TO DIFFERENT PUBLIC LAND MOBILE NETWORK-IDENTIFIER IN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR HANDLING UNAVAILABILITY PERIOD COLLISION IN WIRELESS NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Specifically, the disclosure related to a method for handling unavailability period collision in a wireless network (1000) by a network apparatus (200). The method includes identifying that a collision occurs between a network slice specific authentication and authorization (NSSAA) procedure and a registration procedure. Further, the method includes in case that a registration request message for the registration procedure includes information on an unavailable period, aborting the NSSAA procedure.

title: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR DOWNLINK SYNCHRONIZATION IN WIRELESS NETWORKS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate and relates to a method and system for downlink synchronization in a wireless network, more particularly, the disclosed method and system relate to downlink synchronization technique design for Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) cellular systems.

title: GENERATING DEPTH MAP OF AN ENVIRONMENT IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a communication method and system for converging a 5th-Generation (5G) communication system or a 6th-Generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th-Generation (4G) system. Embodiments herein disclose a method and a network node (110) for generating a depth map of an environment by the network node (110) in a wireless communication system. The method includes training at least one untrained ML model for at least one sub-cell area. Further, the method includes transmitting, by the network node (110), a request for at least one sub-cell area identifier to the UE (120) and receiving a response including the at least one sub-cell area identifier and input data corresponding to the at least one sub-cell area identifier; wherein the input data is a sensing data of the environment of the at least one sub-cell area. Further, the method includes generating, by the network node (110), the depth map of the at least one sub-cell

title: NODE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM AND METHOD PERFORMED BY THE SAME
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate.The present disclosure provides a node in a wireless communication system and a method performed by the same. A method performed by a second node in a wireless communication system according to embodiments of the present disclosure may include: receiving a second message including information about measurement relaxation from a first node; and transmitting a seventh message including a reference signal transmission suggestion to a third node.

title: METHOD OF CONFIGURING HANDOVER USING PHYSICAL LAYER AND MAC LAYER SIGNALING IN NEXT GENERATION MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5G) or sixth generation (6G) communication system to support a data transmission rate higher than before. A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving, from a base station, a radio resource control (RRC) message including lower layer triggered mobility (LTM) configuration information, the LTM configuration information being associated with at least one RRC reconfiguration, receiving, from the base station, a medium access control (MAC) control element (CE) for triggering a LTM handover, the MAC CE including an identifier (ID) corresponding to one of the at least one RRC reconfiguration, and performing the LTM handover to a target cell based on the one of the at least one RRC reconfiguration.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CONNECTING QOS FLOW BASED TERMINAL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a UE in a wireless communication system includes receiving, from at least one electronic device, a first message requesting a connection to a PIN, identifying to generate a QoS flow for the at least one electronic device, based on the first message, transmitting, to an SMF entity, via an AMF entity, a second message requesting a PDU session modification corresponding to the PIN, receiving, from a BS, a PDU session modification command message including a QoS rule, and applying the QoS rule to the at least one electronic device.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CONFIGURING REPEATER BEAM IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates. According to various embodiments of the present disclosure, in a wireless communication system, a method performed by a network-controlled repeater (NCR) may comprise: a step of receiving information indicating at least one beam from a base station; a step of determining a beam to be applied to a backhaul link and an access link based on the received information; a step of receiving a signal from the base station through the backhaul link based on the determined beam; and a step of transmitting the received signal to a terminal through the access link based on the determined beam.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS OF REPETITION SCHEME FOR DOWNLINK DATA CHANNEL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. the present disclosure relates to operations of a terminal and a base station in a wireless communication system, and more particularly, to a method and apparatus for repeatedly transmitting a downlink data channel in a wireless communication system.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PERFORMING MEDIA CALL SERVICE
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates. The present disclosure provides a method for performing a media call service of an originating terminal in a wireless communication network, wherein the method comprises the following operations of: transmitting a first SDP offer message including information indicating a content type of at least one 3D avatar and information on whether the originating terminal supports movement of the at least one 3D avatar to a receiving terminal; receiving, as a response to the first SDP offer message, an SDP response message including information on whether the receiving terminal supports movement of the at least one 3D avatar from the receiving terminal; generating movement information of the at least one 3D avatar based on the support information of the receiving terminal included in the SDP response message; and performing a 3D avatar call service with the receiving terminal using the generated mo

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PHYSICAL downlink SHARED CHANNEL TRANSMISSION FOR FULL DUPLEX IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. the present disclosure describes a method for determining a pucch transmission location in a frequency domain by a terminal in an sbfd environment. in each embodiment, the terminal discloses a method for determining a ul bwp criterion, a method for determining a ul subband criterion, a combination of the methods, or a method for determining a transmission location based on a configuration of a base station.

title: ENHANCED METHOD AND APPARATUS IN TIME DOMAIN FOR FULL DUPLEX COMMUNICATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNCIATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates.
