abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data rate. A method performed by an SMF entity in a wireless communication system may include an operation of receiving TIS binding information for scheduling a TIS service, wherein the TIS service includes at least one first service flow including data having periodicity and at least one second service flow including data having no periodicity, and an operation of identifying a network entity to perform scheduling for the TIS service. The TIS binding information may include binding information including at least one of a TIS binding support indicator or information on mapping between the first service flow and the second service flow.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DCI-BASED DYNAMIC BEAM INDICATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. In particular, the present disclosure relates to a method and apparatus for downlink control information (DCI)-based dynamic beam indication in a wireless communication system. The method performed by a user equipment (UE) includes: receiving a TCI state set including two TCI states in a first DCI; receiving a high layer radio resource control (RRC) signaling, the RRC signaling including a parameter indicating whether a TCI state selection indicator exists in a second DCI that schedules a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH); and receiving the second DCI. The method further includes determining, based on the parameter, whether the TCI state selection indicator exists in the second DCI; identifying at least one TCI state from the set of TCI states to be used for receiving the PDSCH based on the presence or absence of the TCI state selection indicator; and receiving the P

title: Ultra-dense Internet of Things computing unloading method in converter station scene
assignee: GUIYANG BUREAU EHV POWER TRANS CO CHINA SOUTHERN POWER GRID CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides an ultra-dense Internet of Things computing unloading method in a converter station scene. Relates to the field of Internet of Things, and aims to solve the problems that many mobile applications requested by the Internet of Things are compute-intensive, high energy consumption is needed and resources are limited. The technical method provided by the invention comprises the step of providing an ultra-dense Internet of Things computing unloading method in a converter station scene in a 6G mobile communication satellite ground network to realize effective utilization of resources. In a multi-user ultra-dense edge server scene, a system model is established according to the energy consumption of the Internet of Things and the processing delay of a calculation task, and the optimal MECO problem in the ultra-dense Internet of Things is modeled as a constraint optimization problem, so that the overall calculation overhead is minimized while the given wireless channel c

title: Method and apparatus for authenticating user when changing device
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure relates to a method and apparatus for reinstalling a configuration file when changing an eUICC terminal. A method performed by a first terminal in a wireless communication system supporting device change comprises the steps of: transmitting a device change request message for device change for a configuration file of the first terminal to a configuration file server; receiving a device change request response message from the profile server, the device change request response message including an acknowledgement code requirement indicator for the device change; acknowledgement of receipt of an acknowledgement code input based on an acknowledgement code requirement indicator for the device change; generating acknowledgement code hash information based on the acknowledgement code; and transmitting a device change acknowledgement message including the ac

title: Method and device for determining frequency domain resource of physical sidelink feedback channel
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Methods and apparatus for frequency domain enhancement on a physical sidelink feedback channel (PSFCH) in a wireless communication system. A method of operating a user equipment (UE) includes receiving a physical sidelink shared channel (PSSCH) enabling hybrid automatic repeat request (HARQ) feedback, and determining interleaving from a set of interleaving. Each interleaving in the set of interleaving includes a first set of resource blocks (RBs) having uniform intervals. The method further includes determining a set of RBs with contiguous RBs, determining a second set of RBs for physical sidelink feedback channel (PSFCH) transmissions based on an intersection between the interleaving and the set of RBs, performing a sidelink (SL) channel access procedure, and transmitting the PSFCH carrying HARQ feedback in the second set of RBs after successfully performing the SL channel

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR TRANSMITTING UPLINK IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure relates to a method of a terminal in a wireless communication system and, particularly, to a method and a device, the method comprising the steps of: receiving, from a base station, an RRC message including information for determination of a TCI state; receiving, from the base station, DCI including information indicating an integrated TCI state; when multiple TCI states are indicated based on the DCI, determining a TCI state for a PUCCH based on the information for determination of the TCI state; and transmitting uplink control information to the base station on the PUCCH based on the determined TCI state.

title: AUTHORIZATION METHOD AND APPARATUS
abstract: This application provides an authorization method and an apparatus, to verify whether a terminal device is authorized to provide or use a specific relay service, and may be applied to fields such as a 4G system, a 5G system, and a future communication system, for example, a 6G system. The method includes: A policy control function network element receives an authorization request message from an access and mobility management function network element, and determines, based on proximity-based services authorization information of a terminal device, whether the terminal device is authorized to use a relay service code, where the authorization request message includes the relay service code from the terminal device, the authorization request message is for requesting to verify whether the terminal device is authorized to use the relay service code, the relay service code identifies a connection service provided by a relay device for a remote device, and the proximity-based services author

title: METHODS AND APPARATUS FOR PERFORMING LTM IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments disclosed herein relate to methods (200) and systems (100) for managing a Lower-layer Triggered Mobility (LTM) in wireless communication networks for both a User Equipment (UE) (104) and a network. The methods (200) include configuring and performing of mobility in equivalent Stand-alone Non-Public Network (SNPN). The methods (200) include addressing a Centralized Unit (CU)-Distributed Unit (DU) (CU-DU) interaction for handling a reference configuration for LTM. Further, the methods (200) include handling of LTM configuration during a Radio Resource Control (RRC) reestablishment. Further, a Near Radio (NR) sidelink discovery procedure is performed by configuring the UE (104) with sl-RxPool and sl-DiscRxPool for NR sidelink discovery reception in LTM candidate cell configuration. The methods (200) include interaction of a Master Node (MN) (128) and a Secondary Node (SN)

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR OPTIMIZING LOWER TRIGGERED MOBILITY (LTM) IN A COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a terminal in the system, comprises receiving, from a base station, a message comprising configuration information on a successful handover, and in case that the terminal performs a layer 1/layer 2 (L1/L2) triggered mobility (LTM) cell switch, releasing the configuration information on the successful handover, wherein the LTM cell switch is triggered by the base station.

title: COMPENSATION TECHNIQUES IN JOINT COMMUNICATION AND SENSING (JCAS) SYSTEMS
abstract: Embodiments attempt to solve challenges in a wireless communications system. Embodiments describe various techniques, systems, and devices to support a joint communication and sensing (JCAS) system using one or more reference signals of a wireless communications system to detect objects, such as a wireless communications system comprising a Third Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) system, including long-term evolution (LTE), fifth generation (5G) new radio (NR), or sixth generation (6G) cellular networks, among other types of wireless communications systems. Other embodiments are described and claimed.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DETERMINING RESOURCE BLOCK SET IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system includes receiving, from the base station, a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH) including at least one control channel element (CCE), and identifying a resource block (RB) set for an uplink transmission based on the PDCCH in case that a CCE with a first lowest index is overlapped with a guard band in a plurality of RB sets.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ENERGY SAVING OF COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5th generation (5G) or 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a communication system is provided. The method includes receiving uplink wake up signal (UL WUS) configuration information including information on a first plurality of cells, performing UL WUS transmission based on the UL WUS configuration information, identifying a cell for data communication included in the first plurality of cells based on a reference signal received from the cell after performing the UL WUS transmission, and performing the data communication on the cell.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR RANDOM ACCESS OF ENHANCED REDUCED CAPABILITY UE
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The disclosure relates to a user equipment for a reduced capability (RedCap) in a wireless communication system, the UE comprises a transceiver and a processor operatively coupled to the transceiver, the processor configured to receive a message including at least one random access (RA) configuration and a contention free random access (CFRA) resource configuration including an indication of a number of message 1 (Msg1) repetitions, to determine whether a RA configuration, from the at least one RA configuration, is associated with the RedCap and whether the RA configuration is associated with the number of Msg1 repetitions included in the CFRA resource configuration, based on a result of the determination, to select a first RA configuration, from the at least one RA configuration, and to perform an RA procedure based on the first RA configuration.

title: MULTIPLE ACCESS TECHNOLOGY COEXISTENCE METHOD AND DEVICE IN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. A method performed by means of a terminal in a communication system, according to one embodiment of the disclosure, comprises the steps of: receiving, from a first node, information related to the first node; transmitting, to a second node, the information related to the first node; and receiving scheduling information from the second node, wherein the scheduling information received from the second node is based on the information related to the first node, the information related to the first node includes information about a signal from the first node, and a time-frequency resource scheduled by means of the scheduling information received from the second node is scheduled so as not to overlap with a time-frequency resource for the signal from the first node.

title: METHODS AND APPARATUS FOR TCI STATE INDICATION AND UPDATE IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Methods and apparatuses for transmission configuration indication (TCI) state indications and updates. A method performed by a user equipment includes applying a TCI state for UE dedicated control and data channels and receiving, via a medium access control (MAC) control element (CE), a plurality of TCI codepoints. The method further includes identifying a reference TCI codepoint from the plurality of TCI codepoints; determining, from the reference TCI codepoint, one or more TCI states; and determining whether to apply the one or more TCI states or the reference TCI state for the UE dedicated control and data channels.

title: METHOD AND UE FOR HANDLING RESOURCE ALLOCATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). Accordingly, embodiments herein disclose a system and method for handling resource allocation in wireless communication system. The method includes registering, by a UE, a first SIM among a plurality of SIMs with a first network entity in the wireless communication system and a second SIM among plurality of SIMs with a second network entity in wireless communication system. Further, the method includes configuring, by the UE, a RF resource with the first SIM for at least one of a downlink reception and uplink transmission. Further, the method includes determining, by the UE, whether second SIM needs RF resource. Further, the method includes performing, by the UE, provisioning of RF resource based on the determination.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MANAGING INTERFERENCE IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5th generation (5G) or 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method and a Base Station (BS) for determining an optimal equalizer for managing interference in a communication network are provided. The method includes estimating channel coefficients of each slot of a plurality of slots based on received Demodulation Reference Signal (DM-RS) symbols, determining a covariance of interference-and-noise (Rz) matrix for at least one Resource Block (RB) of a plurality of RBs of each slot based on the channel coefficients, determining a noise variance (σ2) based on noise measurements performed for one or more sub-carriers without the interference, and determining an optimal equalizer from a plurality of equalizers for managing the interference, based on diagonal elements of the Rz matrix and σ2 of the at least one RB using a machine learning model.

title: Rapid, Automatic Directional Beam Alignment in 5G/6G Networks
abstract: Beam alignment is a critical requirement in 5G-Advanced and 6G due to the high density of user devices anticipated in the coming years. However, prior-art beam alignment procedures are slow and costly in terms of resource usage. Therefore, disclosed herein are methods enabling a user device to determine the direction toward the base station rapidly at very low cost. The base station emits a tailored pulse with an angle-dependent phase, varying from a first phase at a first angle, to a second phase at a second angle, followed by a uniform-phase calibrator pulse. The user device can measure the as-received phase of the tailored pulse relative to the calibrator pulse, and thereby determine the user's direction relative to the base station. The user device can then inform the base station of the received phase, which is proportional to the angle. Both user and base station thereby obtain instant beam alignment.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DETERMINING RESOURCE BLOCK SET IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system includes receiving, from the base station, a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH) including at least one control channel element (CCE), and identifying a resource block (RB) set for an uplink transmission based on the PDCCH in case that a CCE with a first lowest index is overlapped with a guard band in a plurality of RB sets.

title: Ultra-Lean Beam Optimization in 5G and 6G
abstract: Optimization of transmission beams in 5G/6G requires feedback to the transmitting entity. Current beam alignment/optimization methods are so complex and resource-intensive, that users often delay re-alignment even when required by to changing conditions. Therefore, methods are provided for a transmitter to add two brief, single-resource-element test signals, with slightly different transmission parameters (such as direction), to the end each message. The receiver selects which test signal, or the message itself, that has the best signal, and informs the transmitter using a terse bit-level acknowledgement code. The reception beam must also be aligned, generally without feedback. The transmitter provides a series of identical signals, sequentially in time, while the receiver varies its reception beam parameters while measuring the signal level and optimizing its reception parameters. Both transmitter and receiver can thereby align their beams and optimize their beam parameters for the be

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR APPLYING CONFIGURATION OF CHILD NODE FOR MIGRATION IN BACKHAUL-ACCESS HOLE COMBINED SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to embodiments of the present disclosure, a method and apparatus for applying a configuration of a child node for migration in a backhaul-access hole combined system may be provided.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR AUTHENTICATING USER EQUIPMENT IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G, pre-5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate than a 4G communication system such as LTE. Provided is a method performed by an AUSF entity in a wireless communication system, the method comprising the steps of: receiving, via an access and AMF entity, an authentication request message for registration of user equipment to a stand-alone non-public network; receiving, from a UDM entity, a message including information indicating that initial authentication for the registration of the user equipment to the SNPN needs to be performed by an authentication server outside the SNPN; selecting an authentication server to perform the initial authentication of the user equipment; transmitting the authentication request message for the user equipment to the selected authentication server; receiving an authentication response message from the selected authentication server; and transmitting the authentication response messag

title: METHOD FOR SELECTING SECURITY ALGORITHM IN AUTHENTICATION PROCEDURE OF WIRELESS COMMUNICATION NETWORK
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5G) or sixth generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) for performing network authentication is provided. The method includes determining, by the UE, a K-bit identification indicator indicating whether a length of an authentication key (K) stored in a subscriber identification module (SIM) is identified, selecting, by the UE, at least one security algorithm based on the K-bit identification indicator, and transmitting, by the UE to a network device, a registration request message including the K-bit identification indicator and information about the at least one security algorithm.

title: DEVICE AND METHOD PERFORMED BY DEVICE IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a node (a device) in a wireless communication system, and a node (a device) are provided. A method performed by a first node in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes acquiring time information related to data, and transmitting a first message to a second node based on the time information related to the data, wherein the first message includes identity information of the data and at least one configuration information.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ENERGY SAVING OF COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5th generation (5G) or 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a communication system is provided. The method includes receiving uplink wake up signal (UL WUS) configuration information including information on a first plurality of cells, performing UL WUS transmission based on the UL WUS configuration information, identifying a cell for data communication included in the first plurality of cells based on a reference signal received from the cell after performing the UL WUS transmission, and performing the data communication on the cell.

title: Reliability Optimization by AI-based Fault Mitigation in 5G/6G
abstract: An unsolved problem in 5G-Advanced and especially 6G is message fault mitigation without a costly retransmission. Methods are disclosed for the receiver to analyze each message element's received waveform signal to detect characteristic features of interference and noise, such as excessive amplitude or phase variation within the message element or excessive deviation from the predetermined modulation levels of the modulation scheme, and to provide that data to an AI model trained in message fault correction. The AI model can then identify the faulted message elements, and attempt to correct them according to the likely intent or meaning of the message based on the non-faulted message elements, and on the bit sequences of previously received non-faulted messages, and other criteria that the AI model may apply. By repairing the message upon receipt, the costs in time, transmission power, and background noise generation may be avoided. Next-generation users will enjoy the improved recepti

title: Real-time Fault Mitigation by Modulation Quality in 5G and 6G
abstract: Message faulting is an unsolved problem in 5G-Advanced and 6G, due to higher frequencies, higher pathloss, higher modulation orders, and higher numerology planned for the aggressive next-generation goals. Legacy modulation schemes are not ideal due to limited phase margins and complex noise effects that inhibit mitigation. Hence, the standard plan is to either include bulky redundant bits or simply request a retransmission upon any error. Disclosed herein are methods for the receiver to identify faulted message elements and correct them, without a retransmission, based on the modulation data alone. Two contrasting modulation schemes, 16QAM and multiplexed amplitude-phase modulation, are contrasted and, in some applications, combined to reveal the fault locations. With the enhanced reliability of real-time fault mitigation, and the reduced latency of avoiding the retransmission, the proposed methods can enable the ambitious next-generation goals.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR ENERGY SAVING OF WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure comprises the steps of: receiving, from a terminal, a signal including UE capability information; identifying a base station mode on the basis of the UE capability information; transmitting a base station mode change signal to the terminal on the basis of the base station mode; receiving a response signal from the terminal; and changing the magnitude of the transmission power on the basis of the base station mode so as to transmit a signal to the terminal, wherein: the UE capability information includes at least one piece of support information related to an energy saving mode for a base station, control information related to a frequency band supported by the terminal, and power information related to a channel bandwidth supported by the terminal; the base station mode is either an energy saving mode related to the base station or a general mode rela

title: Access network management and configuration method, system and apparatus
abstract: The present application provides an access network management and configuration method, system and apparatus. The present application can solve the problem that the existing operation and maintenance modes cannot meet the differentiated configuration requirements between multiple operators or between operators and enterprises, and can thus improve the flexibility of network configuration, and can be applied in the fields of 4G systems, 5G systems, and future communication systems, such as 6G systems. The method comprises: a first management unit acquires a first identifier of a first operator and first management and configuration information of an access network management object of the first operator, as well as a second identifier of a second operator and second management and configuration information of an access network management object of the second operator, and then creates, according to the first management and configuration information and the second management and configur

title: Secure access method and system of 6G full decoupling network
abstract: The invention discloses a secure access method and system for a 6G full-decoupling network, and provides an efficient multi-base-station cooperation and user access solution for future 6G full-decoupling wireless access network communication. Through a lightweight group key negotiation strategy based on secret sharing, an interpolation polynomial is constructed to transmit shared key generation information, a trust relationship among internal members of a data base station group is established, and a group leader base station is selected for the base station group, so that the negotiation efficiency of the base station group is improved; on the basis of completing base station group key negotiation, a binomial expression is constructed through a secret sharing technology, and a key for base station group access and session service is negotiated for a user. Compared with a traditional access authentication mechanism, the method has the advantages that efficient and low-overhead security

title: Low-linewidth low-temperature sintering gyromagnetic ferrite phase shifter substrate and preparation method thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a low-linewidth low-temperature sintering gyromagnetic ferrite phase shifter substrate and a preparation method thereof, and belongs to the technical field of electronic materials. The material of the ferrite substrate is Ni < 0.18 > Cu < 0.2 > Zn < 0.62 > Bi < 0.02 > Fe < 1.98 > O < 4 >-nano LiZn spinel type gyromagnetic ferrite (hereinafter referred to as NCZB-nano LiZn for short) material. The invention provides a hot pressing sintering process for preparing the low-temperature sintered gyromagnetic ferrite phase shifter substrate with low line width. In the material formula, Bi < 3 + > ions are adopted to replace NiCuZn ferrite, so that the low-temperature sintering characteristic of the ferrite is realized. Besides, in order to further obtain a lower line width, nano LiZn ferrite is adopted for doping, the ferromagnetic resonance line width of NiCuZn ferrite is further optimized, grain growth is controlled in combin

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SETTING BEAM IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure relates to a method performed by a terminal in a communication system, the method comprising: receiving first downlink control information including first TCI state information from a base station on a first CORESET; determining whether to apply the first TCI state information to the PDCCH reception based on the first CORESET; and receiving the DCI from the base station on the PDCCH based on the first TCI state information when it is determined that the first TCI state information is applied to the PDCCH reception, the first TCI state information corresponding to the common TCI state.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR COEXISTENCE SCHEME OF MULTIPLE RADIO ACCESS TECHNOGY IN COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. The present disclosure relates to an operation of a terminal and a base station in a wireless communication system, and more particularly, to a method for transmitting and receiving an uplink reference signal in a wireless communication system and an apparatus capable of performing the same. The present disclosure provides an apparatus and a method capable of effectively providing a service in a mobile communication system.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ENERGY SAVINGS OF COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system supporting higher data rates. The present disclosure relates to a communication technique and system for integrating a 5G or 6G communication system, which supports higher data rates than 4G systems, with IoT technology. The present disclosure can be applied to intelligent services (e.g., smart homes, smart buildings, smart cities, smart or connected cars, healthcare, digital education, retail, security, and safety-related services) based on 5G communication technology and IoT-related technologies. The present disclosure discloses a method for energy saving in a base station.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DETERMINING RESOURCE BLOCK SET IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data rate. Specifically, the present disclosure relates to a method for determining an RB set in a wireless communication system, comprising the steps of: receiving a first control signal transmitted from a base station; processing the received first control signal; and transmitting a second control signal generated based on the processing to the base station.

title: Method and apparatus for adjusting transmission timing of relay
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. An apparatus and method for transmission timing of a repeater. A method for operating a network control relay (NCR) includes receiving, by an NCR Mobile Terminal (NCR-MT) entity, information of a timing value for an NCR Forwarding (NCR-Fwd) entity on an NCR control link. The method further includes determining, by the NCR, a timing adjustment for the NCR access link based on the timing value and a reference signal (RS). The method also includes receiving, by the NCR-Fwd entity, a radio frequency (RF) signal on the NCR access link based on the timing adjustment.

title: Method and apparatus for configuring artificial intelligence and machine learning traffic transmissions in wireless communication network
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method of configuring artificial intelligence/machine learning (AI/ML) traffic transmissions in a wireless communication network is disclosed. The method includes receiving, by a policy control function (PCF) from a unified data repository (UDR), a notification of an update of AI/ML transmission configuration information; determining, by the PCF, whether an AI/ML protocol data unit (PDU) session or a transmission policy is affected by the update; and notifying, by the PCF, a session management function (SMF) that the SM policy is updated, where the SMF is configured to reconfigure the PDU session based on the updated SM policy.

title: Method and apparatus for supporting sidelink discovery burst
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method and apparatus for discovery burst transmission for a sidelink (SL) in a wireless communication system. A method of a user equipment (UE) includes identifying a sidelink (SL) synchronization signal and a set of physical SL broadcast channel (S-SS/PSBCH) blocks for a SL discovery burst, determining a transmission duration of the SL discovery burst, and determining a duty cycle of the SL discovery burst. The method further comprises the following steps: determining the type of the SL channel access process based on the transmission duration and the duty ratio; executing the SL channel access process based on the type of the SL channel access process; and transmitting the SL discovery burst after successful execution of the SL channel access procedure.

title: Dynamic beam indication and switching method and device
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Methods and apparatus for dynamic beam indication and handover in a wireless communication system. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) includes receiving at least a first downlink control information (DCI) field in a DCI to indicate a first transmission configuration indication (TCI) code point, and receiving radio resource control (RRC) signaling including a first parameter indicating whether a second DCI field indicating a second TCI code point exists in the DCI. The method further includes identifying a presence or absence of a second DCI field in the DCI based on the first parameter, and identifying a set of first and second TCI states based on the first and second DCI fields. The first DCI field is a TCI field, and the second DCI field uses one or more bits of one or more existing DCI fields in the DCI.

title: Method and apparatus for data transmission in RRC inactivity
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a high data transmission rate. A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving a radio resource control (RRC) release message including pause configuration information and configured grant-small data transmission (CG-SDT) configuration information, starting a timer related to time alignment (TA) for the CG-SDT based on the CG-SDT configuration information, identifying that a criterion for a small data transmission (SDT) process is satisfied, and performing a random access-small data transmission (RA-SDT) procedure in a case where any configured grant (CG) occasion corresponding to a synchronization signal block (SSB) having a reference signal reception strength (RSRP) higher than a threshold value is unavailable while a timer related to the TA for the CG-SDT is running, and a standard for the RA-SDT procedure is satisfied.

title: Method and apparatus for sounding reference signal configuration
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. An apparatus and method for SRS configuration are provided. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) includes receiving information about at least one sounding reference signal (SRS) related information (SRS-Info) associated with two sets of antenna ports; receiving an indication indicating X groups of antenna ports of the two groups of antenna ports; identifying X groups of antenna ports based on the indication; and transmitting an uplink (UL) transmission based on the X groups of antenna ports. When X = 1, the UL transmission corresponds to one physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) transmission from one of the two sets of antenna ports. When X = 2, the UL transmission corresponds to one or two PUSCH transmissions from the two sets of antenna ports.

title: 6G full-decoupling network representative channel generation method and system based on comparative learning
abstract: The invention discloses a 6G full-decoupling network representative channel generation method and system based on comparative learning. The method comprises the following steps: establishing a multipath channel model and a multiple-input-multiple-output orthogonal frequency division multiplexing system transmission model; a representative channel generation target is given according to a system transmission model; and establishing a multipath channel information data set according to the multipath channel model, training a Transformer-based encoder by using a comparative learning framework, and outputting multipath channel characterization information by the encoder by taking the multipath channel information as input. According to the multi-path channel information and the multi-path channel representation information, training a decoder based on Transform, and outputting the multi-path channel information by the decoder by taking the multi-path channel representation information as i

title: Method and apparatus for recording connection establishment failure information in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method of a UE in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes initiating a connection establishment procedure or a connection recovery procedure on a cell, and starting a timer corresponding to the connection establishment procedure or the connection recovery procedure, identifying that the timer expires, and if a cell identity of the cell is not equal to a cell identity stored in UE variables for connection failure reporting, reporting a connection failure. In one embodiment, first connection failure report information stored in a UE variable is stored in a new entry of a UE variable list for a plurality of connection failure reports, the UE variable is cleared and second connection failure report information of a cell is stored in the UE variable, and a message including the UE variable and the UE variable list is transmitted.

title: Method and apparatus for measuring QOE in inactive mode and idle mode in wireless communication system
abstract: The application relates to a method and apparatus for measuring QoE in an inactive mode and an idle mode in a wireless communication system. The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate.

title: Compression method and decompression method of non-standard floating point data
abstract: The invention provides a non-standard floating point data compression method, the method is used for carrying out compression expression and/or storage on non-standard floating point data in the field of wireless communication, the wireless communication comprises 5G or 6G communication, the method comprises the following steps: obtaining non-standard floating point data in a preset format, the non-standard floating point data comprises a sign bit, an exponent bit and a first mantissa of M bits; mapping the first mantissa into an N-bit second mantissa by using a preset mapping function, N being less than M; compressed non-standard floating point data is constructed based on the second mantissa, including the sign bits, the exponent bits, and the second mantissa.

title: Simplified coherence probability shaping optical fiber-terahertz integrated communication system and method
abstract: The invention relates to the field of optical fiber-wireless integrated communication, and discloses a simplified coherence probability shaping optical fiber-terahertz integrated communication system and method.The system adopts single side band modulation to generate complex signals with specific probability distribution, and heterodyne beat frequency is utilized to generate terahertz signals after optical fiber transmission. And the receiving terminal recovers the phase and amplitude information of the signal by using an envelope detector and a self-adaptive KK algorithm processing module, so that high-quality simplified coherent receiving is realized. According to the system, the receiver structure is simplified, and the hardware complexity and cost are reduced; the signal anti-interference capability and the transmission rate are improved by adopting a probabilistic shaping technology; the self-adaptive KK algorithm effectively eliminates signal-signal beat frequency interference a

title: System and method for PDCCH skip and scheduling requests
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure provides a method and apparatus for physical downlink control channel (PDCCH) monitoring. The method includes receiving downlink control information (DCI) from a base station, the DCI including an indication indicating that physical downlink control channel (PDCCH) monitoring is skipped over a duration; identifying, if a scheduling request (SR) is triggered and not cancelled, whether the SR is triggered for a buffer status report (BSR); determining at least one serving cell based on a result of the identification; and cancelling the skipped PDCCH monitoring on the at least one serving cell.

title: Manufacturing process of 6G radio frequency stable-phase cable assembly
abstract: The invention discloses a manufacturing process of a 6G radio-frequency stable-phase cable assembly, and the process comprises the following steps: S1, selecting a stable-phase cable and a radio-frequency connector, and enabling the stable-phase cable to be offline; s2, stripping and chamfering the A end of the phase-stabilized cable, and welding the radio frequency connector to the A end of the phase-stabilized cable; s3, phase consistency adjustment is carried out on the phase-stabilized cable; s4, stripping and chamfering the B end of the phase-stabilized cable, and welding the other radio frequency connector to the B end of the phase-stabilized cable; s5, performing phase consistency re-calibration on the phase-stabilized cable; and S6, installing a tail sheath of the phase-stabilized cable, and completing the manufacturing of the 6G radio frequency phase-stabilized cable assembly. The radio frequency stable-phase cable assembly manufactured by the invention can stably work in a 6G

title: Low-Complexity Procedure for 5G/6G Beam Alignment
abstract: A base station and a user device can cooperate to align their transmission and reception beams for optimal communication in 5G and 6G. At a pre-arranged time (or upon a special “start” signal), the base station can transmit a series of beam scan signals in various directions, each beam scan signal occupying a single resource element. The beam scan signals can be transmitted time-spanning or frequency-spanning. A user device can determine which of the beam scan signals provided the best received signal quality or amplitude or power. The user device can then indicate the favored beam direction by transmitting a reply signal in a single resource element having the same frequency and a predetermined time delay after that beam scan signal. The base station can determine the optimal beam direction toward the user device according to the time and frequency of the reply signal. Many other aspects are disclosed.

title: 6G 6G antenna package with low dielectric constant low dielectric loss and heat dissipation
abstract: The present invention relates to a 6G antenna package having a low dielectric constant, low dielectric loss, and heat dissipation. The 6G antenna package includes: an antenna; a low dielectric constant layer including a porous ceramic structure stacked on an upper portion and/or a lower portion of the antenna and a hollow nanosilica coating layer coated on an upper or lower end of the porous ceramic structure; and one or more heat dissipation layers provided between the antenna and the low dielectric constant layer. The hollow nanosilica coating layer is positioned at an uppermost and/or a lowermost end of the 6G antenna package, thereby providing excellent wireless communication performance while withstanding high heat generated in the high frequency bandwidth of 6G.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MEASURING QOE IN RRC INACTIVE MODE AND RRC IDLE MODE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate.

title: MEASUREMENT METHOD AND MEASUREMENT APPARATUS
abstract: This application provides a measurement method and a measurement apparatus, and may be used in various communication fields, such as 5G, 6G, 5G toB, end-to-end, device-to-device, machine-to-machine, vehicle-to-everything, and the internet of things. The method includes: A management device sends a measurement identifier to a measurement device. The measurement identifier is used to be associated with a to-be-measured target service, and the target service is a service corresponding to a data flow that passes through an industry terminal (such as CPE). The management device sends a measurement indication to the measurement device. The measurement indication indicates to measure a performance indicator of the target service associated with the measurement identifier, and the performance indicator of the target service is associated with an abnormal event (or referred to as a fault) of the target service. In this way, the measurement device can quickly recognize a to-be-measured service d

title: TERMINAL MOBILITY MANAGEMENT METHOD AND DEVICE USING NAS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. The present disclosure may provide a device and method for managing the mobility of a terminal in a wireless communication system. Disclosed is a method for selecting a PLMN or SNPN, in a process of confirming service disruption of a first communication provider communication network and utilizing service of a second communication provider communication network, on the basis of capability information about the ability of a terminal in a wireless communication system to utilize a communication network of a provider that the terminal is not subscribed to in a disaster situation.

title: Network and method
abstract: A telecommunications network, for example a 6G network, comprises a state management entity configured to store respective states of intent handlers for respective intents and/or expectations in one or more tables, provide the stored respective states of the intent handlers via an application programming interface, API such as providing the one or more tables via the API; and trigger a subsequent state following a current state. A time management entity is configured to time -schedule respective states of intent handlers and update time schedules of the states. A time estimator comprising an AI module or algorithm is configured to estimate required times for states of intent handlers by analysing actual start times of states and/or by measuring expectations and provide observation times for an intent report.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MANAGING MOBILITY OF UE
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method for managing mobility by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless network is provided. The method may comprise: identifying whether the UE is configured with configuration to report successful primary secondary cell group (SCG) cell (PSCell) change or addition information to a network, when performing a PSCell change; and in case that the UE is configured with the configuration to report the successful PSCell change or addition information to the network, releasing the configuration to report the successful PSCell change or addition information to the network based on the successful PSCell change.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MANAGING INTERFERENCE IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5th generation (5G) or 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method and a Base Station (BS) for determining an optimal equalizer for managing interference in a communication network are provided. The method includes estimating channel coefficients of each slot of a plurality of slots based on received Demodulation Reference Signal (DM-RS) symbols, determining a covariance of interference-and-noise (Rz) matrix for at least one Resource Block (RB) of a plurality of RBs of each slot based on the channel coefficients, determining a noise variance (σ2) based on noise measurements performed for one or more sub-carriers without the interference, and determining an optimal equalizer from a plurality of equalizers for managing the interference, based on diagonal elements of the Rz matrix and σ2 of the at least one RB using a machine learning model.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PERFORMING SCHEDULING REQUEST IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system may comprise the steps of: receiving, from a base station, configuration information about cell discontinuous reception (DRX) configured in a cell of the base station; identifying, on the basis of the configuration data, that the cell is in a non-active period of the cell DRX; and performing a random access procedure when an emergency service is to be initiated while the cell is in the non-active period.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR REDUCING OVERHEAD OF DOWNLINK CONTROL INFORMATION DURING MULTI-CELL SCHEDULING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. Specifically, the present disclosure relates to a method for transmitting and receiving an uplink data channel through MC-DCI and a device capable of performing same in a wireless communication system. The present disclosure provides a device and method capable of effectively providing services in a mobile communication system.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CONFIGURED GRANT (CG) AND SEMI-PERSISTENT SCHEDULING (SPS) OPERATION CONSIDERING DTX/DRX OPERATION OF BASE STATION IN NEXT-GENERATION MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transfer rates. The present disclosure relates to a method for reducing power consumption of a base station and a method for data transmission and reception between a terminal and a base station.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DISCARDING PROTOCOL DATA UNIT (PDU) SET UNIT DOWNLINK PACKET IN SEPARABLE MOBILE COMMUNICATION BASE STATION
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. According to the present disclosure, network radio resources can be efficiently used by performing discarding of a downlink PDCP SDU in units of PDU sets in a separable base station.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR PERFORMING HANDOVER WITHOUT RANDOM ACCESS FOR MOVING INTEGRATED ACCESS AND BACKHAUL SYSTEM IN NEXT-GENERATION MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. According to an embodiment of the present disclosure, when a mobile IAB (mIAB) node moves, a terminal can efficiently perform handover without performing random access. A target logical distributed unit (DU) initiates F1 setup with a target central unit (CU), the terminal is served on the basis of at least one cell activated by the target logical DU on the basis of the F1 setup, and a source logical DU transmits, to a source CU, information related to the at least one cell activated by the target logical DU.

title: INITIAL UPLINK TRANSMISSION IN RACH-LESS HANDOVER
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Methods and apparatuses for a MAC CE for multi-TRP operation in a wireless communication system. A method of a UE comprises: receiving, from a base station, a RACH-less switching command for a RACH-less switching operation to a target cell; determining whether a DRX is configured for the target cell; and retaining an active time during the RACH-less switching operation based on a determination that the DRX is configured for the target cell, monitoring a PDCCH to acquire a UL grant of an initial UL transmission, wherein the initial UL transmission for the RACH-less switching operation includes a transmission of a RRCReconfigurationComplete message to a target satellite or the target cell.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR POSITIONING TERMINAL USING MULTI-ACCURACY IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. Specifically, the present disclosure relates to a method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system, the method comprising the steps of: receiving, from an LMF, a first message including information for positioning measurement for multiple QoS; sequentially performing measurement on the multiple QoS on the basis of the first message; and transmitting, to the LMF, a second message including a result of the measurement.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR REPORTING UPLINK TRAFFIC JITTER IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth-generation (5G) or a sixth-generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving, from a base station, a first message including a configuration associated with a jitter report of an uplink traffic, transmitting, to the base station, a second message including the jitter report, wherein the configuration includes at least one of information on a protocol data unit (PDU) session or information on a timer.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR MANAGING MUSIM GAP PRIORITY IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system to support a higher data transmission rate. In an embodiment of the present disclosure, a method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method comprises the steps of: receiving, from a base station, a first radio resource control (RRC) reconfiguration message including information indicating that the UE is permitted to provide multi-universal subscriber identity module (MUSIM) support information for gap priorities; and transmitting, to the base station, a UE support information message including a MUSIM gap priority preference list indicating MUSIM gap priorities preferred by the UE. The MUSIM gap priority preference list includes information expressing respective priorities for all periodic MUSIM gap preference patterns by using integer values.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSMITTING CLOCK QUALITY INFORMATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system, according to one embodiment of the present disclosure, comprises: a transceiver; and a controller coupled to the transceiver. The controller is configured to: receive, from a base station, a radio resource control (RRC) message including a first event identifier (ID) and information related to clock quality; store the first event ID; and receive a system information block (SIB) including a second event ID from the base station, wherein the first event ID and the second event ID are associated with a timing synchronization status of the UE.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR RESETTING DOWNLINK HARQ FOR MULTICAST RECEPTION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method comprises receiving a radio resource control (RRC) release message including multicast and broadcast services (MBS) configuration information associated with reception of an MBS multicast in an RRC inactive state, performing cell reselection while the UE is in the RRC inactive state and resetting a medium access control (MAC) entity upon the cell reselection.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ENERGY SAVING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system may comprise the steps of: camping on a second cell activated for transmission of data among at least one cell associated with a first cell of a base station for random access; receiving, from the base station, the data on the second cell; and receiving, from the base station, information indicating deactivation of the second cell on the second cell.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MANAGING CONFIGURATION INFORMATION FOR CONTINUOUS CONDITIONAL PSCELL CHANGE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Specifically, the disclosure provides a method and an apparatus for managing configuration information to efficiently support continuous conditional PSCell change. The method of UE comprises: receiving, from a base station, an RRC message including a configuration on a subsequent conditional PSCell addition or change (CPAC), the configuration including information on a candidate SCG configuration of a candidate PSCell and an MCG configuration associated with the candidate SCG configuration; and identifying the configuration included in the RRC message, wherein the configuration includes a reference configuration for the candidate SCG configuration.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR RECEIVING AND TRANSMITTING INFORMATION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure provides a method performed by a UE and a base station in a wireless communication network, the method performed by the UE including: receiving first information for scheduling a first frequency domain resource including a plurality of second frequency domain resources; upon the second information is received, determining, based on the first information and the second information and from the plurality of second frequency domain resources, a second frequency domain resource for downlink transmission or uplink transmission scheduled by the first information, and the second information including indication information indicating that each second frequency domain resource in the plurality of second frequency domain resources is a downlink frequency domain resource or an uplink frequency domain resource; performing downlink transmission or uplink transmission on

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR OVERCOMING BEAM FAILURE IN NEXT-GENERATION MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. The present disclosure relates to a method, performed by a terminal, comprising the steps of: receiving a first message including timing adjustment (TA) information for a random access channel (RACH)-less handover from a mobile integrated access backhaul (mIAB) node; selecting a resource satisfying a threshold value from among one or more resources identified on the basis of first information, which is related to a beam to be used in a target cell, when the first message includes the first information and second information, which is related to the threshold value; and transmitting a second message based on the RACH-less handover on the basis of the selected resource and the TA information.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR HANDLING AI AND ML DATA IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Disclosed is a method of routing Artificial Intelligence (AI) related data in a telecommunication network, comprising determining if the AI-related data is to be routed via a User Plane (UP) or a Control Plane (CP).

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CONFIGURING PRIORITY OF SIDELINK POSITIONING REFERENCE SIGNAL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5G) or sixth generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a transmitting terminal in a wireless communication system of the disclosure is provided. The method includes receiving a sidelink system information block (SIB) from a base station, requesting transmission resources to perform sidelink communication with a receiving terminal from the base station, receiving downlink control information (DCI) through a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH) from the base station, identifying sidelink scheduling information included in the DCI, and performing scheduling based on the sidelink scheduling information.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MANAGING A DATA IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). The embodiments of the present disclosure provide a method performed by a node and a device thereof, which belong to the field of communication technology. A method performed by a user equipment includes receiving information for indicating a set of antenna array element weights, wherein the set of antenna array element weights includes weights for adjusting antenna array elements; receiving a signal based on the information.

title: IMPROVEMENTS IN AND RELATING TO A TELECOMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a User Equipment (UE) is provide. The method includes determining to include unavailability information in a deregistration request message; and transmitting, to a network entity, the deregistration request message, wherein the unavailability information is included in the deregistration request message as non-cleartext information, wherein the deregistration request message includes a non-access stratum (NAS) message container information element (IE), wherein the unavailability information is included in the NAS message container IE, and wherein at least a part of the NAS message container IE is ciphered by the UE.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MANAGING SESSION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. A method performed by a policy control function (PCF) entity in a wireless communication system, according to the present disclosure, may comprise the steps of: receiving, from an application function (AF) entity by using a network exposure function (NEF) entity, a first message including a packet data unit (PDU) set handling indicator and PDU set handling information for a multiplexing service; determining, on the basis of the first message, whether multiplexing service-based PDU set handling is supported; and transmitting, to the AF entity by using the NEF entity, a second message indicating whether multiplexing service-based PDU set handling is supported.

title: DEVICE AND METHOD PERFORMED BY DEVICE IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a node (a device) in a wireless communication system, and a node (a device) are provided. A method performed by a first node in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes acquiring time information related to data, and transmitting a first message to a second node based on the time information related to the data, wherein the first message includes identity information of the data and at least one configuration information.

title: CSI PROCESSING FOR MULTI-TRP COHERENT JOINT TRANSMISSION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Apparatuses and method for channel state information (CSI) processing for multiple transmission and reception points (multi-TRP) coherent joint transmission. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) includes receiving a configuration about a CSI report, based on the configuration, processing and determining the CSI report, and transmitting the CSI report. The configuration includes information about: (i) a parameter codebookType set to 'typeII-CJT-r18', 'typeII-CJT-PortSelection-r18', 'typeII-Doppler-r18', or 'typeII-Doppler-PortSelection-r18', or (ii) a parameter reportQuantity set to 'tdcp'. Processing of the CSI report occupies OCPU CSI processing units (CPUs) for a number of orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) symbols.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR LINKING TWO TECHNIQUES FOR MANAGING KEYS IN DUAL CONNECTIVITY IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system to support a higher data transmission rate. An operation of a master node (MN) connected to a user terminal in a wireless communication system according to the present disclosure may comprise the steps of: selecting a first secondary node (SN) counter value for a first target SN that does not support an optional secondary cell group (SCG) function; on the basis of the first SN counter value, generating a first key value for secure communication between the user terminal and the first target SN; transmitting, to the first target SN, the first key value corresponding to the first target SN; selecting multiple second SN counter values for a second target SN supporting the optional SCG function; on the basis of the multiple second SN counter values, generating multiple second key values for secure communication between the user terminal and the second target SN; transmitting, to the second target SN, the multiple second key

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SL POSITIONING REFERENCE SIGNAL
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Methods and apparatus for sidelink (SL) positioning reference signals (PRS) in a wireless communication system. A method of operating a user equipment (UE) includes receiving configuration information for an SL resource pool. The SL resource pool is used for the SL PRS and control information associated with the SL PRS. The method further includes determining resources for the SL PRS from an SL resource pool based at least on the configuration information; transmitting control information associated with the SL PRS; and transmitting the SL PRS on the determined resource.

title: Transmitting and receiving power in full duplex systems
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a high data transmission rate. An apparatus and method for transmit power in a full duplex system. A method of operating a user equipment (UE) for receiving a downlink (DL) channel or signal includes: operating a user equipment (UE); first information of a first set of parameters of a first DL channel or signal associated with a first subset of time slots from a set of time slots on a cell and second information of a second set of parameters of a second DL channel or signal associated with a second subset of time slots from the set of time slots on the cell are received. The method further includes determining to use a second set of parameters for reception based on the time slot for reception from the second subset of time slots, and receiving a second DL channel or signal in the time slot based on a power adjustment value in the second set of parameters.

title: Method and apparatus for signaling for LTE/NR SL coexistence
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Methods and apparatus for signaling for Long Term Evolution/New Radio (LTE/NR) sidelink (SL) coexistence in a wireless communication system. A method of operating a user equipment (UE) includes performing sensing over an LTE SL interface via an LTE SL entity, including decoding SL control information (SCI) and measuring SL reference signal received power (SL-RSRP); and selecting a resource for the LTE SL transmission of the LTE SL entity. The method further includes identifying information related to the sensed and selected resources for the NR SL entity, and performing NR SL resource selection or reselection for the NR SL entity based on the information related to the sensed and selected resources.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CHANNEL ACCESS PROCEDURE PRIORITY ON SIDELINK
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Methods and apparatus for channel access procedure priority on a sidelink (SL) in a wireless communication system. A method of a user equipment (UE) includes determining a type of an SL channel access procedure prior to an SL transmission, the SL channel access procedure being a type 1 SL channel access procedure or a type 2 SL channel access procedure, and if the type of the SL channel access procedure is a type 1 SL channel access procedure, determining a channel access priority level (CAPC) based on the type of the SL transmission, wherein the CAPC is associated with a parameter p. The method further includes performing the SL channel access procedure prior to the SL transmission based on the type of the SL channel access procedure and the CAPC, and performing the SL transmission if the SL channel access procedure is successfully performed.

title: Method and apparatus for contention window adjustment on sidelink in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Methods and apparatus for contention window adjustment on a sidelink (SL) in a wireless communication system. A method of a user equipment (UE) includes determining whether hybrid automatic repeat request (HARQ) feedback is available after a last update of a contention window size (CW); when the at least one HARQ feedback is determined to be available, determining a set of HARQ feedback corresponding to a physical side link shared channel (PSSCH) in a reference duration; and determining a first condition based on the set of HARQ feedback. The method further includes determining that the CW is reset to a minimum allowed value when the first condition is satisfied, or the CW is increased to a next higher allowed value when the first condition is not satisfied. The method further includes performing an SL channel access procedure based on the CW, and performing an SL transmiss

title: Method and apparatus for sidelink positioning reference signal signaling structure
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Methods and apparatus for a sidelink (SL) positioning reference signal (PRS) signaling structure in a wireless communication system. A method of operating a user equipment (UE) includes transmitting a first SL PRS and first SL control information associated with the first SL PRS to another UE; and receive second SL control information associated with the second SL PRS from another UE. The method further includes determining a resource for the second SL PRS based on the second SL control information, and receiving the second SL PRS on the determined resource.

title: Method and apparatus for handling pre-configured measurement gaps during handover
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein provide a method and a user equipment (UE) (110) for handling pre-configured measurement gaps during handover in a wireless network. The method comprises performing, by the UE (110), a handover in a wireless network from a first network node (130) to a second network node (120). The method further includes deactivating, by the UE (110), the one or more pre-configured measurement gaps without transmitting an uplink media access control control element (UL MAC CE). The method also includes detecting, by the UE (110), that one or more pre-configured measurement gaps need to be activated or reactivated after handover completion. The method further includes activating or reactivating, by the UE (110), the one or more pre-configured measurement gaps by transmitting the UL MAC CE.

title: Apparatus and method for supporting discontinuous reception mode operation in sidelink-based relay communication in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method for operating a sidelink repeater in a wireless communication system according to an embodiment of the present disclosure comprises the steps of: receiving, from a terminal, a first message including assistance information related to a sidelink discontinuous reception mode (DRX) between the sidelink repeater and the terminal; and transmitting a second message including configuration information related to the sidelink discontinuous reception mode to the terminal. The auxiliary information included in the first message may include at least one of a sidelink DRX period, sidelink timer information, and a sidelink start offset preferred by the terminal.

title: System and method for basis-based compression of Doppler coefficients for CSI feedback
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments disclosed herein relate to systems (100) and methods (400, 800) for performing basis-based compression of Doppler coefficients for channel state information (CSI) feedback in a wireless communication network. Systems (100) and methods (400, 800) enable CSI feedback for a user equipment (UE) (102) with a smaller number of base coefficients and reduced feedback when reporting the base coefficients. Systems (100) and methods (400, 800) facilitate differential reporting of Doppler frequency components of CSI feedback across beams and subbands/delays. Systems (100) and methods (400, 800) enable a base station (104) to determine the length of an observation window (OW) and a prediction window (PW) to trigger OW and PW reporting feedback. A system (100) and method (400, 800) use a Slepian sequence at a base station transmitter for a codebook-based enhanced spatial doma

title: Discovery of multi-user session common edge application servers in edge data networks
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein provide a first edge enabler client (EEC) in a wireless communication network and a method for discovering a common edge application server (EAS) for a multi-user session or a multi-application client (AC) session. The method also includes detecting, by the first EEC, a trigger of EAS discovery. The method also includes sending, by the first EEC, an edge enabler server (EES) discovery request message with the packet information to the EAS. The method also includes receiving, by the first EEC, an EAS discovery response message having at least one of the plurality of EASs or the common EAS that has been selected based on the packet information. The method further includes determining, by the first EEC, a common EAS for establishing a multi-user session or a multi-AC session between the first EEC and the at least one second EEC.

title: Method and system for determining 6G air-space-ground network security policy based on AI
abstract: The invention relates to a method and a system for determining a 6G air-space-ground network security policy based on AI (Artificial Intelligence). At least one edge server in a terminal device, a ground-based network, an air-based network or a space-based network is used for determining the security policy of the 6G air-space-ground network. And corresponding models (a space-air-ground integrated network security cross-layer universal large model, a hierarchical sub-model and a professional small model) are deployed at a cloud layer corresponding to the ground core network, so that a multi-layer intelligent cooperation mechanism among the space-air-ground integrated network security cross-layer universal large model, the hierarchical sub-model and the professional small model is realized. Intelligent perception, intelligent analysis, intelligent decision and intelligent arrangement are integrated into each link of a security system, and architecture-level intelligent endogenesis of a

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR PROCESSING APPLICATION DATA IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an embodiment, a network entity in a wireless communication system receives protocol information on which protocols are used for downlink data encoding in an application service, identifies and detects information on a protocol data unit (PDU) set to which downlink data belongs based on the protocol information, and transmitting the detected information on the PDU set and downlink data to the base station, wherein the protocol information includes information on a protocol and a data type for the application service.

title: Mobility in non-terrestrial networks with earth mobile cells
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate.
