A method and apparatus for mobility in a non-terrestrial network having an Earth Mobile Cell in a wireless communication system. A method of a UE includes receiving first cell movement information for a serving cell or a neighbor cell, the first cell movement information including first coordinates of a first reference position at a first period time and a speed of the first reference position; determining whether at least one of the serving cell or the neighbor cell is an earth mobile cell; updating a first coordinate of a first reference position of the at least one of the serving cell or the neighbor cell using the first cell movement information and ephemeris information based on determining that the at least one of the serving cell or the neighbor cell is an earth mobile cell; identifying a distance between the UE and a first coordinate of the first reference position;

title: Ultra-wideband antler-shaped small MIMO antenna based on intelligent driving environment
abstract: The invention discloses an ultra wide band antler-shaped small MIMO antenna based on an intelligent driving environment. The antenna structure comprises four lower-layer centrosymmetric rectangular grounding branches, four lower-layer centrosymmetric equilateral L-shaped decoupling branches, a middle dielectric layer, four upper-layer centrosymmetric antler-like antenna units and four upper-layer centrosymmetric equilateral L-shaped decoupling branches. The antenna generates an ultra-wide band from 5GHz to 10.17 GHz, and covers a V2X communication band, a UWB communication band, a WIFI 6E functional band and an IMT (5G/6G) communication band, so that the communication requirements of vehicles in various complex environments are met, and communication application of a WLAN band and 5G/6G communication are realized. The device is simple in structure, small in size and low in processing cost, and has a great application prospect in intelligent driving communication.

title: Method and apparatus for delivering and storing activation codes for ESIM device changes
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The invention relates to a method and apparatus for installing and managing an eUICC profile. The method includes: receiving, by a transceiver, a message including capability information indicating whether to support a device change function from a terminal; determining that the terminal supports the device change based on the capability information indicating the device change supporting function; and transmitting metadata including the device change configuration to the terminal through the transceiver based on determining that the terminal supports the device change.

title: Method and apparatus for bandwidth partial switching and downlink control information interpretation in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method is provided for interpreting a DCI field in case of switching a BWP by a UE having received a multi-PDSCH scheduling and a multi-PUSCH scheduling configuration. The method comprises: receiving, from a base station, an RRC message including TDRA table information for scheduling a plurality of PDSCHs for at least one bandwidth portion; receiving a DCI including a bandwidth portion indicator field and a TDRA field from the activated BWP; determining whether to switch the activated BWP based on the bandwidth portion indicator field; and receiving at least one PDSCH in the indicated BWP based on at least one of the TDRA field, first TDRA table information of the activated BWP, and second TDRA table information of the indicated BWP when the bandwidth portion indicator field indicates switching of the activated BWP.

title: 6G-oriented cross-domain multi-carrier communication sensing collaborative waveform design method
abstract: The invention discloses a 6G-oriented cross-domain multi-carrier communication perception collaborative waveform design method, which belongs to the technical field of wireless communication perception integration, and comprises the following steps: S1, establishing a continuous multi-OFDM symbol perception sequence fuzzy function mathematical model; s2, optimizing the self-correlation characteristics of the sensing sequence in the interested interval; and S3, optimizing the value of the fuzzy function outside the interested interval and the communication power distribution. According to the 6G-oriented cross-domain multi-carrier communication sensing collaborative waveform design method, a waveform optimization design strategy with the sensing function as the center is adopted, waveform coefficients are optimized in time, frequency, power and even time delay-Doppler domains, and the communication reachable data rate is increased as much as possible while the target sensing performance

title: Robust low-complexity millimeter wave communication sensing integrated beam forming method
assignee: ZHUHAI UM SCIENCE & TECH RESEARCH INSTITUTE
abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of 6G wireless communication, and particularly relates to a robustness multi-beam forming method in a millimeter wave communication and perception integrated system. Most traditional schemes assume that perfect channel state information is known when researching communication perception integrated beam forming, but in practical application, channel estimation errors and movement of a user and a target can bring channel errors. In order to obtain a robust beam forming scheme with good performance and low complexity, the robust multi-beam forming technology provided by the invention combines two sub-beams with a wide beam radiation mode, and the two sub-beams respectively point to a communication user and a radar sensing target. By integrating double phase shifters into the robust hybrid beam forming architecture, experiments show that the robust multi-beam forming technology provided by the invention has good performance in a channel error sc

title: Antenna module and electronic equipment
assignee: SHANGHAI LONGQI INTELLIGENT TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses an antenna module and electronic equipment, a first metal feed wire is connected with a second metal feed wire through a radio frequency antenna matching feed point, the second metal feed wire is connected with a third metal feed wire, and the third metal feed wire is connected with a bottom layer of a metal conductive layer, so that an efficient feed structure is formed. The design effectively amplifies electromagnetic energy and improves the radiation efficiency of the antenna. Besides, by adjusting the size of the coverage area of the second metal feed wire, the antenna module can flexibly adjust the resonant frequency, so that 2.4 G, 5G and 6G multi-band coverage is realized, and the requirements of different wireless communication standards are met. Furthermore, the positions of the retaining walls on the two sides of the metal conductive layer are provided with open grooves, and the size of the open grooves is increased, so that the backflow path of the wh

title: 6G communication signal enhancement method based on reconfigurable intelligent surface and storage medium
assignee: CHINA TELECOM DIGITAL INTELLIGENCE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a 6G communication signal enhancement method based on a reconfigurable intelligent surface and a storage medium. The method comprises the following steps: arranging the reconfigurable intelligent surface between a transmitting end and a receiving end of 6G communication; the transmitting end transmits the transmitted 6G communication signal to the reconfigurable intelligent surface, an optimal receiving signal of the reconfigurable intelligent surface is obtained by adjusting the moving angle of the reconfigurable intelligent surface, and log information of the 6G communication signal is recorded in a database; extracting an interference signal of the 6G communication from a database through a large semantic model, and filtering the optimal receiving signal of the reconfigurable intelligent surface to obtain a filtered communication signal; the power loss rate of the 6G communication signal is calculated through a natural environment attenuation model, the filte

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ENERGY SAVINGS OF WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. The method for processing a control signal in a wireless communication system comprises the steps of: receiving a first control signal transmitted from a base station; processing the received first control signal; and transmitting a second control signal generated based on the processing to the base station.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PERFORMING SCHEDULING REQUEST IN MOBILE NETWORK SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. The method for processing a control signal in a wireless communication system comprises the steps of: receiving a first control signal transmitted from a base station; processing the received first control signal; and transmitting a second control signal generated based on the processing to the base station.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSMITTING AND RECEIVING SOUNDING REFERENCE SIGNAL IN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. According to one embodiment, the method performed by a terminal in a communication system includes the steps of: receiving information on sounding reference signal (SRS) resource setting through higher layer signaling; receiving downlink control information (DCI) for scheduling a plurality of cells, wherein the DCI includes an SRS request field; identifying a plurality of SRS resources for the plurality of cells based on information on the SRS resource setting and a code point of the SRS request field; and transmitting SRS for the plurality of cells based on the plurality of SRS resources.

title: SL-PRS Method and apparatus for resource allocation of SL-PRS in sidelink positioning in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. The present disclosure relates to sidelink positioning of a mobile communication system, and more particularly, to a method and apparatus for allocating sl-prs transmission resources in sidelink positioning.

title: HARQ METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR RESETTING DOWNLINK HARQ FOR MULTICAST RECEPTION
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. The method for processing a control signal in a wireless communication system comprises the steps of: receiving a first control signal transmitted from a base station; processing the received first control signal; and transmitting a second control signal generated based on the processing to the base station.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR HANDLING TRANSMISSION ERRORS ON SPLIT PROCESSING
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. In the communication system, a server receives intermediate data from a terminal, the intermediate data including result values obtained through processing of at least one first layer among multiple layers constituting a neural network, along with error detection codes corresponding to the result values. The server checks for errors in the intermediate data based on the error detection codes. If an error is detected in the intermediate data, the server processes the result values using a second layer trained to correspond to the first layer. The output of the second layer is then processed by at least one third layer, which follows the first layer among the plurality of layers, to derive a final result.

title: apparatus and method to handover without random access for access UEs upon mobile integrated access and backhaul node's migration in next generation mobile wireless communication systems
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. According to an embodiment of the present disclosure, when a mobile iab (miab) node moves, a terminal can efficiently perform handover without performing random access.

title: QoE METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MEASURING QoE IN INACTIVE MODE AND IDLE MODE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data rate. The present disclosure provides a method performed by a terminal of a wireless communication system. The method comprises the steps of: receiving, from a base station, first information indicating the interruption of an application measurement report; storing, by an access stratum (AS) layer of the terminal, an application measurement result associated with the first information; receiving, from the base station, second information indicating the resumption of the application measurement report; and transmitting the stored application measurement result to the base station based on the second information.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR OPTIMIZING MOBILITY ROBUSTNESS FOR VOICE FALLBACK IN NEXT-GENERATION MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. According to the present disclosure, a control signal processing method in a wireless communication system comprises the steps of: receiving a first control signal transmitted from a base station; processing the received first control signal; and transmitting a second control signal generated based on the processing to the base station.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MANAGING REGISTRATION AREA IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data rate. According to an embodiment of the present disclosure, disclosed is a method for managing a registration area (RA) by an access and mobility management function (AMF) in a wireless communication system. The method comprises the steps of: receiving a registration request message including information on RA adjustment capability (RA adjustment capability) of a user equipment (UE) from the UE; receiving an RA adjustment request from a network entity; adjusting the RA for the UE based on the RA adjustment capability of the UE and the RA adjustment request; and transmitting a registration update message including information indicating the adjusted RA to the UE.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CONFIGURING PRIORITY OF SIDELINK POSITIONING REFERENCE SIGNAL IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data rate. A method performed by a transmitting terminal in a wireless communication system of the present disclosure may comprise the steps of: receiving a sidelink system information block (SIB) from a base station; requesting a transmission resource for performing sidelink communication with a receiving terminal from the base station; receiving downlink control information (DCI) from the base station through a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH); identifying sidelink scheduling information included in the DCI; and performing scheduling based on the sidelink scheduling information.

title: protocol data unit PDU set downlink The method and apparatus for discarding Protocol Data Unit PDU Set based downlink packets in split radio access network RAN
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. According to the present disclosure, a separate base station performs discarding of a downlink pdcp sdu in units of pdu sets, thereby efficiently using network radio resources.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSMITTING CLOCK QUALITY INFORMATION
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. The present disclosure can be applied to intelligent services (for example, smart home, smart building, smart city, smart car or connected car, health care, digital education, retail business, security and safety related services, etc.) based on 5g communication technology and iot related technology.

title: MUSIM METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR HANDLING MUSIM GAP PRIORITY IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data rate. The present disclosure provides a method performed by a terminal of a wireless communication system. The method comprises the steps of: transmitting preference information for at least one multi universal subscriber identity module (MUSIM) gap to a base station; receiving priority information for the at least one MUSIM gap from the base station; and applying any one of a MUSIM gap setting or a measurement gap setting based on the priority information.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR REPORTING UPLINK TRAFFIC JITTER IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. The present disclosure relates to an operation of a terminal and a base station in a wireless communication system, and more particularly, to a method and an apparatus for setting and performing an operation of reporting measured jitter information to a base station and a core network together with an identifier mapped to uplink traffic when a jitter occurring for uplink traffic transmitted by the terminal to a network can be measured.

title: PDU PDU METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SUPPORTING ESTABLISHMENT OF PDU SESSION BASED ON PDU SET IMPORTANCE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method for supporting the establishment of a protocol data unit (PDU) session based on the importance of a PDU set in the access & mobility management function (AMF) of a wireless communication system, according to the present disclosure, comprises the operations of: receiving a PDU session establishment request message from an application function (AF); and transmitting a response message to the AF in response to the request message, wherein the request message and the response message include at least one parameter used for expressing the importance of the PDU set.

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR LOCATION ESTIMATING OF USER EQUIPMENT USING MULTIPLE ACCURACY REQUIREMENT IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. Specifically, the present disclosure relates to a method for deriving a location of a specific terminal in a wireless communication system, comprising the steps of: receiving a first control signal transmitted from a base station; processing the received first control signal; and transmitting a second control signal generated based on the processing to the base station.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR OVERHEAD REDUCTION ON MULTI-CELL SCHEDULING DOWNLINK CONTROL INFORMATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. The present disclosure relates to an operation of a terminal and a base station in a wireless communication system, and more particularly, to a method for transmitting and receiving an uplink data channel through MC-DCI in a wireless communication system and an apparatus capable of performing the same. The present disclosure provides an apparatus and a method capable of effectively providing a service in a mobile communication system.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ENHANCING DRX CONFIGURATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5th generation, 5G) or sixth generation (6th generation, 6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. In one embodiment, a method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving configuration information on discontinuous reception (DRX) from a base station, starting a first timer based on the configuration information, and monitoring a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH) based on the first timer, in which the configuration information includes a first timer value for a first subcarrier spacing (SCS) group and a first timer value for a second SCS group.

title: Method and apparatus for energy saving in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure comprises the steps of: receiving a signal including UE capability information from a terminal; identifying a base station pattern based on the UE capability information; transmitting a base station mode change signal to the terminal based on the base station mode; receiving a response signal from the terminal; and changing a magnitude of the transmission power based on the base station mode in order to transmit a signal to the terminal, in which: the UE capability information includes at least one of support information related to an energy saving mode of the base station, control information related to a frequency band supported by the terminal, and power information related to a channel bandwidth supported by the terminal; the base station mode is an energy-saving mode related to the base station or a general mode related to the base station; and t

title: DCI field for multi-cell scheduling
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present invention relates to an apparatus and a method for a downlink control information (DCI) field for multi-cell scheduling, and more particularly, to an apparatus and a method for a downlink control information (DCI) field for multi-cell scheduling. In one embodiment, a method includes receiving first information for a set of cells, and identifying a first time-domain resource allocation (TDRA) table having a one-to-one mapping with cells in the set of cells. The method further includes receiving second information of a second TDRA table for receiving a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH) on a cell of the group of cells, a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH) that provides a downlink control information (DCI) format, the DCI format including a TDRA field indicating a first row of the second TDRA table, and receiving the second information of the second TD

title: Method and apparatus for downlink control information interpretation in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure provides a method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system. The method includes receiving a high layer signal including time domain resource allocation (TDRA) information from a base station; receiving downlink control information (DCI) including an antenna port field from the base station; and identifying an antenna port table of a first mapping type and an antenna port table of a second mapping type based on the antenna port field in a case where the DCI includes a plurality of pieces of scheduling information of different mapping types.

title: FREQUENCY MULTIPLIER EQUIPPED WITH ADAPTIVE VOLTAGE CONTROL BLOCK AND METHOD THEREFOR
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a communication method and system for converging a 5th-Generation (5G) communication system or a 6th-Generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th-Generation (4G) system. A frequency multiplier of a wireless communication system is provided. The frequency multiplier includes an input circuit to which a local oscillator (LO) signal is input, a multiplier circuit having one end connected to the input circuit and another end connected to a lower terminal of a load circuit, a load circuit having an upper terminal connected to a voltage controller, and a voltage controller configured between the upper terminal of the load circuit and an input power source, wherein the voltage controller may be configured to drop a voltage between the input power source and the upper terminal of the load circuit and reinput a feedback voltage based on an upper terminal voltage of the load circuit to the voltage controller, and a method of

title: Artificial Intelligence for Optimizing 5G/6G Wireless Network Performance
abstract: 5G and 6G wireless networks can use artificial intelligence models to optimize performance by measuring the rate of message faults, and in particular the rate of various types of faults. For example, the amplitude faults include a modulation amplitude distorted by a small or large amplitude change, and whether the faults cluster in the high or low amplitude modulation portions of a constellation chart, among many other inputs related to network operations. The artificial intelligence model can be configured to predict the subsequent network performance according to each modulation scheme available to the network, thereby enabling the network to select a more effective modulation scheme. Alternatively, the artificial intelligence model can select the preferred modulation scheme and recommend the change to the network operators, or it can implement the change automatically if enabled to do so.

title: Multi-Parameter Fault Detection and Mitigation in 5G and 6G Messaging
abstract: Message reliability is a key requirement of 5G/6G communications. In many challenging network environments, two successive retransmissions of a message can both be corrupted, greatly reducing reliability. Therefore, methods are disclosed for identifying faulted message elements according to a metric that includes the waveform or SNR of the message element, its modulation quality, and a consistency check between the received versions. The receiver can then assemble a merged message version by selecting the higher quality version of each message element from the two (or more) corrupted versions, and thereby avoid requesting yet another retransmission. In addition, the receiver can monitor the background level and, if it is above a predetermined limit, can request that the receiver store the message for a predetermined time, or until the background level subsides below the limit.

title: Self-Selected Unique Identification for Sidelink 5G/6G Communications
abstract: User devices can form a spontaneous temporary local network for communications in 5G and 6G, without involvement of a base station. However, each member user device in the local network must have an identification code different from the other member user devices, so that they can communicate specifically with each other. Procedures are provided for each member user device to select an identification code different from the others, and for a newly arriving user device to select its own identification code different from the existing members, and for each member user device to respond to unexpected conflicts between the identification codes of member user devices. Each member user device can thereby transmit messages, such as an emergency collision-avoidance message, to a specific other member device when needed.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR UE CAPABILITY REPORT FOR MULTIPLE TIMING ADVANCE AND SIMULTANEOUS TRANSMISSION BASED ON MULTI PANEL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5g or 6g communication system for supporting a higher data rate. title: System, method and apparatus for mobility management in mobile networks
abstract: A wireless communication system 100 comprises a first network entity 101, e.g. a 5G/6G gNB, comprising a transceiver and signal processor 108 and configured to support a plurality of wireless communication units. A radio access network (RAN) device 151, e.g. a 5G/6G RAN data collection (RDC) device, is coupled to the first network entity 101 wherein the first network entity 101 is configured to collect RAN mobility data of the plurality of wireless communication units and provide the RAN mobility data to the RAN device 151. A RAN data analytics device 170, e.g. a RAN data analytics network function (RDANF), is coupled to the RAN device 151 and comprising a database 171. A latent space representation circuit 159 is configured to generate compressed, low-dimensional latent space-based representation of the RAN mobility data 175 and transmit this representation of the RAN mobility data 175 to the RAN data analytics device 170 that stores the representation of the RAN mobility data 175 in

title: METHOD FOR COOPERATIVE COMMUNICATION BETWEEN TRANSMISSION AND RECEPTION NODES FOR CONTROLLING MULTI-PANEL SIMULTANEOUS TRANSMISSION TERMINAL
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. Disclosed are a method and device for reporting signals and information wherein when a terminal arbitrarily determines the number of panels to be used in uplink communication and performs or intends to perform uplink transmission by using multiple panels simultaneously, relevant information is reported to a base station to enable the base station to control or schedule uplink communication in consideration of multi-panel operations of the terminal.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR MANAGING REGISTRATION AND CONNECTION OF TERMINAL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. In a wireless communication system, a method performed by an access mobility and management function (AMF) node may comprise the steps of: receiving, from a radio access network (RAN) node, an N2 message including a request to register a terminal and information on a satellite used by the terminal; receiving, from a network data analytics function (NWDAF) node, information on the mobility of the terminal; determining a periodic registration update timer value on the basis of the N2 massage and the information on the mobility of the terminal; and transmitting the periodic registration update timer value to the terminal.

title: COMMUNICATION METHOD AND DEVICE FOR EDGE COMPUTING SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. In a wireless communication system, a method performed by an edge enabler server (EES) comprises the steps of: receiving, from an edge enabler client (EEC), a request message including information related to a predicted movement path of a terminal including the EEC; and determining an application context storage time on the basis of information related to the predicted movement path of the terminal.

title: METHODS AND APPARATUS FOR HOSTING REAL TIME LAYER AND NON-REAL TIME LAYER IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). Embodiments herein provide a method and apparatus (301) to host real time layer and non-real time layer in a telecommunication network. The method includes detecting, by apparatus (301), a plurality of network layers. Further splitting a first set of network layers of the plurality of network layers into a real time layer and a second set of network layers of the plurality of network layers into a non-real time layer. Furthermore, hosting the RT layer (313) of the apparatus (301) for managing RT functionalities and the NRT layer (311) of the apparatus (301) for managing NRT functionalities of the telecommunication network.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SUPPORTING AD HOC GROUP STANDALONE SHORT DATA SERVICES IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Specifically, the disclosure related to apparatus and methods for supporting ad hoc group standalone SDS in MCData service. A method incudes receiving, by MCData server of primary MCData system, first data transfer request message from MCData client of primary MCData system for determining target MCData users for standalone SDS. Further, determines whether criteria for determining a list of MCData users or a list of MCData users associated with primary and partner MCData system is received. Further, determining list of MCData users based on criteria when criteria is received, and creates an ad hoc group, assigns pre-configured group and ad hoc group ID. Also, creating an ad hoc group based on list of MCData users associated with primary and partner MCData system when list of MCData users are received in first data transfer request message, and assigning a pre-configured group and a

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR COMMUNICATION BETWEEN APPLICATION CLIENT (AC) AND EDGE ENABLER CLIENT (EEC) IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein provide to method for communication between AC and EEC for ACR. The method includes detecting, by UE (201), whether an Edge Application Server (EAS) is unavailable at a target node during a mobility of the UE. Further, transmitting EEC service notification message from EEC (103) of the UE to the AC (101) of the UE for relocating application context from EAS at the source node to CAS when the EAS is detected to be unavailable at target node. Further, receiving CAS information report message from the AC to the EEC. Further, relocating the application context from the EAS at the source node to the CAS based on the CAS information. Further, transmitting CAS information report acknowledgement message to the AC of the UE indicating successful reception of the CAS information report and successful relocation of the application context.

title: Sub-Network Topology for Cybersecure 5G and 6G Communications
abstract: Cybersecurity is a critical requirement for 5G-Advanced and 6G, in part due to the large attack surface represented by the exponentially increasing number of reduced-capability devices needing wireless service but intensely cost-constrained by their applications. Therefore, disclosed is a network topology that enables an unlimited number of basic sensor/actuator “end devices” to communicate, securely, with a user device of a managed 3GPP-compliant network. The user device or “hub” device serves as both manager and gateway, and more importantly, as a firewall preventing attackers from penetrating the managed network through the end devices. In addition, the end devices can obtain iron-clad protection by booting and operating from ROM only, and transferring only pre-configured replies up to their hub device. The topology enables the hub and end devices to perform the edge work largely autonomously, while insulating the managed network from cyberattacks.

title: Message Fault Localization and Correction in 5G and 6G
abstract: Message faults are likely to be common in the noisy, high-density wireless environments planned for 5G and 6G. Disclosed is a method for a receiver to recover the correct message from one or more corrupted message copies, by (a) measuring the modulation quality of each message element, and (b) determining which message elements of two corrupted copies are “inconsistent”, that is, the corresponding message elements are different. The modulation quality can be determined according to how close the message element's modulation is to the predetermined modulation levels of the modulation scheme. The receiver can assemble a merged message by selecting whichever message elements of the two copies have the best modulation quality, and determine whether the merged message is still corrupted. If so, the receiver can sequentially replace the inconsistent message elements with those of the other copy, singly or in a comprehensive nested search, testing each version until successful.

abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR INTEGER AND NON-INTEGER BASED VECTOR PERTURBATION PRECODING IN MU-MIMO
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Vector perturbation precoding of data is enabled or disabled by a base station, which transmits to a user equipment information including vector perturbation schemes and parameters related to vector perturbation precoding. Perturbation vectors are determined based on the vector perturbation schemes, perturbation vectors, and data signals are modulated with the perturbation vectors, with the modulated data signals being transmitted to the user equipment. The vector perturbation precoding parameters may include a power normalization factor, a modulo threshold, and quantization parameters. A downlink control information may indicate a switch between the vector perturbation schemes and a linear precoding scheme.

The method comprises identifying a first signal and a second signal, wherein the first signal is associated with a sum beam signal and the second signal is associated with a differential beam signal and performing first self-interference cancellation on the first signal based on a first parameter set to obtain a first residual signal associated with a sensing.

The asymmetric switch-on-chip comprises: a first radio frequency port, a first switch arm, a second switch arm, a second radio frequency port and a third radio frequency port. A first parallel resonance unit connected via the first switch arm and a second parallel resonance unit connected via the second switch arm can form a coupled resonance structure, and parallel resonance of the second parallel resonance unit is used to achieve a high-resistance state, thereby reducing influences of a loading effect of the second switch arm on the loss of the first switch arm. abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Further, the method includes performing a network slice admission control for the PDU session, based on the determination, by interacting the NF service consumer with a Network Slice Admission Control Function (NSACF) entity (300).

In order to promote edge-end collaboration, energy consumption of the system is reduced by optimizing network topology and task scheduling and migration. title: CDR method for solving real-time communication interruption
abstract: According to the CDR method for solving real-time communication interruption provided by the invention, a communication mode with the highest reliability can be recommended to a user in a remote mountainous area or an emergency disaster relief scene under the conditions that signals are limited and the electric quantity of a communication terminal is insufficient. For example, when a common voice channel is congested, a user can communicate in an OTT mode automatically. According to the real-time CDR prediction and control method, adaptive optimization of communication interruption processing strategies and flows is carried out according to real-time data, a user is helped to automatically select the most reliable mode for communication through an intelligent selection algorithm in an emergency situation, the user is enabled to realize non-inductive re-establishment of connection, disconnection is avoided in a transient no-service situation, and the user experience is improved. abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. An apparatus and method for channel state information (CSI) codebook parameters. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) includes receiving information about a channel state information (CSI) report, the information indicating Ngt; and one CSI reference signal (CSI-RS) resource and a codebook. The codebook includes a spatial domain (SD) basis component, a frequency domain (FD) basis component, and a coefficient component. The SD base component comprises Lr base vectors for each CSI-RS resource, r = 1,..., N. The FD basis component includes Mv basis vectors. The coefficient component includes coefficients associated with the (SD, FD) basis vector pair. The information includes codebook parameters. title: OFDM (Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing)-based communication sensing integrated system and robust target sensing method
abstract: The invention provides an OFDM (Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing)-based communication sensing integrated system and a robust target sensing method, and belongs to the technical field of communication sensing integration. The method comprises the following steps: performing reciprocal filtering, IFFT (Inverse Fast Fourier Transform) and spectral peak search on a received target echo signal at a sensing receiving end to obtain prior information of a system; and then regularization reciprocal filtering is carried out, and two-dimensional fast Fourier transform is carried out on a regularization reciprocal filtering result to obtain the distance and the speed of the target. According to the invention, perception is realized without changing a hardware architecture and a frame structure of a current OFDM communication system, and the problem that detection of a weak target is affected to a great extent because the sidelobe level is increased when the randomness of data is enhanced

There is provided a method performed by a first node in a wireless communication system, the method comprising: receiving configuration information including a perception-related first parameter; and transmitting a first signal for sensing based on sensing-related information, which is obtained based on the first parameter or the first condition, when a first condition is satisfied.

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD PERFORMED BY THE SAME IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. An apparatus and a method performed by the same in a wireless communication system are disclosed. The method includes: receiving configuration information from a base station; and receiving a downlink signal from the base station based on the configuration information, the downlink signal including downlink data. The downlink data includes a unicast physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH) and/or a multicast/broadcast PDSCH, the unicast PDSCH includes a dynamically scheduled unicast PDSCH and/or a unicast semi-persistent scheduling (SPS) PDSCH, and the multicast/broadcast PDSCH includes a dynamically scheduled multicast/broadcast PDSCH and/or a multicast/broadcast SPS PDSCH. The present disclosure improves the efficiency of uplink or downlink transmission.

abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates, and discloses a method for transmitting a sounding reference signal (SRS) in a wireless communication system. abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5<sup>th</sup> generation (5G) or 6<sup>th</sup> generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate than a 4<sup>th</sup> generation (4G) communication system such as long-term evolution (LTE). A method performed by a network data collection and analysis function (NWDAF) in a wireless communication system may comprise the steps of: receiving, from a policy control function (PCF), user equipment (UE) route selection policy (URSP) information relating to a UE policy to be applied to a UE; requesting session information that is being used by the UE, on the basis of the URSP information; requesting a report on traffic information for each application used by the UE on the basis of the URSP and the session information; and receiving the traffic information for each application from the UE.

abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR FORMING A USER CENTRIC CLUSTER
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present invention generally relates to the field of femtocell, and more specifically relates to a method and a system for forming a User Centric Cluster (UCC) for at least one Cell Free (CF) femtocell deployment.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR NF SELECTION FOR ACCESS AND MOBILITY MANAGEMENT IN A SERVICE-BASED ARCHITECTURE
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Present disclosure describes multiple embodiments relating to network function (NF) selection for access and mobility management in a service-based architecture. In an embodiment, a central unit (CU)-control plane (CP) NF directly communicates with a target Access and Mobility Function (AMF) using locally configured parameters. In an embodiment, an AMF selection is done via a Network Repository Function (NRF) by the CU-CP. In an embodiment, the CU-CP NF does discovery of an AMF by querying the NRF, and sends the request to SCP containing the address of a selected AMF. In an embodiment, the CU-CP NF adds any necessary discovery and selection parameters required to find a suitable AMF to a request message for the SCP.

title: COMPOSITION FOR MANUFACTURE OF A DIELECTRIC SUBSTRATE AND METHOD FOR PRODUCING A FOIL-CLAD DIELECTRIC MATERIAL
abstract: A composition and method for manufacturing thin (150 μm or less) polymeric dielectric substrates with low dielectric losses (less than 0.001) at superhigh frequencies (5G, 6G), which are foil clad with an electrically conductive layer on one or two sides. The claimed polymer substrate composition consists of an injection moldable fluoroplastic and a quartz filler. The fluoroplastic is selected from among F-4MB grade perfluorinated copolymers of ethylene and propylene, or analogs thereof, namely Teflon FEP, Neoflon FEP, Dyneon FEP. The quartz filler can consist of continuous fibers (yarns or tows, or fabrics) and/or dispersed fillers (discontinuous fibers, powders, flakes and the like). The proposed technology for producing a foil-clad dielectric material does not entail the use of a special adhesive layer between the foil and the polymer substrate and consists in hot pressing three separate components - a polymer film, a quartz filler and a foil, i.e. in a single operation, the filler

title: POLAR CODES WITH PARTIAL REPETITION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A threshold for weights of rows with indices belonging to an information set is obtained in a polar coding generator matrix, a smallest index of the information set with a weight less than or equal to the threshold is obtained. A first candidate index is selected based on a frozen index that is larger than the smallest index, and a second candidate index is selected based on information indices that are smaller than a largest candidate frozen index and that corresponds to a weight less than or equal to the threshold. Support of the first candidate index is determined to be distinct from support of the second candidate index in at least two elements, based on which at least one bit from the second candidate index is repeated to the first candidate index.

title: POLARIZATION-ADJUSTED CONVOLUTIONAL CODES WITH FREEZER
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Error-correcting performance of polarization-adjusted convolutional codes is improved by refreezing low weight indices. For information bits ( k ), where k is a positive integer, a plurality of convolutionally encoded bits (n) are generated by performing a convolutional encoding operation on the plurality of information bits and a plurality of frozen bits (n - k ), where n is a positive integer. A refreezing operation is performed on the convolutionally encoded bits, including dynamically assigning zeros to selected frozen indices such that a number of low weight codewords is reduced. Polar encoded bits are generated by performing a polar encoding operation on an output of the refreezing operation, providing polar encoded bits for transmission or storage.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SENSING A BEAM SELECTION IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). A method performed by a first user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system, the method comprising transmitting, to a second UE, a request message for collaborative sensing, receiving, from the second UE, a response message including sensing measurements based on the request message, and transmitting, to the second UE, a sensing signal for a beam selection based on the sensing measurements, wherein the second UE is a UE which is identified as a UE with the collaborative sensing based on the request message.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR UPLINK RETRANSMISSION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate than that of a 4G communication system such as LTE. This method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system, may comprise the steps of: receiving, from a base station, downlink control information (DCI) including an uplink (UL) grant for transmitting an uplink signal; identifying a UL transmission resource for transmitting a first transport block (TB) and a second TB on the basis of the UL grant; and transmitting the uplink signal on the UL transmission resource, wherein the first TB and the second TB may be associated with different hybrid automatic repeat and request (HARQ) process identifiers (IDs).

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR RECONFIGURATION FUNCTIONAL SPLIT OPTION OF DIGITAL UNIT AND RADIO UNIT IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE).　The disclosure relates to a method performed by an radio unit (RU) of a base station in a wireless communication system, particularly, the disclosure may provide a method performed by an RU of a base station in a wireless communication system, comprising preparing for a functional split reconfiguration, receiving a functional split reconfiguration message from a digital unit (DU) of the base station, and reconfiguring a functional split option so that an artificial intelligence (AI) function of the RU is moved to the DU based on split option reconfiguration information included in the functional split reconfiguration message.

title: SENSING SESSION CREATION METHOD AND APPARATUS
assignee: BEIJING XIAOMI MOBILE SOFTWARE CO LTD [CN]
abstract: The present disclosure relates to the technical field of communications. Provided are a sensing session creation method and apparatus. The sensing session creation method provided in the present disclosure comprises: a session management function (SMF) receiving a sensing service request sent by a user equipment (UE) by means of an access and mobility management function (AMF), wherein the sensing service request includes sensing service information of a sensing service task; and creating a sensing service session between the UE and a user plane function (UPF) according to the sensing service information. The present disclosure provides an establishment procedure for a sensing session in a 6G core network, such that computing power resources of a UE can be fully invoked on the basis of the sensing session to perform computation and analysis, and thus new services that may emerge from the 6G era and high-quality requirements for data and computing power therein can be met.

title: METHODS AND APPARATUS FOR DETERMINING PLMN WITH DISASTER CONDITION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method for determining a Public Land Mobile Network, PLMN, with disaster condition by an AMF in a wireless network is provided. The method comprises: receiving a registration request message from a UE: identifying that a 5GS registration type IE in the registration request message is set to a “disaster roaming initial registration” or a “disaster roaming mobility registration updating”; identifying whether a PLMN with disaster condition IE is included in the registration request message based on identifying that the 5GS registration type IE is set to the “disaster roaming initial registration” or the “disaster roaming mobility registration updating”; and determining a PLMN with disaster condition in the PLMN with disaster condition IE based on identifying that the PLMN with disaster condition IE is included in the registration request message.

title: A METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR HANDLING BUSY INDICATION FOR MUSIM UE
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The embodiment herein is to provide a method performed by a multiple subscriber information module user equipment (MUSIM UE) in a wireless communication system. The method includes receiving, from a second network, a paging request on a second SIM in a radio resource control (RRC) inactive state, while receiving service on a first SIM from a first network and in case that the MUSIM UE rejects the paging request, and criteria for transmitting a busy indication is met, transmitting the busy indication to the second network in response to the paging request, and in case that the MUSIM UE rejects the paging request, and the criteria for transmitting the busy indication is met, avoid transmitting the busy indication to the second network in response to the paging request.

title: RECONSTITUTED SUBSTRATE FOR RADIO FREQUENCY APPLICATIONS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to methods and apparatus for forming thin-form-factor reconstituted substrates and semiconductor device packages for radio frequency applications. The substrate and package structures described herein may be utilized in high-density 2D and 3D integrated devices for 4G, 5G, 6G, and other wireless network systems. In one embodiment, a silicon substrate is structured by laser ablation to include cavities for placement of semiconductor dies and vias for deposition of conductive interconnections. Additionally, one or more cavities are structured to be filled or occupied with a flowable dielectric material. Integration of one or more radio frequency components adjacent the dielectric-filled cavities enables improved performance of the radio frequency elements with reduced signal loss caused by the silicon substrate.

title: METHOD FOR DETERMINING SIDELINK RESOURCE
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes determining a resource selection window (RSW) corresponding to partial sensing, determining a sensing window corresponding to the partial sensing and determining transmission resources for sidelink transmission based on the partial sensing result.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR TIME AND PHASE SYNCHRONIZATION BETWEEN BASE STATIONS IN NETWORK COOPERATIVE COMMUNICATION
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure provides a method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system and a device for performing same, the method comprising the steps of: measuring a first reference signal transmitted from a first transmission and reception point (TRP) of a base station, and measuring a second reference signal transmitted from a second TRP; calculating a time delay difference between the first reference signal and the second reference signal; receiving, from the base station, terminal report configuration information indicating a report on the time delay difference between the first reference signal and the second reference signal; transmitting information on the time delay difference between the first reference signal and the second reference signal to the base station on the basis of the terminal report configuration information; receiving, from the first

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSMITTING UE CAPABILITY REPORT ON MULTIPLE TIMING ADVANCE AND SIMULTANEOUS TRANSMISSION OF MULTIPLE PANELS IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The disclosure relates to a method of reporting a UE capability and an apparatus for performing the same in a wireless communication system. In accordance with an aspect of the disclosure, a method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes: transmitting, to a base station, UE capability information indicating that the UE supports a receiving (Rx) timing difference larger than a cyclic prefix (CP) length; and performing a parallel processing for multi-transmission and reception point (TRP) transmission and/or reception, wherein each of the multi-TRP is associated with a respective timing advance (TA), wherein the Rx timing difference is a period between a first downlink (DL) reference timing and a second DL reference timing.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR RECONFIGURATION FUNCTIONAL SPLIT OPTION OF DIGITAL UNIT AND RADIO UNIT IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). The disclosure relates to a method performed by an radio unit (RU) of a base station in a wireless communication system, particularly, the disclosure may provide a method performed by an RU of a base station in a wireless communication system, comprising preparing for a functional split reconfiguration, receiving a functional split reconfiguration message from a digital unit (DU) of the base station, and reconfiguring a functional split option so that an artificial intelligence (AI) function of the RU is moved to the DU based on split option reconfiguration information included in the functional split reconfiguration message.

title: DYNAMIC RE-CONSTITUTION OF A SOFTWARE DEFINED PERIMETER (SDP) FOR MICRO-SERVICES NETWORK APPLICATIONS IN A 5G/6G NETWORK
abstract: Dynamic re-constitution of a software defined perimeter (SDP) for micro-services network applications in a 5G/6G telecommunications network includes authenticating both a device and also a user of the device seeking access to an initial set of network resources defining an initial SDP and, responsive to the authentication, generating an individual network communications link through a northbound API to one or more micro-services network applications of the initial SDP. Data traffic from the micro-services network applications is monitored over the link and the monitored data traffic is submitted to a predictor predicting a traffic pattern necessitating a change in the initial set of the network resources of the initial SDP. Finally, in response to the prediction of the traffic patterns, a new SDP is defined with a different set of the network resources, the network communications link terminated, and a new communications link established between the authenticated device and the new SDP

title: Amplitude-Phase Exclusion Zones for 5G/6G Message Fault Detection
abstract: A wireless receiver in 5G and 6G can localize message faults by defining “exclusion regions” that do not include any of the predetermined allowed modulation states of a modulation scheme. Then, upon receiving a message, the receiver can measure (or calculate from the I and Q branch amplitudes) the received amplitude and phase of the waveform signal in each message element. If either the received amplitude or the received phase is within any of the exclusion regions, the receiver determines that message element is faulted. If the received amplitude and received phase are not within any of the exclusion regions, the message element is demodulated according to the closest modulation state. In addition, the receiver can increment an amplitude fault tally and a phase fault tally according to which parameter—amplitude or phase—is inside the exclusion regions, thereby greatly simplifying recovery of the message without a retransmission.

title: METHODS FOR FAULT RECOVERY WITH COLLISION-TOLERANT MODULATION IN 5G AND 6G
abstract: In 5G and especially 6G, message fault recovery is a critical requirement, due to interference in crowded networks. Therefore, modulation schemes are proposed in which a collided message can be recovered by analysis of the sum-signal resulting from the addition of two different signals. The collision generates a predictable signal which can be decomposed into the component signals, thereby enabling the original message to be recovered in some cases. To do so, the modulation scheme is restricted to a subset of allowed states, in which any sum of two states is not one of the allowed states. When the receiver detects one of the disallowed states, the receiver can allocate the value to one of the allowed states, or at least reduce the number of possible combinations, and thereby recover the message.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR ENERGY SAVING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a fifth generation (5G) or sixth generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method for determining a mapping order of channel state information (CSI) fields for CSI reports of a terminal to determine spatial domain adaptation and power domain adaptation when network energy saving is applied is provided. A method performed by a terminal in a communication system is provided. The method includes receiving, from a base station, configuration information for a channel state information (CSI) report, wherein the configuration information includes a CSI report configuration including a plurality of sub-configurations for sub-reports, receiving, from the base station, channel state information reference signals (CSI-RSs) associated with the CSI report configuration, identifying CSI based on the CSI-RSs and the configuration information, and transmitting, to the base station, the CSI, wherein the CSI includes CSI fields of s

title: AUTHORIZATION FRAMEWORK FOR NF(S) FOR SIXTH-GENERATION CORE NETWORKS USING DISTRIBUTED LEDGER
abstract: Embodiments of the present disclosure relate to network function validation and authentication. An example apparatus can be configured to send a message to an entity of a communication network, the message comprising property information associated with the apparatus, a public key associated with the apparatus, and a network function unique identifier associated with the apparatus, and receive, from the entity of the communication network, a response to the message, the response comprising a signed hash of the property information, an indication that the property information associated with the apparatus is accepted, and a decentralized identifier (DID) number associated with the apparatus. The apparatus can be configured to include the DID number in requests for service or data in order to expedite integrity verification of the requests for service or data and authentication of the apparatus.

title: Method and apparatus for reducing energy consumption of base station in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. In addition, the invention provides a method and equipment for reducing the energy consumption of the base station in the mobile communication system. According to the invention, the problem of excessive energy consumption of the base station in the mobile communication system is solved, and higher energy efficiency can be realized. According to one embodiment of the present disclosure, a method performed by a base station in a communication system may comprise the steps of: determining a transition from a first state to a second state; transmitting first state transition information indicating a transition from the first state to the second state to the terminal; transitioning the state of the base station from the first state to a second state; and performing communication with the terminal based on the second state.

title: On-demand service matching method, device and equipment in 6G air-space-ground Internet of Vehicles
abstract: The invention provides an on-demand service matching method, an on-demand service matching device and on-demand service matching equipment in a 6G air-space-ground Internet of Vehicles. The method comprises the following steps: carrying out virtualization processing on physical network resources through SDN and NFV technologies to obtain virtual network resources; obtaining a user demand and carrying out fine-grained demand analysis based on scene recognition on the user demand to obtain a fine-grained user demand; performing initial grouping processing on all users based on the virtual network resources and the fine-grained user requirements to obtain a plurality of initial user groups; performing problem modeling by using the virtual network resources, the fine-grained user requirements and the plurality of initial user groups to obtain an optimization problem model; performing problem reconstruction on the optimization problem model to obtain a TMSC framework; performing R-TMSC simp

title: Hybrid decoding method and device for LDPC (Low Density Parity Check) code close to maximum likelihood
abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of digital information transmission, in particular to an LDPC (Low Density Parity Check) code-oriented hybrid decoding method and device close to maximum likelihood, and the method comprises the steps: determining at least one preset iteration belief propagation algorithm according to a target code, obtaining an algorithm parameter configuration list corresponding to all the certain iteration belief propagation algorithms based on at least one certain iteration belief propagation algorithm; obtaining an original coding bit soft information vector of the target decoding, and determining a list of corresponding coding bit soft information vectors according to the original coding bit soft information vector; and obtaining serial decoding output corresponding to the list of the coding bit soft information vectors according to the algorithm parameter configuration list and the list of the coding bit soft information vectors. Therefore, the proble

title: Method and apparatus for uplink multi-panel simultaneous transmission in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure relates to a method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system, the method comprising the steps of: transmitting terminal capability information including information related to multi-panel simultaneous transmission (STxMP) to a base station; receiving an upper layer signal from the base station; checking whether the upper layer signal comprises a parameter for supporting STxMP; if the upper layer signal includes a parameter for supporting the STxMP, checking support for the STxMP based on multiple downlink control information (DCI); receiving, from a base station, a first DCI for scheduling a first physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) from a first control resource set (CORESET) with a CORESET pool index set to 0, and a second DCI for scheduling a second PUSCH from a second CORESET with a CORESET pool index set to 1; and performing a

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CONFIGURING SENSING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond 4G communication systems such as Long Term Evolution (LTE). The joint configuration of cellular communication and radar sensing involves reporting UE sensing capability information including co-existence of sensing functionality with cellular communication functionality within the UE, UE sensing hardware capabilities, and capabilities related to UE sensing parameters or modes. The sensing configuration request of the UE includes a sensing application type, a sensing range, and a sensing periodicity. The sensing configuration of the network includes sensing transmit power, power control parameters, waveforms, and sensing resources and periodicity. The sensing process is performed based on the sensing configuration.

title: Mining 5G multi-frequency fusion device and base station
abstract: The utility model provides a mining 5G multi-frequency fusion device and a base station, and relates to the technical field of underground wireless communication, and the mining 5G multi-frequency fusion device comprises a base plate, a base station signal controller, a display screen, a sixth generation mobile hotspot WIFI 6 board card, a power supply board card, a 10-gigabit Ethernet board card, an ultra wide band UWB board card, and a 5G base station board card. The base station signal controller, the display screen, the WIFI 6 board card, the power supply board card, the 10-gigabit Ethernet board card, the UWB board card and the 5G base station board card are all arranged on the bottom plate; the base station signal controller is connected with the 5G base station board card through a communication line; the power supply board card is connected with the base station signal controller, the display screen, the 10-gigabit Ethernet board card, the WIFI 6 board card, the UWB board card

title: Delay reduction in transmission or reception of data
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. An apparatus and method for delay reduction of transmission or reception of data. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) includes receiving first information of a first configuration grant (CG) configuration and second information of a second CG configuration. The method further includes determining to enable scheduling of a physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) having a transport block (TB) associated with the first CG configuration through a downlink control information (DCI) format based on the first information; determining to disable scheduling of the PUSCH having the TB associated with the second CG configuration through the DCI format based on the second information; starting a timer after the first CG-PUSCH transmission using the first TB associated with the first CG configuration; and ignoring the timer after the second CG-PUSCH transmission using a second TB

title: Method and apparatus for GNSS operation in non-terrestrial networks
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present disclosure provides a method and apparatus for GNSS operation in a non-terrestrial network. A method includes receiving information related to a measurement gap for GNSS measurements and information related to a first time interval for transmitting feedback information associated with the GNSS measurements. The method further includes determining a start of measuring the gap based on information related to measuring the gap; determining feedback information associated with the GNSS measurements during the measurement gap; and determining a transmission time of the feedback information based on the information related to the first time interval. The method further includes transmitting feedback information at the transmission time.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DOWNLINK AND UPLINK CARRIER PHASE REPORTING
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. An apparatus and method for uplink carrier phase reporting. A method of operating a user equipment (UE) includes receiving N downlink (DL) positioning reference signals (PRSs) from N antennas of a base station, respectively, and measuring carrier phase differences of the received DL PRSs corresponding to adjacent pairs of the N antennas. The DL PRS is received in the orthogonal resource, and Ngt; 1. The method further includes determining a first carrier phase difference to report based on the measured carrier phase difference, and reporting the first carrier phase difference to the network.

title: Method and apparatus for energy saving in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5th-Generation (5G) or 6th-Generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes: receiving first configuration information on a synchronization signal block (SSB) from a base station; receiving, from the base station, second configuration information about the SSB, the second configuration information including information indicating whether to cancel the SSB transmission; and determining whether to receive the SSB based on the first configuration information and the second configuration information.

title: Electronic packaging material and preparation method and application thereof
abstract: The invention relates to an electronic packaging material as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The electronic packaging material is prepared from the following raw materials: at least one prepreg containing an anhydride curing agent and hydroxylated heat-conducting particles, the prepreg containing the anhydride curing agent is combined with the hydroxylated heat-conducting particles through ester groups. The problems of compatibility and dispersity of the heat-conducting particles in the prepregs are solved by utilizing transesterification between hydroxyl groups of the heat-conducting particles and ester groups in the prepregs containing the acid anhydride compounds, the prepared electronic packaging material is excellent in heat-conducting and insulating properties, the service life of a third-generation semiconductor is prolonged, and the electronic packaging material is suitable for industrial production.
