title: 6G 5G-NR High Mobility support System and Methods using 5G-NR Physical Layer Elements to Improve Link Reliability of 6G Non-Terrestrial Networks
abstract: Provided are a high mobility support system using 5G-NR physical layer elements to improve link reliability of a 6G non-terrestrial network and a method thereof. The method comprises the steps of: encoding a PDSCH; performing modulation through orthogonal frequency division of a precoding code; modeling a Tapped Delay Line (TDL) propagation channel, adding noise, and performing timing synchronization for link-level simulation; performing Demodulation Reference Signal (DM-RS)-based channel estimation and compensation, and, after completing the DM-RS-based channel estimation and compensation, performing demodulation through orthogonal frequency division of the precoding code; and analyzing the link-level performance of the PDSCH in terms of BER, maximum throughput, and spectrum.

title: DU RU METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR RECONFIGURATION FUNCTIONAL SPLIT OPTION OF DIGITAL UNIT AND RADIO UNIT IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data rate. The present disclosure relates to a method performed by an RU of a base station in a wireless communication system and, more specifically, to a method performed by an RU of a base station in a wireless communication system, comprising the steps of: preparing functional split reconfiguration; receiving a functional split reconfiguration message from a digital unit (DU) of the base station; and reconfiguring a functional separation option so that an artificial intelligence (AI) function of the RU is moved to the DU based on separation option reconfiguration information included in the functional split reconfiguration message.

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR UPLINK RETRANSMISSION IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system that supports a higher data rate than 4G communication systems, such as LTE. In a wireless communication system, a method performed by a terminal may comprise the steps of: receiving, from a base station, downlink control information (DCI) including an uplink (UL) grant for transmitting an uplink signal; identifying, based on the UL grant, UL transmission resources for transmitting a first transport block (TB) and a second TB; and transmitting the uplink signal on the UL transmission resources, wherein the first TB and the second TB may each be associated with different hybrid automatic repeat and request (HARQ) process identifiers (IDs).

title: Method and apparatus for PDCCH monitoring for multi-cell scheduling
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method includes receiving information of a plurality of cell sets and information of a user equipment (UE)-specific search space (USS) set for receiving PDCCH candidates on a scheduling cell. The USS set has a USS set identification and is associated with a downlink control information (DCI) format for scheduling over more than one cell. The method further includes determining a set of cells from the plurality of sets of cells associated with the USS set and a reference cell from the set of cells. The size of the DCI format is counted among a plurality of sizes of the DCI format for scheduling on the reference cell, and is not counted among a plurality of sizes of the DCI format for scheduling on cells other than the reference cell from the set of cells.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR OPTIMIZING CONDITIONAL PSCELL ADDITION AND CHANGE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5th-Generation (5G) or 6th-Generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates. A method for optimizing conditional primary-secondary cell (PSCell) addition/change (CPAC) in a communication system and an apparatus capable of performing the same are provided. The methods include methods for reporting successfully completed (C) PAC related information and CPAC failure related information.

title: Method and apparatus for indicating dynamic beam switching via downlink control information
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method and apparatus for indicating dynamic beam switching via downlink control information (DCI) in a wireless communication system.

title: Information interaction method and device and readable storage medium
abstract: The invention relates to the field of mobile communication, can be applied to a protocol framework such as NR or 6G, and particularly relates to an information interaction method and device and a readable storage medium, the method comprises the following steps: determining a first receiving beam, sending T1 reference signals by using T1 sending beams corresponding to the first receiving beam, and receiving a first CSI report, the first CSI report comprising T1 beam measurement results, the T1 sending beams corresponding to the T1 sending beams corresponding to the T1 sending beams corresponding to the T1 sending beams corresponding to the T1 sending beams corresponding to the T1 sending beams corresponding to the T1 sending beams corresponding to the T1 sending beams corresponding to the T1 sending beams corresponding to the T1 sending beams; first information is sent, the first information is used for determining a second receiving beam set to be scanned, and the second receiving bea

title: Traffic Collison Avoidance or Minimization by Supercomputer Assist in 5G/6G
abstract: Traffic collisions involving autonomous vehicles can be greatly reduced by timely initiation of evasive action. However, calculating a suitable sequence of actions capable of avoiding or minimizing the collision may require the speed of a supercomputer. Therefore, disclosed is a method for the autonomous vehicle to transmit an emergency message with sensor data to a nearby access point in 5G or 6G, and the access point can forward the data to a supercomputer trained in collision avoidance. The supercomputer, millions or billions of times faster than vehicle computers, explores many sequences of actions and selects the one most likely to avoid the collision or if unavoidable, the sequence of actions that results in the least harm. Again using an exclusive channel, the supercomputer and the access point can relay the selected sequence to the autonomous vehicle, for immediate collision avoidance or harm minimization.

title: Procedures for Efficiently Defaulting QAM Messages in 5G and 6G
abstract: Message faults are expected to be a major impediment to 5G and future 6G throughput. The disclosed procedures enable a wireless receiver to recover many types of message faults based on the demodulation quality of each message element, among other diagnostic tests, and then to recover the correct message either by calculation (based on an embedded error-detection code) or by substitution (based on a search of all other modulation states in place of the faulted message elements). The method also includes determining, according to the modulation quality, when there are too many faults to efficiently mitigate, in which case a retransmission of just the affected portion is requested. The receiver can then merge the two versions of the message, selecting the better-quality message element at each position, and thereby correct the faulted message versions.

title: SYSTEM,METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ENHANCED MDT MEASUREMENT FOR UE ASSISTED AI ANALYTICS
abstract: Enhanced minimisation of drive tests (MDT) measurements in 5G/6G mobile networks, with additional procedures for UEs to log measurements, to improve UE mobility prediction and so to optimise handover decisions. A wireless communication system (200, 300) comprises a network entity, such as a gNB (242), and wireless communication units, such as UE (280). The network entity (242) comprises a transceiver operably coupled to a signal processor (258) and configured to support wireless communication for wireless communication units (280). The wireless communication unit (280) comprises a transceiver (306, 324) operably coupled to a signal processor (308) and a memory device (316), and is configured to perform radio resource control connected (RRC_CONNECTED) state measurements, store these measurements in the memory device (316) for a period of time to form a history of RRC_CONNECTED state measurements, and access the memory device (316) and transmit the history of RRC_CONNECTED state measurem

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR ADAPTIVE POLAR CODING CONFIGURATION TRANSMISSION OR RECEPTION
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a data transmission rate higher than that of a 4G communication system such as LTE. The present disclosure provides a method and a device for transmitting or receiving channel coding-related information between a base station and a terminal in a communication system. An embodiment of the present disclosure may include a method of a base station in a wireless communication system, the method comprising the steps of: determining a polar coding configuration for a terminal; and transmitting, to the terminal, configuration information on the determined polar coding configuration, wherein the configuration information includes information on at least one among the number of polar decoders, a polar code length, a polar decoding algorithm, a size of a list of polar decoding algorithms, the number of reconfiguration bits for reconfiguration of an index in polar decoding, and a polar sequence.

title: COMMUNICATION METHOD AND APPARATUS
abstract: This application provides a communication method and apparatus, to resolve a problem of a high air interface transmission delay, and may be applied to fields such as a 4G system, a 5G system, and a future communication system like a 6G system. The method includes: A communication device determines a time offset value based on scheduling and orchestration information and time of arrival, and sends the time offset value to an application network element. The scheduling and orchestration information indicates a scheduling moment, the time of arrival indicates a moment at which a first data packet in a service flow arrives at the communication device, and the time offset value is a difference between the moment at which the first data packet arrives at the communication device and the scheduling moment.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR CONFIGURING REPEATER BEAM IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates. Disclosed herein are a method and device for configuring a beam for a repeater in a wireless communication system. Specifically, a method performed by a repeater in a wireless communication system comprises the steps of: receiving control information from a base station, wherein the control information includes information associated with a beam for a signal to be transmitted from the repeater to a terminal; receiving the signal from the base station; and transmitting the signal, received from the base station, to the terminal on the basis of the beam.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DESIGNING HARQ CODEBOOK FOR FULL-DUPLEX COMMUNICATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The present invention provides a method for generating a type-1 HARQ-ACK codebook in a wireless communication system. A method performed by a terminal of a communication system according to an embodiment of the present invention may comprise the steps of: receiving configuration information related to subband non-overlapping full duplex (SBFD); identifying at least one occasion for receiving a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH); and receiving the PDSCH in a valid occasion among the at least one occasion, wherein, when the configuration information corresponds to a first configuration, the valid occasion is defined as either an occasion including only a first type symbol or an occasion including only a second type symbol, and when the configuration information corresponds to a second configuration, the valid occasion is defined as an occasion including only a first typ

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVIDING SECURITY ANALYSIS SERVICE IN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 6G communication system for accomplishing a high data transmission speed and an ultra-low latency after 4G and 5G communication systems. According to an embodiment, a method performed by a first node in a communication system may be provided. The method may include: obtaining vulnerability-related information related to the communication system; receiving a first message including a request for a security-related service from a second node; and providing a second message including the security-related service, based on the vulnerability-related information, after receiving the request for the security-related service.

title: INFORMATION INTERACTION METHOD, APPARATUS, AND READABLE STORAGE MEDIUM
abstract: The present application relates to the field of mobile communications, and in particular to an information interaction method, an apparatus, and a readable storage medium, and can be applied to protocol frameworks such as NR or 6G. The method comprises: determining a first receiving beam; transmitting T1 reference signals by using T1 transmitting beams corresponding to the first receiving beam; receiving a first CSI report, the first CSI report comprising T1 beam measurement results; transmitting first information, wherein the first information is used for determining a second receiving beam set to be scanned, the second receiving beam set is a subset of receiving beams, the first information is determined on the basis of a first prediction result, and the first prediction result is a processing result of inputting the T1 beam measurement results into an AI model for receiving and transmitting beam pair prediction. title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR OPTIMIZING RANDOM ACCESS IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. Embodiments herein provide a method and network apparatus (200) for optimizing a RA of a UE (100) in a wireless communication network. the method comprises receiving, from a network entity, an information request message, setting information on a random access (RA) procedure and transmitting, to the network entity, an information response message including the information on the RA procedure, wherein the information on the RA procedure includes information on a feature triggering the RA procedure.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CHANGING CONDITIONAL PSCELL IN CONSIDERATION OF CELL GROUP ACTIVATION OR DEACTIVATION IN NEXT-GENERATION MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to embodiments of the present disclosure, a method and apparatus for quickly activating cells in a next-generation mobile communication system may be provided.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PUSCH RESOURCE ALLOCATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: This disclosure relates to a 6G communication system for achieving high data transmission rates and ultra-low latency beyond 4G and 5G communication systems. The disclosure provides a method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system or an apparatus related thereto, the method including receiving first information about at least one resource pattern associated with resource allocation of an uplink reference signal (RS) from a base station, receiving second information indicating one resource pattern among the at least one resource pattern from the base station, identifying a physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) resource based on the first information and the second information, and transmitting an uplink signal to a base station based on the identified PUSCH resource.

title: SIDELINK POSITIONING METHOD AND DEVICE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. The disclosure relates to a method by which a first terminal performs positioning through a sidelink in a wireless communication system, comprising the steps of: allocating a resource for transmitting a first sidelink positioning signal; transmitting the first sidelink positioning signal on the basis of the allocated resource; receiving, from at least one second terminal, a second sidelink positioning signal including a transmission/reception signal time difference of the at least one second terminal; measuring a transmission/reception signal time difference of the first terminal on the basis of the second sidelink positioning signal; and measuring terminal positioning on the basis of the transmission/reception signal time difference of the first terminal and the transmission/reception signal time difference of the second terminal, wherein the first sidelink positioning sig

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVIDING SECURITY ANALYSIS SERVICE IN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 6G communication system for accomplishing a high data transmission speed and an ultra-low latency after 4G and 5G communication systems. According to an embodiment, a method performed by a first node in a communication system may be provided. The method may include: obtaining vulnerability-related information related to the communication system; receiving a first message including a request for a security-related service from a second node; and providing a second message including the security-related service, based on the vulnerability-related information, after receiving the request for the security-related service.

title: AUTOMATED ROOT CAUSE DETERMINATION USING A PRE-TRAINED GENERATIVE MODEL
abstract: Systems and methods are provided for determining a root cause of an incident that occurred in a 5G/6G multi-access edge computing and core network system. In particular, the disclosed technology is directed to using a pre-trained generative model to determine the root cause for an incident as recorded in event data of a system log. The present disclosure generates a prompt for the generative model to determine the root cause for an incident as recorded in the system log. The prompt comprises a combination including event data from a system log, function hierarchy graph data, and function information as grounding information in a prefix of the prompt. The prompt further includes a question that requests determining a root cause of an incident as recorded in the event data. An answer as generated by the pre-trained generative model indicates of the root cause and a function call associated with the incident.

title: Method and apparatus for optimizing non-public network (NPN) in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system, the method comprising: receiving logged minimization of drive test (MDT) configuration information comprising a list of at least one non-public network (NPN) identity; performing the logged MDT on the independent NPN (SNPN) based on the logged MDT configuration information; identifying whether the identification of the SNPN selected or registered by the UE matches the list; transmitting, to a base station (BS), an availability indicator indicating that a result of the logged MDT is stored in the UE, if the identification of the SNPN selected or registered by the UE matches the list; receiving a UE information request message from the BS; and transmitting a UE information response message including the result of the logged MDT of the SNPN to the BS in response to the UE information request messag

title: Session management method and device based on dynamic backhaul network time delay in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an aspect of the present disclosure, a method of a session management function (SMF) in a wireless communication system may include receiving a first message from an access and mobility management function (AMF), the first message including information indicating use of a dynamic satellite backhaul; a second message indicating the use of the dynamic satellite backhaul is sent to a policy control function (PCF) based on receiving the first message, wherein the PCF triggers a quality of service (QoS) monitoring operation for the dynamic satellite backhaul based on the second message.

title: Vanadium dioxide difunctional terahertz metasurface and coding metasurface for 6G communication
abstract: The invention provides a 6G communication vanadium dioxide dual-function terahertz metasurface and coding metasurface, the vanadium dioxide dual-function terahertz metasurface comprises at least one metasurface unit structure, and each metasurface unit structure comprises a silicon layer, an intermediate dielectric layer and a phase change layer which are sequentially arranged from top to bottom. The silicon layer is a square silicon column with the same side length, the four corner positions of the square silicon column are corner square blocks with the same side length, the maximum length of the corner square blocks located on the same side of the square silicon column forms the structural side length of the square silicon column, and the phase change layer is a square block which is made of vanadium dioxide and has the same side length. Vanadium dioxide of the phase change layer is in a medium state, and the metasurface unit structure transmits terahertz wave beams. At high temperat

title: Method and apparatus for transmitting network information to AI/ML application in wireless communication system
abstract: Disclosed is a 6G communication system for implementing a high data transmission rate and an ultra-low delay time after 4G, 5G communication systems. A method performed by a terminal in a wireless communication system includes transmitting an identifier of an application and a network state parameter list of the application to an access and mobility management function (AMF), wherein the application is an artificial intelligence (AI) application or a machine learning (ML) application; receiving authentication information of a network state information request or a network state analysis information request and address information about a first network entity collecting network state information from the AMF; transmitting a network state information request or a network state analysis information request to the first network entity based on the authentication information and the address information; receiving network state information corresponding to the network state information reque

title: Method and apparatus for supporting priority of network slices in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. A method performed by an access and mobility management function (AMF) entity in a wireless communication system includes receiving a registration request message including a network slice access layer (AS) group (NSAG) information request from a UE; receiving, from another entity, NSAG information including single network slice selection assistance information (S-NSSAI) priority information and mapping information between the S-NSSAI and an NSAG identifier; transmitting at least one of the NSAG information or NSAG tracking area (TA) boundary information to a policy control function (PCF) entity; receiving NSAG priority information from the PCF entity based on the NSAG information; and transmitting a registration response message including at least one of the NSAG information, the NSAG TA boundary information, or the NSAG priority information to the UE.

title: Method and device for exposing network of group
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to an embodiment, a method for operating a network exposure function (NEF) in a wireless communication system includes receiving, from an application function (AF), a request message including an external group ID and an aggregation condition used outside the network, converting the external group ID to an internal group ID used inside the network, performing aggregation on a response or report based on the aggregation condition and the internal group ID, and transmitting the aggregated response or report to the AF. And transmitting a response message corresponding to the request message to the AF. The aggregation condition may include a first condition for the data transmission and a second condition for the event.

title: Method and apparatus for providing synchronization service based on subscriber information to terminal in wireless communication system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate. According to the present disclosure, if a 5G communication system becomes a sync source such that a synchronization service is provided to a terminal, whether to provide the service is determined based on subscription information about the terminal, and service provision can be controlled.

title: Method and system for designing signaling radio bearers and handling control plane data transmission and reception
abstract: In embodiments, methods of signaling radio bearers and handling control plane data transmission and reception for a 6G network architecture are disclosed. The method includes flexible and simple network functions for providing a degree of freedom for network function placement due to clouding and virtualization of the network functions. The method also includes a 6G network architecture in which any network node communicates with any other network node located in the RAN or core network function, thereby enabling a single anchor of the UE to exchange control signaling with the network. The method also enables the new network architecture of the 6G to design signaling radio bearers required for communication between the network and the UE. The method also defines procedures for handling control plane messages for transmission and reception between the UE and the network entity.

title: 6G conduction signal fusion method, device and system for space-air-ground integration
abstract: The invention relates to a 6G conduction signal fusion method, device and system for space-air-ground integration. The method comprises the following steps: generating a conduction fusion signal at a transmitting end; modulating the conduction fusion signal from a time delay-Doppler domain to a time delay-time domain, and performing parallel-serial conversion to obtain a time domain conduction fusion signal; quickly capturing a satellite at a receiving end; after a satellite is captured, a received time domain conduction fusion signal is converted and restored to obtain an accurately estimated fractional Doppler value and fractional time delay, a channel matrix is reconstructed, data demodulation is performed by using the channel matrix, and navigation message information of the captured satellite is obtained; doppler positioning is carried out by using the fractional Doppler value and satellite information to obtain coarse positioning estimation of a receiver; and according to the fou

title: Phase programmable three-dimensional metamaterial unit
abstract: The invention discloses a phase programmable three-dimensional metamaterial unit which comprises three layers of structures from top to bottom: a three-dimensional structure layer, a dielectric substrate layer and a metal substrate structure layer, and by increasing the spatial degree of freedom of the structure, the regulation and control capability of the unit on electromagnetic waves is improved.
